# 📘 第 4 章　Physical Layer Logical Block (Chapter 4. Physical Layer Logical Block)

> 📄 **Source pages**: 351–362 | 📁 **File**: `chapter_04_aa.md`
> 🎨 **Format**: 中英对照双语 · 图表原始保留 · 中文背景色灰色 · GitHub Flavored Markdown

---

<!-- 📄 Page 351 -->
---

<a id="sec-4"></a>
## 4. Physical Layer Logical Block § | 物理层逻辑块 §


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

The Physical Layer isolates the Transaction and Data Link Layers from the signaling technology used for Link data interchange. The Physical Layer is divided into the logical and electrical sub-blocks (see § Figure 4-1).

§ Chapter 4. describes the logical sub-block and § Chapter 8. describes the electrical sub-block.

The logical sub-block has two main sections: a Transmit section that prepares outgoing information passed from the Data Link Layer for transmission by the electrical sub-block, and a Receiver section that identifies and prepares received information before passing it to the Data Link Layer.

The logical sub-block and electrical sub-block coordinate the state of each Transceiver through a status and control register interface or functional equivalent. The logical sub-block directs control and management functions of the Physical Layer.

PCI Express uses three types of encoding (8b/10b, 128b/130b, and 1b/1b) and two Data Stream modes (Flit Mode and Non-Flit Mode). A Data Stream in the Non-Flit Mode is defined as a contiguous collection of TLPs, DLLPs, and Logical Idle/IDL Token, starting at the end of an Ordered Set and ending with another Ordered Set or a Link Electrical Idle. A Data Stream in Flit Mode is defined as a set of Flits (see § Section 4.2.3), starting at the end of the first SKP Ordered Set after an SDS Ordered Set, and ending with the last Flit prior to an Ordered Set other than SKP Ordered Set that causes the Link to exit out of L0 state or if the Link enters Electrical Idle. The encoding is determined by the Data Rate of the Link.

The Data Stream mode is determined during initial Link training. If not disabled (see Flit Mode Disable) and if both the Ports (and all Pseudo-Ports, if any) support it, (see Flit Mode Supported), Flit Mode is chosen. Otherwise, Non-Flit Mode is chosen. See § Table 4-1 for valid encoding and symbol placement mode combinations.

> **Note 58.** Prior to [PCIe-4.0] § Chapter 4. described both logical and electrical sub-blocks. With [PCIe-4.0], the electrtical section was moved to a new § Chapter 8. and a new Retimer section was added.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

物理层 (Physical Layer) 将事务层 (Transaction Layer) 和数据链路层 (Data Link Layer) 与链路 (Link) 数据交互所使用的信号技术隔离开。物理层被划分为逻辑子块 (Logical Sub-block) 与电气子块 (Electrical Sub-block) (见 § 图 4-1)。

§ 第 4 章 描述逻辑子块,§ 第 8 章 描述电气子块。

逻辑子块包含两个主要部分:发送 (Transmit) 部分负责准备从数据链路层下发的待发送信息,以供电气子块传输;接收 (Receiver) 部分负责识别并准备接收到的信息,再将其传递给数据链路层。

逻辑子块和电气子块通过状态和控制寄存器接口 (或功能等价机制) 协调各个收发器 (Transceiver) 的状态。逻辑子块主导物理层的控制和管理功能。

PCI Express 使用三种编码 (8b/10b 编码、128b/130b 编码 与 1b/1b 编码) 以及两种数据流模式 (Flit 模式 (Flit Mode) 与 非 Flit 模式 (Non-Flit Mode))。非 Flit 模式下的数据流 (Data Stream) 定义为:从某个有序集 (Ordered Set) 的结尾开始,到另一个有序集或链路电气空闲 (Electrical Idle) 处结束的,由 TLP、DLLP 和逻辑空闲/IDL Token (Logical Idle/IDL Token) 组成的连续集合。Flit 模式下的数据流定义为:从 SDS 有序集之后第一个 SKP 有序集结束处开始,到导致链路退出 L0 状态的非 SKP 有序集之前最后一个 Flit 处 (或链路进入电气空闲时) 结束的一组 Flit (见 § 第 4.2.3 节)。编码方式由链路的数据速率 (Data Rate) 决定。

数据流模式在初始链路训练 (Link Training) 期间确定。如果未被禁用 (见 Flit Mode Disable) 并且两个端口 (及所有伪端口,如有) 都支持 Flit 模式 (见 Flit Mode Supported),则选择 Flit 模式;否则选择非 Flit 模式。有效的编码与符号放置模式组合见 § 表 4-1。

> **注释 58.** 在 [PCIe-4.0] 之前,§ 第 4 章 同时描述逻辑子块和电气子块。自 [PCIe-4.0] 起,电气部分被移至新的 § 第 8 章,新增 Retimer (重定时器) 部分。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

> **Figure 4-1.** Layering Diagram Highlighting Physical Layer

</div>

<a id="sec-4-2"></a>
## 4.2 Logical Sub-block § | 逻辑子块 §


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

**Table 4-1 Valid Encoding and Data Stream Mode Combinations**

| Current Data Rate | Flit Mode Negotiated during Configuration when LinkUp=0b | Encoding | Data Stream |
|---|---|---|---|
| 2.5 GT/s, 5.0 GT/s | No | 8b/10b | Non-Flit Mode |
| 2.5 GT/s, 5.0 GT/s | Yes | 8b/10b | Flit Mode |
| 8.0 GT/s, 16.0 GT/s, 32.0 GT/s | No | 128b/130b | Non-Flit Mode |
| 8.0 GT/s, 16.0 GT/s, 32.0 GT/s | Yes | 128b/130b | Flit Mode |
| 64.0 GT/s | Yes (mandatory) | 1b/1b | Flit Mode |

The Ordered Set encoding follows the 8b/10b, 128b/130b, and 1b/1b encoding as defined in § Table 4-2.

**Table 4-2 Valid Encoding for Ordered Sets**

| Current Data Rate | Flit Mode Negotiated during Configuration when LinkUp=0b | Encoding | Comments |
|---|---|---|---|
| 2.5 GT/s, 5.0 GT/s | Yes / No | 8b/10b | Same Ordered Sets are used in Flit Mode as well as Non-Flit Mode. |
| 8.0 GT/s | No | 128b/130b | Only Standard SKP Ordered Set sent when SKP OS needs to be sent. Rest of the Ordered Sets are identical for Non-Flit Mode and Flit Mode in 8.0 GT/s. |
| 16.0 GT/s, 32.0 GT/s | No | 128b/130b | Alternates between Standard SKP OS and Control SKP OS, when SKP OS needs to be sent. Rest of the Ordered Sets are identical for Non-Flit Mode and Flit Mode in the corresponding Data Rate. |
| 8.0 GT/s, 16.0 GT/s, 32.0 GT/s | Yes | 128b/130b | Alternates between Standard SKP OS and Control SKP OS, when SKP OS needs to be sent. Rest of the Ordered Sets are identical for Non-Flit Mode and Flit Mode in the corresponding Data Rate. |
| 64.0 GT/s | Yes (mandatory) | 1b/1b | All Ordered Sets follow 1b/1b encoding at 64.0 GT/s with PAM4 signaling. Only Control SKP OS sent when SKP OS needs to be sent. |

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

**表 4-1 有效的编码与数据流模式组合**

| 当前数据速率 | 配置阶段 LinkUp=0b 时协商的 Flit 模式 | 编码 | 数据流 |
|---|---|---|---|
| 2.5 GT/s、5.0 GT/s | No | 8b/10b | 非 Flit 模式 |
| 2.5 GT/s、5.0 GT/s | Yes | 8b/10b | Flit 模式 |
| 8.0 GT/s、16.0 GT/s、32.0 GT/s | No | 128b/130b | 非 Flit 模式 |
| 8.0 GT/s、16.0 GT/s、32.0 GT/s | Yes | 128b/130b | Flit 模式 |
| 64.0 GT/s | Yes (mandatory,强制) | 1b/1b | Flit 模式 |

有序集 (Ordered Set) 的编码遵循 8b/10b、128b/130b 与 1b/1b 编码,定义见 § 表 4-2。

**表 4-2 有序集的有效编码**

| 当前数据速率 | 配置阶段 LinkUp=0b 时协商的 Flit 模式 | 编码 | 注释 |
|---|---|---|---|
| 2.5 GT/s、5.0 GT/s | Yes / No | 8b/10b | Flit 模式与非 Flit 模式使用相同的有序集。 |
| 8.0 GT/s | No | 128b/130b | 需要发送 SKP OS 时,仅发送标准 SKP 有序集。8.0 GT/s 下其余有序集在非 Flit 模式与 Flit 模式中完全相同。 |
| 16.0 GT/s、32.0 GT/s | No | 128b/130b | 需要发送 SKP OS 时,在标准 SKP OS 与控制 SKP OS 之间交替。其余有序集在对应数据速率的非 Flit 模式与 Flit 模式中完全相同。 |
| 8.0 GT/s、16.0 GT/s、32.0 GT/s | Yes | 128b/130b | 需要发送 SKP OS 时,在标准 SKP OS 与控制 SKP OS 之间交替。其余有序集在对应数据速率的非 Flit 模式与 Flit 模式中完全相同。 |
| 64.0 GT/s | Yes (mandatory,强制) | 1b/1b | 64.0 GT/s 下所有有序集采用 1b/1b 编码,配合 PAM4 信号。需要发送 SKP OS 时,仅发送控制 SKP OS。 |

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>

<a id="sec-4-2-1"></a>
## 4.2.1 8b/10b Encoding for 2.5 GT/s and 5.0 GT/s Data Rates § | 2.5 GT/s 与 5.0 GT/s 数据速率下的 8b/10b 编码 §

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

At 2.5 and 5.0 GT/s, PCI Express uses an 8b/10b transmission code. The definition of this transmission code is identical to that specified in ANSI X3.230-1994, clause 11 (and also IEEE 802.3z, 36.2.4). Using this scheme, 8-bit data characters are treated as 3 bits and 5 bits mapped onto a 4-bit code group and a 6-bit code group, respectively. The control bit in conjunction with the data character is used to identify when to encode one of the 12 Special Symbols included in the 8b/10b transmission code. These code groups are concatenated to form a 10-bit Symbol. As shown in § Figure 4-2, ABCDE maps to abcdei and FGH maps to fghj.

**IMPLEMENTATION NOTE:**
**FLIT MODE IDENTIFICATION THROUGHOUT THE DOCUMENT**

Support for Flit Mode behavior is referenced five times in the specification through the use of the following fields/variables:

**Flit Mode Supported bit** - Bit 0 of the Data Rate Identifier Symbol of 8b/10b and 128b/130b encoded TS1s and TS2s. This bit is Set when the Flit Mode Supported bit in the PCI Express Capabilities Register is Set and the Flit Mode Disable bit in the Link Control Register is Clear. See § Table 4-25, § Table 4-26, and § Table 4-27.

**Flit_Mode_Enabled** - Variable that indicates whether or not Flit Mode has been sucesfully negotiated. See § Section 4.2.7.1.1 and § Section 4.2.7.3.2.

**Flit Mode Supported** - Field in the PCI Express Capabilities Register. Flit Mode is supported when this bit is Set. See § Section 7.5.3.2.

**Flit Mode Disable** - Field in the Link Control Register. This bit used to disable Flit Mode. This bit is most useful in Downstream Ports but is also defined for Upstream Ports (useful for crosslinks or by device firmware). See § Section 7.5.3.7. Setting this bit may be useful to workaround faulty hardware.

**Flit Mode Status** - Field in the Link Status 2 Register. Indicates that that the Link is or will be operating in Flit Mode. Should match the Flit_Mode_Enabled variable. See § Section 7.5.3.20.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

在 2.5 GT/s 与 5.0 GT/s 速率下,PCI Express 使用 8b/10b 传输编码。该传输编码的定义与 ANSI X3.230-1994 第 11 节 (亦即 IEEE 802.3z 第 36.2.4 节) 中的规定完全相同。在该方案中,8 位数据字符被拆分为 3 位和 5 位两组,分别映射到 4 位码组 (code group) 和 6 位码组。控制位 (control bit) 与数据字符一起用于指示何时将 8b/10b 传输码中包含的 12 个特殊符号 (Special Symbols) 之一进行编码。这些码组被串接形成一个 10 位的符号 (Symbol)。如 § 图 4-2 所示,ABCDE 映射为 abcdei,FGH 映射为 fghj。

**实现提示 (IMPLEMENTATION NOTE):**
**全文档的 Flit 模式识别**

本规范中有 5 处通过以下字段/变量引用 Flit 模式支持:

**Flit Mode Supported bit** (Flit 模式支持位) — 8b/10b 与 128b/130b 编码 TS1 和 TS2 中 Data Rate Identifier Symbol (数据速率标识符号) 的 Bit 0。当 PCI Express 能力寄存器 (Capabilities Register) 中的 Flit Mode Supported 位置 1 且链路控制寄存器 (Link Control Register) 中的 Flit Mode Disable 位清 0 时,此位置 1。参见 § 表 4-25、§ 表 4-26 与 § 表 4-27。

**Flit_Mode_Enabled** — 表示 Flit 模式是否已成功协商的变量。参见 § 第 4.2.7.1.1 节 与 § 第 4.2.7.3.2 节。

**Flit Mode Supported** (Flit 模式支持) — PCI Express 能力寄存器中的字段。该位置 1 时表示支持 Flit 模式。参见 § 第 7.5.3.2 节。

**Flit Mode Disable** (Flit 模式禁用) — 链路控制寄存器中的字段。该位用于禁用 Flit 模式。该位对下游端口 (Downstream Port) 最为有用,但在上游端口 (Upstream Port) 中也有定义 (可用于交叉链路 crosslink 或由设备固件使用)。参见 § 第 7.5.3.7 节。设置该位可能有助于绕过有缺陷的硬件。

**Flit Mode Status** (Flit 模式状态) — 链路状态 2 寄存器 (Link Status 2 Register) 中的字段。表示链路正在或将要在 Flit 模式下工作,应与 Flit_Mode_Enabled 变量一致。参见 § 第 7.5.3.20 节。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

[⬆️ 返回目录](#-本章目录-table-of-contents)

## 4.2.1.1 Symbol Encoding § | 符号编码 §

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

The bits of a Symbol are placed on a Lane starting with bit "a" and ending with bit "j". Examples are shown in § Figure 4-3 and § Figure 4-4.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

符号的各个位在通道 (Lane) 上以位 "a" 起始、位 "j" 结束的顺序发送。示例如 § 图 4-3 与 § 图 4-4 所示。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

<!-- 📄 Page 354 -->
---

<a id="sec-4-2-1-1-1"></a>
## 4.2.1.1.1 Serialization and De-serialization of Data § | 数据的串行化与解串行化 §


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

The 8b/10b encoding scheme provides Special Symbols that are distinct from the Data Symbols used to represent Characters. These Special Symbols are used for various Link Management mechanisms described later in this chapter. Special Symbols are also used to frame DLLPs and TLPs in Non-Flit Mode, using distinct Special Symbols to allow these two types of Packets to be quickly and easily distinguished. When Flit Mode is enabled, each Symbol (Byte) of the Data Stream is still encoded with 8b/10b encoding without the Framing described. The Flit Mode operation is described in § Section 4.2.3.1. Even when Flit Mode is enabled, the Ordered Sets follow the description provided in § Section 4.2.1, when operating in 2.5 GT/s or 5.0 GT/s Data Rates.

§ Table 4-3 shows the Special Symbols used for PCI Express and provides a brief description for each. These Symbols will be discussed in greater detail in following sections. Each of these Special Symbols, as well as the data Symbols, must be interpreted by looking at the 10-bit Symbol in its entirety.

**Table 4-3 Special Symbols in 8b/10b Encoding**

| Encoding | Symbol | Name | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| K28.5 | COM | Comma | Used for Lane and Link initialization and management |
| K27.7 | STP | Start TLP | Marks the start of a Transaction Layer Packet in Non-Flit Mode. Reserved in Flit Mode. |
| K28.2 | SDP | Start DLLP | Marks the start of a Data Link Layer Packet in Non-Flit Mode. Reserved in Flit Mode. |
| K29.7 | END | End | Marks the end of a Transaction Layer Packet or a Data Link Layer Packet in Non-Flit Mode. Reserved in Flit Mode. |
| K30.7 | EDB | EnD Bad | Marks the end of a nullified TLP in Non-Flit Mode. Reserved in Flit Mode. |
| K23.7 | PAD | Pad | Used in Framing in Non-Flit Mode only. It is also used in Link Width and Lane ordering negotiations in both Non-Flit Mode and Flit Mode. |
| K28.0 | SKP | Skip | Used for compensating for different bit rates for two communicating Ports. Used identically in Flit Mode and Non-Flit Mode. |
| K28.1 | FTS | Fast Training Sequence | Used within an Ordered Set to exit from L0s to L0 in Non-Flit Mode. No usage for this Encoding is defined in Flit Mode. |
| K28.3 | IDL | Idle | Used in the Electrical Idle Ordered Set (EIOS). Used identically in Flit Mode and Non-Flit Mode. |
| K28.4 | Reserved | Reserved | |
| K28.6 | Reserved | Reserved | |
| K28.7 | EIE | Electrical Idle Exit | Reserved in 2.5 GT/s. Used in the Electrical Idle Exit Ordered Set (EIEOS) and sent prior to sending FTS at data rates other than 2.5 GT/s |

The Symbol tables for the valid 8b/10b codes are given in Appendix B. These tables have one column for the positive disparity and one column for the negative disparity.

> **Note 59.** In § Chapter 4., PMUX packets follow the TLP framing rules for Non-Flit Mode. PMUX is not supported in Flit Mode.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

8b/10b 编码方案提供了一组与表示字符的数据符号 (Data Symbols) 不同的特殊符号 (Special Symbols)。这些特殊符号用于本章后述的各种链路管理 (Link Management) 机制。在非 Flit 模式中,特殊符号还用于对 DLLP 和 TLP 进行成帧 (Framing),使用不同的特殊符号以便快速、轻松地区分这两类报文。当 Flit 模式使能时,数据流的每个符号 (字节) 仍然使用 8b/10b 编码,但不使用上述成帧机制。Flit 模式的操作在 § 第 4.2.3.1 节 中描述。即使 Flit 模式使能,在 2.5 GT/s 或 5.0 GT/s 数据速率下,有序集仍然遵循 § 第 4.2.1 节 中给出的描述。

§ 表 4-3 列出了 PCI Express 使用的特殊符号,并对每个符号给出了简要说明。这些符号将在后续小节中详细讨论。每个特殊符号以及数据符号都必须通过查看完整的 10 位符号来解释。

**表 4-3 8b/10b 编码中的特殊符号**

| 编码 | 符号 | 名称 | 描述 |
|---|---|---|---|
| K28.5 | COM | Comma (逗号) | 用于通道和链路的初始化与管理 |
| K27.7 | STP | Start TLP (TLP 起始) | 在非 Flit 模式下标记 TLP (Transaction Layer Packet, 事务层包) 的开始。在 Flit 模式中保留。 |
| K28.2 | SDP | Start DLLP (DLLP 起始) | 在非 Flit 模式下标记 DLLP (Data Link Layer Packet, 数据链路层包) 的开始。在 Flit 模式中保留。 |
| K29.7 | END | End (结束) | 在非 Flit 模式下标记 TLP 或 DLLP 的结束。在 Flit 模式中保留。 |
| K30.7 | EDB | EnD Bad (错误结束) | 在非 Flit 模式下标记作废 (nullified) TLP 的结束。在 Flit 模式中保留。 |
| K23.7 | PAD | Pad (填充) | 仅在非 Flit 模式的成帧中使用。同时也用于 Flit 模式和非 Flit 模式下的链路宽度 (Link Width) 与通道排序 (Lane ordering) 协商。 |
| K28.0 | SKP | Skip (跳过) | 用于补偿两个通信端口之间的比特率差异。Flit 模式与非 Flit 模式中的使用相同。 |
| K28.1 | FTS | Fast Training Sequence (快速训练序列) | 在非 Flit 模式下,作为有序集的一部分用于从 L0s 退出至 L0。Flit 模式中未定义此编码的用途。 |
| K28.3 | IDL | Idle (空闲) | 用于电气空闲有序集 (EIOS, Electrical Idle Ordered Set)。Flit 模式与非 Flit 模式中的使用相同。 |
| K28.4 | Reserved | 保留 | |
| K28.6 | Reserved | 保留 | |
| K28.7 | EIE | Electrical Idle Exit (电气空闲退出) | 在 2.5 GT/s 速率下保留。在其他数据速率下,用于电气空闲退出有序集 (EIEOS, Electrical Idle Exit Ordered Set),在发送 FTS 之前发送 |

有效的 8b/10b 编码符号表见附录 B。这些表分别为正极性 (positive) 和负极性 (negative) 不平衡度 (disparity) 提供一列。

> **注释 59.** 在 § 第 4 章 中,PMUX 报文遵循非 Flit 模式的 TLP 成帧规则。Flit 模式不支持 PMUX。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

> **Figure 4-3.** Bit Transmission Order on Physical Lanes - x1 Example
> <img src="figures/chapter_04/fig_0354_2_tight.png" width="700">

**Figure 4-3 | 图 4-3**: 物理通道上的比特传输顺序 - x1 示例 (Bit Transmission Order on Physical Lanes - x1 Example)

> **Figure 4-4.** Bit Transmission Order on Physical Lanes - x4 Example
> <img src="figures/chapter_04/fig_0355_1_tight.png" width="700">

<a id="sec-4-2-1-1-2"></a>
## 4.2.1.1.2 Special Symbols for Framing and Link Management (K Codes) § | 成帧与链路管理的特殊符号 (K 码) §

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

(see Table 4-3 above for the full Special Symbols table)

(See following sections for Symbol tables for the valid 8b/10b codes in Appendix B. These tables have one column for the positive disparity and one column for the negative disparity.)

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

(完整特殊符号表见上方 表 4-3)

(完整的 8b/10b 编码符号表见附录 B,这些表分别为正负极性不平衡度提供一列。)

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

[⬆️ 返回目录](#-本章目录-table-of-contents)

---

<a id="sec-4-2-1-1-3"></a>
## 4.2.1.1.3 8b/10b Decode Rules § | 8b/10b 解码规则 §


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

A Transmitter is permitted to pick any disparity, unless otherwise required, when first transmitting differential data after being in an Electrical Idle state. The Transmitter must then follow proper 8b/10b encoding rules until the next Electrical Idle state is entered.

The initial disparity for a Receiver that detects an exit from Electrical Idle is set to the disparity of the first Symbol used to obtain Symbol lock. Disparity may also be re-initialized if Symbol lock is lost and regained during the transmission of differential information due to an implementation specific number of errors. All following received Symbols after the initial disparity is set must be found in the proper column corresponding to the current running disparity.

If a received Symbol is found in the column corresponding to the incorrect running disparity or if the Symbol does not correspond to either column, the Physical Layer must notify the Data Link Layer that the received Symbol is invalid. This is a Receiver Error, and is a reported error associated with the Port (see § Section 6.2) in Non-Flit Mode. In Flit Mode, the Symbol in error is passed to the FEC logic to correct; the Receiver is permitted to send any 8-bit value to the FEC logic if an 8b/10b error or k-char is detected inside the Flit boundary.

There are two classes of framing and application of Symbols to Lanes. The first class consists of the Ordered Sets. The second class consists of TLPs and DLLPs in the Data Stream. Ordered Sets are always transmitted serially on each Lane, such that a full Ordered Set appears simultaneously on all Lanes of a multi-Lane Link. The Non-Flit Mode of Data Stream is described below. The Flit Mode description for Data Stream is described in § Section 4.2.3.2 and § Section 4.2.3.3.

There are no defined framing-related errors while using 8b/10b encoding in Flit Mode.

The Framing mechanism uses Special Symbol K28.2 "SDP" to start a DLLP and Special Symbol K27.7 "STP" to start a TLP. The Special Symbol K29.7 "END" is used to mark the end of either a TLP or a DLLP.

The conceptual stream of Symbols must be mapped from its internal representation, which is implementation dependent, onto the external Lanes. The Symbols are mapped onto the Lanes such that the first Symbol (representing Character 0) is placed onto Lane 0; the second is placed onto Lane 1; etc. The x1 Link represents a degenerate case and the mapping is trivial, with all Symbols placed onto the single Lane in order.

When no packet information or special Ordered Sets are being transmitted, the Transmitter is in the Logical Idle state. During this time idle data must be transmitted. The idle data must consist of the data byte 0 (00h), scrambled according to the rules of § Section 4.2.1.3 and 8b/10b encoded according to the rules of § Section 4.2.1.1, in the same way that TLP and DLLP Data Symbols are scrambled and encoded. Likewise, when the Receiver is not receiving any packet information or special Ordered Sets, the Receiver is in Logical Idle and shall receive idle data as described above. During transmission of the idle data, the SKP Ordered Set must continue to be transmitted as specified in § Section 4.2.8.

For the following rules, "placed" is defined to mean a requirement on the Transmitter to put the Symbol into the proper Lane of a Link.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

在从电气空闲 (Electrical Idle) 状态退出后首次发送差分数据时,允许发送器 (Transmitter) 选择任意极性的不平衡度,除非另有要求。此后发送器必须遵循正确的 8b/10b 编码规则,直到下一次进入电气空闲状态。

对于检测到退出电气空闲的接收器 (Receiver),其初始不平衡度被设置为用于获得符号锁定 (Symbol lock) 的第一个符号的不平衡度。如果在差分数据传输过程中由于特定于实现的错误数导致符号锁定丢失并重新获得,不平衡度也可以被重新初始化。在设定初始不平衡度之后,所有后续接收到的符号必须出现在与当前运行不平衡度 (running disparity) 相对应的正确列中。

如果接收到的符号出现在与当前运行不平衡度不对应的列中,或者该符号在两列中均无对应项,则物理层必须通知数据链路层该接收符号无效。这是一个接收器错误 (Receiver Error),在非 Flit 模式下是与该端口 (Port) 关联的可报告错误 (见 § 第 6.2 节)。在 Flit 模式下,出错的符号会被传递给 FEC (前向纠错) 逻辑进行纠正;如果在 Flit 边界内检测到 8b/10b 错误或 k-char,接收器可以向 FEC 逻辑发送任意 8 位值。

符号到通道的成帧与应用分为两类。第一类是有序集。第二类是数据流中的 TLP 和 DLLP。有序集总是以串行方式在每个通道上发送,使得完整的有序集同时出现在多通道链路的全部通道上。数据流的非 Flit 模式描述如下;数据流的 Flit 模式描述见 § 第 4.2.3.2 节 与 § 第 4.2.3.3 节。

在 Flit 模式中使用 8b/10b 编码时,没有定义与成帧相关的错误。

成帧机制使用特殊符号 K28.2 "SDP" 来开始一个 DLLP,使用特殊符号 K27.7 "STP" 来开始一个 TLP。特殊符号 K29.7 "END" 用于标记 TLP 或 DLLP 的结束。

符号的内部概念流必须从其依赖于实现的内部表示映射到外部通道。符号到通道的映射方式为:第一个符号 (表示字符 0) 放在 Lane 0 上,第二个放在 Lane 1 上,依此类推。x1 链路是一种退化情况,映射方式很平凡,所有符号按顺序放在单个通道上。

当没有发送任何报文信息或特殊有序集时,发送器处于逻辑空闲 (Logical Idle) 状态。在此期间必须发送空闲数据。空闲数据必须由数据字节 0 (00h) 组成,按 § 第 4.2.1.3 节 的规则进行加扰 (scrambling),并按 § 第 4.2.1.1 节 的规则进行 8b/10b 编码,其方式与 TLP 和 DLLP 数据符号的加扰和编码方式相同。同样,当接收器未接收到任何报文信息或特殊有序集时,接收器处于逻辑空闲状态,并应按上述描述接收空闲数据。在发送空闲数据期间,必须按照 § 第 4.2.8 节 的规定继续发送 SKP 有序集。

在下列规则中,"放置 (placed)" 的定义是要求发送器将符号放入链路的正确通道上。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>


[⬆️ 返回目录](#-本章目录-table-of-contents)

<!-- 📄 Page 357 -->

<a id="sec-4-2-1-2"></a>
## 4.2.1.2 Framing and Application of Symbols to Lanes § | 符号的成帧与到通道的应用 §

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

- TLPs must be framed by placing an STP Symbol at the start of the TLP and an END Symbol or EDB Symbol at the end of the TLP (see § Figure 4-5).
- A properly formed TLP contains a minimum of 18 symbols between the STP and END or EDB Symbols. If a received sequence has less than 18 symbols between the STP and END or EDB symbols, the receiver is permitted to treat this as a Receiver Error.
  - If checked, this is a reported error associated with the Receiving Port (see § Section 6.2).
- DLLPs must be framed by placing an SDP Symbol at the start of the DLLP and an END Symbol at the end of the DLLP (see § Figure 4-6).

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

- TLP 必须通过在 TLP 起始处放置一个 STP 符号,在 TLP 结束处放置一个 END 符号或 EDB 符号进行成帧 (见 § 图 4-5)。
- 一个正常组成的 TLP 在 STP 与 END 或 EDB 符号之间至少包含 18 个符号。如果接收到的序列在 STP 与 END 或 EDB 符号之间少于 18 个符号,接收器可将其视为接收器错误。
  - 如果进行检查,则这是与接收端口 (Receiving Port) 关联的可报告错误 (见 § 第 6.2 节)。
- DLLP 必须通过在 DLLP 起始处放置一个 SDP 符号,在 DLLP 结束处放置一个 END 符号进行成帧 (见 § 图 4-6)。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

[⬆️ 返回目录](#-本章目录-table-of-contents)

---

<a id="sec-4-2-1-2-1"></a>
## 4.2.1.2.1 Framing and Application of Symbols to Lanes for TLPs and DLLPs in Non-Flit Mode § | 非 Flit 模式下 TLP 和 DLLP 的成帧与到通道的应用 §


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

- Logical Idle is defined to be a period of one or more Symbol Times when no information: TLPs, DLLPs or any type of Special Symbol is being Transmitted/Received. Unlike Electrical Idle, during Logical Idle the Idle data Symbol (00h) is being transmitted and received.
  - When the Transmitter is in Logical Idle, the Idle data Symbol (00h) shall be transmitted on all Lanes. This is scrambled according to the rules in § Section 4.2.1.3.
  - Receivers must ignore incoming Idle data Symbols, and must not have any dependency other than scramble sequencing on any specific data patterns.
- For Links wider than x1, the STP Symbol (representing the start of a TLP) must be placed in Lane 0 when starting Transmission of a TLP from a Logical Idle Link condition.
- For Links wider than x1, the SDP Symbol (representing the start of a DLLP) must be placed in Lane 0 when starting Transmission of a DLLP from a Logical Idle Link condition.
- The STP Symbol must not be placed on the Link more frequently than once per Symbol Time.
- The SDP Symbol must not be placed on the Link more frequently than once per Symbol Time.
- As long as the above rules are satisfied, TLP and DLLP Transmissions are permitted to follow each other successively.
- One STP Symbol and one SDP Symbol may be placed on the Link in the same Symbol Time.
  - Links wider than x4 can have STP and SDP Symbols placed in Lane 4*N, where N is a positive integer. For example, for x8, STP and SDP Symbols can be placed in Lanes 0 and 4; and for x16, STP and SDP Symbols can be placed in Lanes 0, 4, 8, or 12.
- For xN Links where N is 8 or more, if an END or EDB Symbol is placed in a Lane K, where K does not equal N-1, and is not followed by an STP or SDP Symbol in Lane K+1 (i.e., there is no TLP or DLLP immediately following), then PAD Symbols must be placed in Lanes K+1 to Lane N-1.
  - For example, on a x8 Link, if END or EDB is placed in Lane 3, PAD must be placed in Lanes 4 to 7, when not followed by STP or SDP.
- The EDB Symbol is used to mark the end of a nullified TLP. Refer to § Section 3.6.2.1 for information on the usage of EDB.
- Receivers may optionally check for violations of the rules of this section. These checks are independently optional (see § Section 6.2.3.4). If checked, violations are Receiver Errors, and are reported errors associated with the Port (see § Section 6.2).

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

- 逻辑空闲 (Logical Idle) 定义为一个或多个符号时间 (Symbol Time) 的时间段,在此期间没有传输/接收任何信息:TLP、DLLP 或任何类型的特殊符号。与电气空闲不同,在逻辑空闲期间,空闲数据符号 (00h) 正在被发送和接收。
  - 当发送器处于逻辑空闲时,空闲数据符号 (00h) 应在所有通道上发送。此数据按 § 第 4.2.1.3 节 的规则进行加扰。
  - 接收器必须忽略输入的空闲数据符号,且除加扰序列之外,不得对任何特定数据模式产生依赖。
- 对于宽度大于 x1 的链路,STP 符号 (表示 TLP 的开始) 在从逻辑空闲链路状态开始发送 TLP 时必须放置在 Lane 0。
- 对于宽度大于 x1 的链路,SDP 符号 (表示 DLLP 的开始) 在从逻辑空闲链路状态开始发送 DLLP 时必须放置在 Lane 0。
- STP 符号在链路上放置的频率不得超过每个符号时间一次。
- SDP 符号在链路上放置的频率不得超过每个符号时间一次。
- 只要满足上述规则,TLP 和 DLLP 的传输可以紧接彼此连续进行。
- 一个 STP 符号和一个 SDP 符号可以放置在同一符号时间的链路上。
  - 宽度大于 x4 的链路可以将 STP 和 SDP 符号放置在 Lane 4*N 上,其中 N 为正整数。例如,对于 x8,STP 和 SDP 符号可放置在 Lane 0 和 Lane 4;对于 x16,STP 和 SDP 符号可放置在 Lane 0、4、8 或 12。
- 对于 N ≥ 8 的 xN 链路,如果 END 或 EDB 符号被放置在 Lane K 上 (K ≠ N-1),且其后没有紧跟 STP 或 SDP 符号 (即没有立即跟随的 TLP 或 DLLP),则必须在 Lane K+1 到 Lane N-1 上放置 PAD 符号。
  - 例如,在 x8 链路上,如果 END 或 EDB 被放置在 Lane 3,当其后没有 STP 或 SDP 时,PAD 必须被放置在 Lane 4 到 Lane 7。
- EDB 符号用于标记作废 TLP 的结束。EDB 的使用信息见 § 第 3.6.2.1 节。
- 接收器可选择性地检查对本节规则的违反。这些检查可独立启用 (见 § 第 6.2.3.4 节)。如果进行检查,违反规则将作为接收器错误,作为与该端口关联的可报告错误上报 (见 § 第 6.2 节)。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

> **Figure 4-5.** TLP with Framing Symbols Applied
> <img src="figures/chapter_04/fig_0358_1_tight.png" width="700">

> **Figure 4-6.** DLLP with Framing Symbols Applied
> <img src="figures/chapter_04/fig_0359_1_tight.png" width="700">

> **Figure 4-7.** Framed TLP on a x1 Link
> <img src="figures/chapter_04/fig_0359_2_tight.png" width="700">

**Figure 4-7 | 图 4-7**: x1 链路上成帧后的 TLP (Framed TLP on a x1 Link)

> **Figure 4-8.** Framed TLP on a x2 Link
> <img src="figures/chapter_04/fig_0360_1_tight.png" width="700">

**Figure 4-8 | 图 4-8**: x2 链路上成帧后的 TLP (Framed TLP on a x2 Link)

</div>


[⬆️ 返回目录](#-本章目录-table-of-contents)

---

<!-- 📄 Page 358 -->
---

---

<!-- 📄 Page 359 -->
---

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

In order to improve electrical characteristics of a Link, data is typically scrambled. This is applicable for both the Flit Mode as well as Non-Flit Mode at 2.5 GT/s and 5.0 GT/s Data Rates. This involves XORing the data stream with a pattern generated by a Linear Feedback Shift Register (LFSR). On the Transmit side, scrambling is applied to characters prior to the 8b/10b encoding. On the Receive side, de-scrambling is applied to characters after 8b/10b decoding.

On a multi-Lane Link, the scrambling function can be implemented with one or many LFSRs. When there is more than one Transmit LFSR per Link, these must operate in concert, maintaining the same simultaneous (Lane-to-Lane Output Skew) value in each LFSR. When there is more than one Receive LFSR per Link, these must operate in concert, maintaining the same simultaneous (Lane-to-Lane Skew) value in each LFSR. Regardless of how they are implemented, LFSRs must interact with data on a Lane-by-Lane basis as if there was a separate LFSR as described here for each Lane within that Link.

The LFSR is graphically represented in § Figure 4-10. Scrambling or unscrambling is performed by serially XORing the 8-bit (D0-D7) character with the 16-bit (D0-D15) output of the LFSR. An output of the LFSR, D15, is XORed with D0 of the data to be processed. The LFSR and data register are then serially advanced and the output processing is repeated for D1 through D7. The LFSR is advanced after the data is XORed. The LFSR implements the polynomial:

G(X) = X^16 + X^5 + X^4 + X^3 + 1

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">



为了改善链路的电气特性,数据通常会被加扰 (scramble)。这在 2.5 GT/s 和 5.0 GT/s 数据速率下的 Flit 模式与非 Flit 模式中均适用。该过程将数据流与一个由线性反馈移位寄存器 (Linear Feedback Shift Register, LFSR) 生成的模式进行异或 (XOR)。在发送端,加扰在 8b/10b 编码之前对字符进行;在接收端,解扰在 8b/10b 解码之后对字符进行。

在多通道链路上,加扰功能可以使用一个或多个 LFSR 实现。当一条链路上有多个发送 LFSR 时,这些 LFSR 必须协同工作,在每个 LFSR 中保持相同的同步 (Lane-to-Lane Output Skew,通道间输出偏移) 值。当一条链路上有多个接收 LFSR 时,这些 LFSR 必须协同工作,在每个 LFSR 中保持相同的同步 (Lane-to-Lane Skew,通道间偏移) 值。无论如何实现,LFSR 必须以逐通道 (Lane-by-Lane) 的方式与数据交互,就好像该链路内每条通道都有一个独立的 LFSR 一样。

LFSR 的图形化表示见 § 图 4-10。加扰或解扰通过对 8 位 (D0-D7) 字符与 LFSR 的 16 位 (D0-D15) 输出进行串行异或完成。LFSR 的输出 D15 与待处理数据的 D0 进行异或。然后 LFSR 和数据寄存器串行前进,重复对 D1 到 D7 进行输出处理。LFSR 在数据异或之后前进。该 LFSR 实现的多项式为:

G(X) = X^16 + X^5 + X^4 + X^3 + 1

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

<a id="sec-4-2-1-3"></a>
## 4.2.1.3 Data Scrambling § | 数据加扰 §


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

The mechanism(s) and/or interface(s) utilized by the Data Link Layer to notify the Physical Layer to disable scrambling is implementation specific and beyond the scope of this specification.

The data scrambling rules are the following:

- The COM Symbol initializes the LFSR.
- The LFSR value is advanced eight serial shifts for each Symbol except the SKP.
- All data Symbols (D codes) except those within Ordered Sets (e.g., TS1, TS2, EIEOS), the Compliance Pattern (see § Section 4.2.9), and the Modified Compliance Pattern (see § Section 4.2.10) are scrambled.
- All special Symbols (K codes) are not scrambled.
- The initialized value of an LFSR seed (D0-D15) is FFFFh. Immediately after a COM exits the Transmit LFSR, the LFSR on the Transmit side is initialized. Every time a COM enters the Receive LFSR on any Lane of that Link, the LFSR on the Receive side is initialized.
- Scrambling can only be disabled at the end of Configuration (see § Section 4.2.7.3.5).
- Scrambling does not apply to a Loopback Follower.
- Scrambling is always enabled in Detect by default.

For more information on scrambling, see § Appendix C.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

数据链路层用于通知物理层禁用加扰的机制和/或接口是实现相关的,超出本规范的范围。

数据加扰的规则如下:

- COM 符号用于初始化 LFSR。
- LFSR 的值在每个符号上前进 8 次串行移位,SKP 除外。
- 除有序集 (例如 TS1、TS2、EIEOS)、一致性测试码型 (Compliance Pattern,见 § 第 4.2.9 节) 和修改后的一致性测试码型 (Modified Compliance Pattern,见 § 第 4.2.10 节) 内的数据符号外,所有数据符号 (D codes) 都会被加扰。
- 所有特殊符号 (K codes) 不被加扰。
- LFSR 种子 (D0-D15) 的初始值为 FFFFh。在 COM 离开发送 LFSR 后,发送端的 LFSR 立即被初始化。每当 COM 在该链路的任何一条通道上进入接收 LFSR 时,接收端的 LFSR 即被初始化。
- 加扰只能在配置 (Configuration) 结束时被禁用 (见 § 第 4.2.7.3.5 节)。
- 加扰不适用于 Loopback Follower (回环从端)。
- 默认情况下,Detect 状态中加扰始终处于使能状态。

有关加扰的更多信息,见 § 附录 C。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

> **Figure 4-10.** LFSR with 8b/10b Scrambling Polynomial
> <img src="figures/chapter_04/fig_0361_1_tight.png" width="700">

</div>

<a id="sec-4-2-2"></a>
## 4.2.2 128b/130b Encoding for 8.0 GT/s, 16.0 GT/s, and 32.0 GT/s Data Rates § | 8.0 GT/s、16.0 GT/s 与 32.0 GT/s 数据速率下的 128b/130b 编码 §

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

> **IMPLEMENTATION NOTE:**
> **DISABLING SCRAMBLING**
>
> Disabling scrambling is intended to help simplify test and debug equipment. Control of the exact data patterns is useful in a test and debug environment. Since scrambling is reset at the Physical Layer there is no reasonable way to reliably control the state of the data transitions through software. Thus, the Disable Scrambling bit in the TS1 and TS2 Ordered Sets is provided for these purposes.
>
> The mechanism(s) and/or interface(s) utilized by the Data Link Layer to notify the Physical Layer to disable scrambling is implementation specific and beyond the scope of this specification.

When a PCI Express Link is operating at a data rate of 8.0 GT/s, 16.0 GT/s, or 32.0 GT/s, it uses the 128b/130b encoding rules described in this section. For backwards compatibility, the Link initially trains to L0 at the 2.5 GT/s data rate using 8b/10b encoding as described in § Section 4.2.1, then when the data rate is changed to 8.0 GT/s, 16.0 GT/s, or 32.0 GT/s, 128b/130b encoding is used. 128b/130b encoding uses a Link-wide packetization mechanism in Non-Flit Mode and a per-Lane block code with scrambling in both Flit Mode and Non-Flit Modes. In the Flit Mode, the Data Stream follows the same mechanism described in § Section 4.2.3.1, for the 128b payload inside the 128b/130b Data Block(s).

The basic entity of data transmission is an 8-bit data character, referred to as a Symbol, as shown in § Figure 4-11 and § Figure 4-12.

The Physical Layer uses a per-Lane block code. Each Block consists of a 2-bit Sync Header and a payload. There are two valid Sync Header encodings: 10b and 01b. The Sync Header defines the type of payload that the Block contains.

A Sync Header of 10b indicates a Data Block. Each Data Block has a 128 bit payload, resulting in a Block size of 130 bits. The payload is a Data Stream described in § Section 4.2.2.3.

A Sync Header of 01b indicates an Ordered Set Block. Each Ordered Set Block has a 128 bit payload, resulting in a Block size of 130 bits except for the SKP Ordered Set which can be of variable length.

All Lanes of a multi-Lane Link must transmit Blocks with the same Sync Header simultaneously, except when transmitting Jitter Measurement Pattern in Polling.Compliance.

The bit transmission order is as follows. A Sync Header represented as 'H1H0' is placed on a Lane starting with 'H0' and ending with 'H1'. A Symbol, represented as 'S7S6S5S4S3S2S1S0', is placed on a Lane starting with 'S0' and ending with 'S7'. In the diagrams that show a time scale, bits represent the transmission order. In layout diagrams, bits are arranged in little-endian format, consistent with packet layout diagrams in other chapters of this specification.

> **Figure 4-11.** Example of Bit Transmission Order in a x1 Link Showing 130 Bits of a Block
> <img src="figures/chapter_04/fig_0362_1_tight.png" width="700">

> **IMPLEMENTATION NOTE:**
> **SYMBOL IN 128B/130B ENCODING SCHEME**
> In the 128b/130b encoding scheme, the Symbol is one byte long, similar to the 10-bit Symbol of 8b/10b encoding.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

> **实现提示 (IMPLEMENTATION NOTE):**
> **禁用加扰 (DISABLING SCRAMBLING)**
>
> 禁用加扰的目的是简化测试和调试设备。对精确数据模式的控制在测试与调试环境中非常有用。由于加扰在物理层被复位,无法通过软件以合理方式可靠地控制数据转换的状态。因此,TS1 与 TS2 有序集中提供了 Disable Scrambling 位 (禁用加扰位) 用于此目的。
>
> 数据链路层用于通知物理层禁用加扰的机制和/或接口是实现相关的,超出本规范的范围。

当 PCI Express 链路以 8.0 GT/s、16.0 GT/s 或 32.0 GT/s 的数据速率工作时,它使用本节描述的 128b/130b 编码规则。为保持向后兼容,链路最初使用 § 第 4.2.1 节 描述的 8b/10b 编码在 2.5 GT/s 数据速率下训练到 L0,之后当数据速率切换到 8.0 GT/s、16.0 GT/s 或 32.0 GT/s 时,改用 128b/130b 编码。128b/130b 编码在非 Flit 模式下使用一种链路范围 (Link-wide) 的成包机制,并在 Flit 模式和非 Flit 模式下都使用一种带加扰的逐通道 (per-Lane) 块编码。在 Flit 模式中,数据流遵循 § 第 4.2.3.1 节 中针对 128b/130b 数据块内 128 位载荷所描述的相同机制。

数据传输的基本实体是一个 8 位数据字符,称为符号 (Symbol),如 § 图 4-11 和 § 图 4-12 所示。

物理层使用逐通道的块编码。每个块 (Block) 由 2 位同步头 (Sync Header) 和一个载荷 (payload) 组成。同步头有两个有效的编码值:10b 和 01b。同步头定义了块所包含的载荷类型。

同步头 10b 表示这是一个数据块 (Data Block)。每个数据块具有 128 位载荷,因此块大小为 130 位。载荷是 § 第 4.2.2.3 节 描述的数据流。

同步头 01b 表示这是一个有序集块 (Ordered Set Block)。每个有序集块具有 128 位载荷,因此块大小为 130 位,SKP 有序集除外,它的长度是可变的。

多通道链路的所有通道必须同时发送具有相同同步头的块,但在 Polling.Compliance 中发送抖动测量码型 (Jitter Measurement Pattern) 时除外。

位传输顺序如下。表示为 'H1H0' 的同步头从 'H0' 开始、到 'H1' 结束地放置在通道上。表示为 'S7S6S5S4S3S2S1S0' 的符号从 'S0' 开始、到 'S7' 结束地放置在通道上。在带时间刻度的图中,位表示传输顺序;在布局图中,位按小端 (little-endian) 格式排列,与本规范其他章节中的报文布局图保持一致。


> **实现提示 (IMPLEMENTATION NOTE):**
> **128b/130b 编码方案中的符号 (SYMBOL IN 128B/130B ENCODING SCHEME)**
> 在 128b/130b 编码方案中,符号长度为 1 字节,与 8b/10b 编码中的 10 位符号类似。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

---

<!-- 📄 Page 362 -->
---


---

<a id="sec-4-2-2-1"></a>
## 4.2.2.1 Lane Level Encoding | 通道级编码


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

An Ordered Set Block contains a Sync Header followed by one Ordered Set. All Lanes of a multi-Lane Link must transmit the same Ordered Set type simultaneously. The first Symbol of the Ordered Set defines the type of Ordered Set. Subsequent symbols of the Ordered Set are defined by the Ordered Set type and need not be identical across lanes of a multi-Lane Link. The Ordered Sets are described in detail in § Section 4.2.5 and § Section 4.2.8 . Ordered Set Blocks are the same for both Flit Mode and Non-Flit Mode except for the use and frequency of SKP Ordered Set. In Flit Mode at 8.0 GT/s, both Standard SKP Ordered Sets and Control SKP Ordered Sets are used.

During Link training, the 130 bits of the Electrical Idle Exit Ordered Set (EIEOS) are a unique bit pattern that Receivers use to determine the location of the Block Sync Headers in the received bit stream. Conceptually, Receivers can be in three different phases of Block alignment: Unaligned, Aligned, and Locked. These phases are defined to illustrate the required behavior, but are not meant to specify a required implementation.

**Unaligned Phase**

Receivers enter this phase after a period of Electrical Idle, such as when the data rate is changed to one that uses 128b/130b encoding or when they exit a low-power Link state, or if directed (by an implementation specific method). In this phase, Receivers monitor the received bit stream for the EIEOS bit pattern. When one is detected, they adjust their alignment to it and proceed to the Aligned phase.

**Aligned Phase**

Receivers monitor the received bit stream for the EIEOS bit pattern and the received Blocks for a Start of Data Stream (SDS) Ordered Set. If an EIEOS bit pattern is detected on an alignment that does not match the current alignment, Receivers must adjust their alignment to the newly received EIEOS bit pattern. If an SDS Ordered Set is received, Receivers proceed to the Locked phase. Receivers are permitted to return to the Unaligned phase if an undefined Sync Header (00b or 11b) is received.

**Locked Phase**

Receivers must not adjust their Block alignment while in this phase. The Data Stream starts after an SDS Ordered Set, and adjusting the Block alignment would interfere with the processing of these Blocks. Receivers must return to the Unaligned or Aligned phase if an undefined Sync Header is received.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

有序集 Block (Ordered Set Block) 由一个 Sync Header 后跟一个 Ordered Set 组成。多通道链路 (multi-Lane Link) 的所有通道必须同时发送相同类型的有序集。有序集的第一个 Symbol 定义有序集的类型。有序集的后续符号由有序集类型定义,在多通道链路的不同通道之间不必完全相同。有序集的详细描述见 § 4.2.5 和 § 4.2.8。除了 SKP 有序集的使用和频率外,Flit 模式和非 Flit 模式的有序集 Block 相同。在 8.0 GT/s 的 Flit 模式下,使用标准 SKP 有序集和控制 SKP 有序集两种。

在链路训练 (Link Training) 期间,电气空闲退出有序集 (Electrical Idle Exit Ordered Set, EIEOS) 的 130 个比特构成一个唯一的比特模式,接收器 (Receiver) 使用它来确定接收比特流中 Block Sync Header 的位置。从概念上讲,接收器在块对齐 (Block Alignment) 中可能处于三个不同阶段:未对齐 (Unaligned)、已对齐 (Aligned) 和锁定 (Locked)。定义这些阶段是为了说明所要求的行为,但并不指定具体的实现方式。

**未对齐阶段 (Unaligned Phase)**

在经历一段电气空闲 (Electrical Idle) 之后,例如当数据速率切换到使用 128b/130b 编码的速率,或退出低功耗链路状态时,或被(以实现特定的方式)指示时,接收器进入此阶段。在此阶段,接收器监视接收到的比特流中的 EIEOS 比特模式。一旦检测到 EIEOS,接收器将其对齐调整到该 EIEOS,然后进入已对齐阶段。

**已对齐阶段 (Aligned Phase)**

接收器监视接收比特流中的 EIEOS 比特模式,并监视接收 Block 中的数据流开始 (Start of Data Stream, SDS) 有序集。如果在与当前对齐位置不匹配的位置上检测到 EIEOS 比特模式,接收器必须将其对齐调整到新接收的 EIEOS 比特模式。如果接收到 SDS 有序集,接收器进入锁定阶段。如果接收到未定义的 Sync Header(00b 或 11b),允许接收器返回到未对齐阶段。

**锁定阶段 (Locked Phase)**

在此阶段,接收器不得调整其块对齐。数据流从 SDS 有序集之后开始,调整块对齐会干扰对这些 Block 的处理。如果接收到未定义的 Sync Header,接收器必须返回到未对齐或已对齐阶段。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

> **Figure 4-12.** Example of Bit Placement in a x4 Link with One Block per Lane
> <img src="figures/chapter_04/fig_0363_1_tight.png" width="700">

</div>


[⬆️ 返回目录](#-本章目录-table-of-contents)

---

<a id="sec-4-2-2-2"></a>
## 4.2.2.2 Ordered Set Blocks | 有序集块

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

**Additional Requirements:**

- While in the Aligned or Locked phase, Receivers must adjust their alignment as necessary when a SKP Ordered Set is received. See § Section 4.2.8 for more information on SKP Ordered Sets.
- After any LTSSM transition to Recovery, Receivers must ignore all received TS Ordered Sets until they receive an EIEOS. Conceptually, receiving an EIEOS validates the Receiver's alignment and allows TS Ordered Set processing to proceed. If a received EIEOS initiates an LTSSM transition from L0 to Recovery, Receivers are permitted to process any TS Ordered Sets that follow the EIEOS or ignore them until another EIEOS is received after entering Recovery.
- Receivers are permitted to transition from the Locked phase to the Unaligned or Aligned phase as long as Data Stream processing is stopped. See § Section 4.2.2.3 for more information on Data Stream requirements.
- **Loopback Leads:** While in Loopback.Entry, Leads must be capable of adjusting their Receiver's Block alignment to received EIEOS bit patterns. While in Loopback.Active, Leads are permitted to transmit an EIEOS and adjust their Receiver's Block alignment to the looped back bit stream.
- **Loopback Followers:** While in Loopback.Entry, Followers must be capable of adjusting their Receiver's Block alignment to received EIEOS bit patterns. While in Loopback.Active, Followers must not adjust their Receiver's Block alignment. Conceptually, the Receiver is directed to the Locked phase when the Follower starts to loop back the received bit stream.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

**附加要求:**

- 在已对齐或锁定阶段,接收到 SKP 有序集时,接收器必须根据需要调整其对齐。有关 SKP 有序集的更多信息,请参见 § 4.2.8。
- 在 LTSSM (链路训练与状态机) 任何向 Recovery 状态的转换之后,接收器必须忽略所有接收到的 TS 有序集,直到接收到 EIEOS。从概念上讲,接收到 EIEOS 即验证了接收器的对齐,并允许 TS 有序集处理继续进行。如果接收到的 EIEOS 启动了 LTSSM 从 L0 到 Recovery 的转换,则允许接收器处理该 EIEOS 之后紧跟的 TS 有序集,也可以忽略它们,直到进入 Recovery 之后再接收到另一个 EIEOS 为止。
- 只要停止数据流处理,允许接收器从锁定阶段转换到未对齐或已对齐阶段。有关数据流要求的更多信息,请参见 § 4.2.2.3。
- **回环引导方 (Loopback Leads):** 在 Loopback.Entry 期间,引导方 (Leads) 必须能够根据接收到的 EIEOS 比特模式调整其接收器的块对齐。在 Loopback.Active 期间,允许引导方发送 EIEOS 并将其接收器的块对齐调整到环回的比特流。
- **回环跟随方 (Loopback Followers):** 在 Loopback.Entry 期间,跟随方 (Followers) 必须能够根据接收到的 EIEOS 比特模式调整其接收器的块对齐。在 Loopback.Active 期间,跟随方不得调整其接收器的块对齐。从概念上讲,当跟随方开始环回接收到的比特流时,其接收器被引导进入锁定阶段。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

[⬆️ 返回目录](#-本章目录-table-of-contents)

<a id="sec-4-2-2-2-1"></a>
## 4.2.2.2.1 Block Alignment | 块对齐

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

> **IMPLEMENTATION NOTE: DETECTION OF LOSS OF BLOCK ALIGNMENT**
> The sequence of EIEOS and TS Ordered Sets transmitted during training sequences will cause misaligned Receivers to detect an undefined Sync Header.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

> **实现说明:块对齐丢失的检测**
> 在训练序列期间发送的 EIEOS 和 TS 有序集序列将使未对齐的接收器检测到未定义的 Sync Header。

</td>

<a id="sec-4-2-2-3"></a>
## 4.2.2.3 Data Blocks | 数据块


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

The payload of Data Blocks is a stream of Symbols defined as a "Data Stream". In Non-Flit Mode, the Data Stream consists of Framing Tokens, TLPs, and DLLPs. In Flit Mode, the Data Stream is described in § Section 4.2.3.1 . Each Symbol of the Data Stream is placed on a single Lane of the Link, and the stream of Symbols is striped across all Lanes of the Link and spans Block boundaries.

A Data Stream starts with the first Symbol of the Data Block that follows an SDS Ordered Set. It ends either when a Framing Error is detected or with the last Symbol of the Data Block that precedes an Ordered Set other than a SKP Ordered Set. SKP Ordered Sets that occur within a Data Stream have specific requirements as described in the following sections.

The Framing Tokens used by the Physical Layer in Non-Flit Mode are shown in § Table 4-4. Each Framing Token specifies or implies the number of Symbols associated with the Token and therefore the location of the next Framing Token. § Figure 4-15 shows an example of TLPs, DLLPs, and IDLs transmitted on a x8 link.

The first Framing Token of a Data Stream is always located in Symbol 0 of Lane 0 of the first Data Block of the Data Stream. For the rest of this chapter, the terms Framing Token and Token are used interchangeably.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

数据块 (Data Block) 的有效负载是定义为"数据流 (Data Stream)"的 Symbol 流。在非 Flit 模式 (Non-Flit Mode) 下,数据流由帧定界符 (Framing Token)、TLP 和 DLLP 组成。在 Flit 模式下,数据流的描述见 § 4.2.3.1。数据流的每个 Symbol 放置在链路的一个通道 (Lane) 上,Symbol 流以条带方式分布在链路的所有通道上,并跨越 Block 边界。

数据流从 SDS 有序集之后第一个数据块的第一个 Symbol 开始。它在以下两种情况下结束:检测到帧错误 (Framing Error),或者到达非 SKP 有序集之前最后一个数据块的最后一个 Symbol。数据流中出现的 SKP 有序集具有以下各节中所述的特定要求。

非 Flit 模式下物理层使用的帧定界符如 § 表 4-4 所示。每个帧定界符指定或隐含了与该 Token 关联的 Symbol 数,因此也就指定了下一个帧定界符的位置。§ 图 4-15 显示了在 x8 链路上发送 TLP、DLLP 和 IDL 的示例。

数据流的第一个帧定界符始终位于数据流第一个数据块 Lane 0 的 Symbol 0。在本章其余部分,术语"Framing Token"和"Token"可互换使用。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>


[⬆️ 返回目录](#-本章目录-table-of-contents)

---

<a id="sec-4-2-2-3-1"></a>
## 4.2.2.3.1 Framing Tokens in Non-Flit-Mode | 非 Flit 模式下的帧定界符

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

**Table 4-4 Framing Token Encoding | 表 4-4 帧定界符编码**

| Framing Token | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| **IDL** | Logical Idle | Logical Idle. The Framing Token is 1 Symbol. This Token is transmitted when no TLPs or DLLPs or other Framing Tokens are being transmitted. |
| **SDP** | Start of DLLP | Start of DLLP. The Framing Token is 2 Symbols long and is followed by the DLLP information. |
| **STP** | Start of TLP | Start of TLP. The Framing Token is 4 Symbols long and includes the 12-bit TLP Sequence Number. It is followed by the TLP information. |
| **EDB** | EnD Bad | EnD Bad. The Framing Token is 4 Symbols long and is used to confirm that the previous TLP was nullified. |
| **EDS** | End of Data Stream | End of Data Stream. The Framing Token is four Symbols long and indicates that the next Block will be an Ordered Set Block. |

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

**Table 4-4 Framing Token Encoding | 表 4-4 帧定界符编码**

| 帧定界符 | 类型 | 描述 |
|---|---|---|
| **IDL** | 逻辑空闲 (Logical Idle) | 逻辑空闲。该帧定界符为 1 个 Symbol。当没有 TLP、DLLP 或其他帧定界符正在发送时,发送此 Token。 |
| **SDP** | DLLP 起始 (Start of DLLP) | DLLP 起始。该帧定界符长 2 个 Symbol,后跟 DLLP 信息。 |
| **STP** | TLP 起始 (Start of TLP) | TLP 起始。该帧定界符长 4 个 Symbol,包含 12 位的 TLP 序列号 (Sequence Number),后跟 TLP 信息。 |
| **EDB** | 异常结束 (EnD Bad) | 异常结束。该帧定界符长 4 个 Symbol,用于确认前一个 TLP 已被作废 (nullified)。 |
| **EDS** | 数据流结束 (End of Data Stream) | 数据流结束。该帧定界符长 4 个 Symbol,表示下一个 Block 将是有序集 Block。 |

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

<a id="sec-4-2-2-3-1-tlp-layout"></a>
### 物理层 TLP 布局 / Physical Layer TLP Layout


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

The Physical Layer TLP layout is shown in § Figure 4-14. Details of the STP Framing Token are shown in § Figure 4-13. The length of the TLP (in DWs) being transmitted is specified by an 11-bit field called TLP Length. The TLP Length field is the total amount of information transferred, including the Framing Token, TLP Prefixes (if any), TLP Header, TLP data payload (if any), TLP digest (if any), TLP PCRC (if any), TLP MAC (if any), and TLP LCRC. For example, if a TLP has a 3 DW header, a 1 DW data payload, and does not include a TLP digest, the TLP Length field value is 6: 1 (Framing Token) + 0 (TLP Prefixes) + 3 (TLP header) + 1 (TLP data payload) + 0 (TLP digest) + 1 (TLP LCRC). If the same TLP included a TLP digest, the TLP Length field value would be 7. When a TLP is nullified, the EDB Token is considered an extension of the TLP but is not included in the calculation of the TLP Length field.

The TLP Length field is protected by a 4-bit CRC (Frame CRC), and an even parity bit (Frame Parity) protects both the TLP Length and Frame CRC fields. The Frame CRC and Frame Parity are calculated as follows:

```
C[0] = L[10] ^ L[7] ^ L[6] ^ L[4] ^ L[2] ^ L[1] ^ L[0]
C[1] = L[10] ^ L[9] ^ L[7] ^ L[5] ^ L[4] ^ L[3] ^ L[2]
C[2] = L[9] ^ L[8] ^ L[6] ^ L[4] ^ L[3] ^ L[2] ^ L[1]
C[3] = L[8] ^ L[7] ^ L[5] ^ L[3] ^ L[2] ^ L[1] ^ L[0]
P = L[10] ^ L[9] ^ L[8] ^ L[7] ^ L[6] ^ L[5] ^ L[4] ^ L[3] ^ L[2] ^ L[1] ^ L[0] ^ C[3] ^ C[2] ^ C[1] ^ C[0]
```

The Frame Parity reduces to `P = L[10] ^ L[9] ^ L[8] ^ L[6] ^ L[5] ^ L[2] ^ L[0]`

The TLP Length field is represented in the above equations as L[10:0], where L[0] is the least significant bit and L[10] is the most significant bit. Transmitters calculate the Frame CRC and Frame Parity before transmission. Receivers must calculate the Frame CRC and Frame Parity using the same algorithm as the transmitter and then compare the calculated values to the received values.

STP Tokens do not have a TLP Length field value of 1. If a received sequence of Symbols matches the format of an STP Token with a TLP Length field value of 1, the Symbols are evaluated to determine whether they match the EDS Token.

Note that, for TLPs, the Data Link Layer prepends 4 Reserved bits (0000b) to the TLP Sequence Number field before it calculates the LCRC. These Reserved bits are not explicitly transmitted when using 128b/130b encoding, and Receivers assume that the 4 bits received are 0000b when calculating the LCRC.

Packets containing a TLP Length field that is greater than 1535 are PMUX Packets. For such packets, the actual packet length is computed differently, the TLP Sequence Number field in the STP Token contains other information, and the Link CRC is computed using different rules. See § Appendix G. for details.

Packets containing a TLP Length field that is between 1152 and 1535 (inclusive) are reserved for future standardization.

Transmitters must transmit all DWs of a TLP specified by the TLP Length field of the STP Framing Token. TLPs are never truncated when using 128b/130b encoding - even when nullified. § Figure 4-16 shows an example of a nullified 23 DW TLP.

> **IMPLEMENTATION NOTE: FRAME CRC AND FRAME PARITY**
> The Frame CRC bits are effectively calculated as (L[0] X14 + L[1] X13 + … + L[9] X5 + L[10] X4) mod (X4 + X +1). It should be noted that X4 + X + 1 is a primitive polynomial and the CRC can detect two bit errors. The Frame Parity bit can detect an odd number of bit errors. Thus, the Frame CRC and Frame Parity together guarantee three bit error detection for the TLP Length field. It must be noted that even though in the reduced Frame Parity equation all terms are not present, it still maintains the property of detecting odd bit errors. Only those TLP Length field bits which are present in an even number of CRC terms are used in the calculation.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

物理层 TLP 布局如 § 图 4-14 所示。STP 帧定界符的详细信息如 § 图 4-13 所示。正在发送的 TLP 长度(以 DW 为单位)由一个称为 TLP Length 的 11 位字段指定。TLP Length 字段是所传输信息的总量,包括帧定界符、TLP 前缀 (Prefix, 如果有)、TLP 包头 (Header)、TLP 数据负载 (Payload, 如果有)、TLP 摘要 (Digest, 如果有)、TLP PCRC(如果有)、TLP MAC(如果有)和 TLP LCRC。例如,如果一个 TLP 包含 3 DW 包头、1 DW 数据负载且不含 TLP 摘要,则 TLP Length 字段值为 6:1(帧定界符)+ 0(TLP 前缀)+ 3(TLP 包头)+ 1(TLP 数据负载)+ 0(TLP 摘要)+ 1(TLP LCRC)。如果同一 TLP 包含 TLP 摘要,则 TLP Length 字段值为 7。当 TLP 被作废时,EDB Token 被视为 TLP 的扩展,但不计入 TLP Length 字段的计算。

TLP Length 字段由 4 位 CRC(Frame CRC)保护,一位偶校验位 (Frame Parity) 同时保护 TLP Length 和 Frame CRC 字段。Frame CRC 和 Frame Parity 的计算方法如下:

```
C[0] = L[10] ^ L[7] ^ L[6] ^ L[4] ^ L[2] ^ L[1] ^ L[0]
C[1] = L[10] ^ L[9] ^ L[7] ^ L[5] ^ L[4] ^ L[3] ^ L[2]
C[2] = L[9] ^ L[8] ^ L[6] ^ L[4] ^ L[3] ^ L[2] ^ L[1]
C[3] = L[8] ^ L[7] ^ L[5] ^ L[3] ^ L[2] ^ L[1] ^ L[0]
P = L[10] ^ L[9] ^ L[8] ^ L[7] ^ L[6] ^ L[5] ^ L[4] ^ L[3] ^ L[2] ^ L[1] ^ L[0] ^ C[3] ^ C[2] ^ C[1] ^ C[0]
```

Frame Parity 可化简为 `P = L[10] ^ L[9] ^ L[8] ^ L[6] ^ L[5] ^ L[2] ^ L[0]`

在上述等式中,TLP Length 字段表示为 L[10:0],其中 L[0] 是最低有效位,L[10] 是最高有效位。发送器在发送前计算 Frame CRC 和 Frame Parity。接收器必须使用与发送器相同的算法计算 Frame CRC 和 Frame Parity,然后将计算值与接收值进行比较。

STP Token 的 TLP Length 字段值不会为 1。如果接收到的 Symbol 序列与 TLP Length 字段值为 1 的 STP Token 格式相匹配,则会进一步评估这些 Symbol,以确定它们是否与 EDS Token 匹配。

请注意,对于 TLP,数据链路层在计算 LCRC 之前会在 TLP Sequence Number 字段前添加 4 个保留比特 (0000b)。使用 128b/130b 编码时,这些保留比特不会被显式发送,接收器在计算 LCRC 时假设接收到的 4 个比特为 0000b。

TLP Length 字段值大于 1535 的报文是 PMUX 报文 (PMUX Packet)。对于这些报文,实际报文长度的计算方法不同,STP Token 中的 TLP Sequence Number 字段包含其他信息,并且链路 CRC 使用不同的规则计算。详见 § 附录 G。

TLP Length 字段值在 1152 到 1535(含)之间的报文保留供未来标准化使用。

发送器必须发送 STP 帧定界符的 TLP Length 字段所指定 TLP 的所有 DW。在使用 128b/130b 编码时,TLP 永远不会被截断,即使被作废也不会。§ 图 4-16 显示了一个 23 DW 的已作废 TLP 的示例。

> **实现说明:FRAME CRC 与 FRAME PARITY**
> Frame CRC 位的有效计算为 (L[0] X14 + L[1] X13 + … + L[9] X5 + L[10] X4) mod (X4 + X +1)。值得注意的是 X4 + X + 1 是一个本原多项式,该 CRC 可以检测两个比特错误。Frame Parity 位可以检测奇数个比特错误。因此,Frame CRC 和 Frame Parity 共同保证对 TLP Length 字段的三位比特错误检测能力。必须注意,即使在化简后的 Frame Parity 等式中并非所有项都出现,它仍保持检测奇数个比特错误的特性。仅在偶数个 CRC 项中出现的那些 TLP Length 字段比特才参与该计算。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>
