<a id="sec-4-2-2-3-2"></a>
## 4.2.2.3.2 Transmitter Framing Requirements in Non-Flit Mode | 非 Flit 模式下的发送器帧定界要求


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

The following requirements apply to the transmitted Data Stream.

**To Transmit a TLP:**

- Transmit an STP Token immediately followed by the complete TLP information provided by the Data Link Layer.
- All DWs of the TLP, as specified by the TLP Length field of the STP Token, must be transmitted, even if the TLP is nullified.
- If the TLP is nullified, an EDB Token must be transmitted immediately following the TLP. There must be no Symbols between the last Symbol of the TLP and the first Symbol of the EDB Token. The value of the TLP Length field of a nullified TLP's STP Token is not adjusted to account for the EDB Token.
- The STP Token must not be transmitted more frequently than once per Symbol Time.

**To Transmit a DLLP:**

- Transmit an SDP Token immediately followed by the complete DLLP information provided by the Data Link Layer.
- All 6 Symbols of the DLLP must be transmitted.
- The SDP Token must not be transmitted more frequently than once per Symbol Time.

**To Transmit a SKP Ordered Set within a Data Stream:**

- Transmit an EDS Token in the last DW of the current Data Block. For example, the Token is transmitted on Lane 0 in Symbol Times 12-15 of the Block for a x1 Link, and on Lanes 12-15 of Symbol Time 15 of the Block for a x16 Link.
- Transmit the SKP Ordered Set following the current Data Block.
- Transmit a Data Block following the SKP Ordered Set. The Data Stream resumes with the first Symbol of the Data Block. If multiple SKP Ordered Sets are scheduled for transmission, each SKP Ordered Set must be preceded by a Data Block with an EDS Token.

**To end a Data Stream:**

- Transmit an EDS Token in the last DW of the current Data Block, followed in the next block by an EIOS or an EIEOS. An EIOS is transmitted for LTSSM power management state transitions, and an EIEOS is transmitted for all other cases. For example, the Token is transmitted on Lane 0 in Symbol Times 12-15 of the Block for a x1 Link, and on Lanes 12-15 of Symbol Time 15 of the Block for a x16 Link.

- The IDL Token must be transmitted on all Lanes when not transmitting a TLP, DLLP, or other Framing Token.

**Multi-Lane Links:**

- After transmitting an IDL Token, the first Symbol of the next STP or SDP Token must be transmitted in Lane 0 of a future Symbol Time. An EDS Token can be transmitted after an IDL Token in the same Symbol Time, since it must be transmitted in the last DW of a Block.
- For xN Links where N is 8 or more, if an EDB Token, TLP, or DLLP ends in a Lane K, where K does not equal N-1, and it is not followed by the first Symbol of an STP, SDP, or EDB Token in Lane K+1, then IDL Tokens must be placed in Lanes K+1 to N-1. For example, on a x8 Link, if a TLP or DLLP ends in Lane 3, IDL Tokens must be placed in Lanes 4 to 7. The EDS Token is an exception to this requirement, and can be transmitted following IDL Tokens.
- Tokens, TLPs, and DLLPs are permitted to follow each other successively such that more than one Token may be transmitted in the same Symbol Time as long as their transmission conforms with the other requirements stated in this section.
  - Links wider than x4 can have Tokens placed starting on Lane 4*N, where N is a positive integer. For example, Tokens can be placed in Lanes 0 and 4 of a x8 Link, and Tokens can be placed in Lanes 0, 4, 8, or 12 of a x16 Link.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

以下要求适用于所发送的数据流。

**发送 TLP:**

- 发送一个 STP Token,紧接其后是由数据链路层提供的完整 TLP 信息。
- TLP 的所有 DW(由 STP Token 的 TLP Length 字段指定)都必须发送,即使 TLP 已被作废也是如此。
- 如果 TLP 被作废,必须在 TLP 之后立即发送一个 EDB Token。TLP 的最后一个 Symbol 与 EDB Token 的第一个 Symbol 之间不得有任何 Symbol。已作废 TLP 的 STP Token 中 TLP Length 字段的值不因 EDB Token 而调整。
- STP Token 的发送频率不得高于每个 Symbol Time 一次。

**发送 DLLP:**

- 发送一个 SDP Token,紧接其后是由数据链路层提供的完整 DLLP 信息。
- DLLP 的全部 6 个 Symbol 都必须发送。
- SDP Token 的发送频率不得高于每个 Symbol Time 一次。

**在数据流中发送 SKP 有序集:**

- 在当前数据块的最后一个 DW 发送一个 EDS Token。例如,对于 x1 链路,该 Token 在 Block 的 Symbol Time 12-15 的 Lane 0 上发送;对于 x16 链路,该 Token 在 Block 的 Symbol Time 15 的 Lanes 12-15 上发送。
- 在当前数据块之后发送 SKP 有序集。
- 在 SKP 有序集之后发送一个数据块。数据流从该数据块的第一个 Symbol 处恢复。如果安排了多个 SKP 有序集发送,则每个 SKP 有序集都必须由一个带有 EDS Token 的数据块作为前缀。

**结束数据流:**

- 在当前数据块的最后一个 DW 发送一个 EDS Token,然后在下一个块中发送 EIOS 或 EIEOS。LTSSM 电源管理状态转换发送 EIOS,其他所有情况发送 EIEOS。例如,对于 x1 链路,该 Token 在 Block 的 Symbol Time 12-15 的 Lane 0 上发送;对于 x16 链路,该 Token 在 Block 的 Symbol Time 15 的 Lanes 12-15 上发送。

- 当不发送 TLP、DLLP 或其他帧定界符时,必须在所有通道上发送 IDL Token。

**多通道链路:**

- 在发送 IDL Token 之后,下一个 STP 或 SDP Token 的第一个 Symbol 必须在未来某个 Symbol Time 的 Lane 0 上发送。EDS Token 可在同一 Symbol Time 中 IDL Token 之后发送,因为它必须在 Block 的最后一个 DW 中发送。
- 对于 N 为 8 或更大的 xN 链路,如果 EDB Token、TLP 或 DLLP 在 Lane K(K 不等于 N-1)上结束,且其后的 Lane K+1 上未紧跟 STP、SDP 或 EDB Token 的第一个 Symbol,则必须在 Lanes K+1 到 N-1 上填充 IDL Token。例如,在 x8 链路上,如果 TLP 或 DLLP 在 Lane 3 结束,则必须在 Lanes 4 到 7 填充 IDL Token。EDS Token 是此要求的例外,可以在 IDL Token 之后发送。
- Token、TLP 和 DLLP 允许依次紧接发送,只要其发送符合本节中的其他要求,就可以在同一 Symbol Time 中发送多个 Token。
  - 宽度大于 x4 的链路可以将 Token 放置在从 Lane 4*N 开始的通道上,其中 N 是正整数。例如,在 x8 链路上,Token 可以放置在 Lanes 0 和 4;在 x16 链路上,Token 可以放置在 Lanes 0、4、8 或 12。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>


[⬆️ 返回目录](#-本章目录-table-of-contents)

---

<!-- 📄 Page 370 -->
---

<a id="sec-4-2-2-3-3"></a>
## 4.2.2.3.3 Receiver Framing Requirements in Non-Flit Mode | 非 Flit 模式下的接收器帧定界要求

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

The following requirements apply to the received Data Stream and the Block type transitions that occur at the beginning and end of the Data Stream.

- When processing Symbols that are expected to be a Framing Token, receiving a Symbol or sequence of Symbols that does not match the definition of a Framing Token is a Framing Error. It is strongly recommended that Receivers of a multi-Lane Link report an error in the Lane Error Status Register for the Lane that receives the first Symbol of an expected Framing Token when that Symbol does not match Symbol +0 of an STP (bits [3:0] only), IDL, SDP, EDB, or EDS Token (see § Figure 4-13).
- All optional error checks and error reports in this section are independently optional (see § Section 6.2.3.4 ).
- **When an STP Token is received:**
  - Receivers must calculate the Frame CRC and Frame Parity of the received TLP Length field and compare the results to the received Frame CRC and Frame Parity fields. A Frame CRC or Frame Parity mismatch is a Framing Error.
    - An STP Token with Framing Error is not considered part of a TLP for the purpose of reporting to the Data Link Layer.
  - If the TLP Length field is 1, the Symbols are not an STP Token and are instead evaluated to determine whether they are an EDS Token.
  - Receivers are permitted to check whether the TLP Length field has a value of 0. If checked, receiving a TLP Length field of 0 is a Framing Error.
  - Receivers are permitted to check whether the TLP Length field has a value of 2, 3, or 4. If checked, receiving such a TLP Length field is a Framing Error.
  - Receivers are permitted to check whether the TLP Length field has a value between 1152 and 1535 (inclusive). If checked, receiving such a TLP Length field is a Framing Error.
  - Receivers on Ports that do not support Protocol Multiplexing are permitted to check whether the TLP Length field has a value greater than 1535. If checked, receiving such a TLP Length field is a Framing Error.
  - Receivers on Ports that support Protocol Multiplexing, shall process STP Tokens with a TLP Length field that is greater than 1535 as the start of a PMUX Packet as defined in § Appendix G. .

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

以下要求适用于所接收的数据流以及数据流开始和结束处发生的 Block 类型转换。

- 在处理预期为帧定界符的 Symbol 时,接收到与帧定界符定义不匹配的 Symbol 或 Symbol 序列,即为帧错误 (Framing Error)。强烈建议多通道链路的接收器在 Lane Error Status Register 中,对于接收到的预期帧定界符第一个 Symbol 与 STP(仅 [3:0] 位)、IDL、SDP、EDB 或 EDS Token 的 Symbol +0 不匹配的通道(参见 § 图 4-13)报告错误。
- 本节中的所有可选错误检查和错误报告都是各自独立的(参见 § 6.2.3.4)。
- **当接收到 STP Token 时:**
  - 接收器必须对所接收 TLP Length 字段计算 Frame CRC 和 Frame Parity,并将结果与所接收的 Frame CRC 和 Frame Parity 字段进行比较。Frame CRC 或 Frame Parity 不匹配即为帧错误。
    - 在向数据链路层报告时,带有帧错误的 STP Token 不视为 TLP 的一部分。
  - 如果 TLP Length 字段为 1,则这些 Symbol 不是 STP Token,而是进一步评估以确定它们是否是 EDS Token。
  - 允许接收器检查 TLP Length 字段值是否为 0。如果检查,接收到 TLP Length 字段为 0 即为帧错误。
  - 允许接收器检查 TLP Length 字段值是否为 2、3 或 4。如果检查,接收到这样的 TLP Length 字段即为帧错误。
  - 允许接收器检查 TLP Length 字段值是否在 1152 到 1535(含)之间。如果检查,接收到这样的 TLP Length 字段即为帧错误。
  - 对于不支持协议多路复用 (Protocol Multiplexing) 的端口上的接收器,允许检查 TLP Length 字段值是否大于 1535。如果检查,接收到这样的 TLP Length 字段即为帧错误。
  - 对于支持协议多路复用的端口上的接收器,必须将 TLP Length 字段值大于 1535 的 STP Token 作为 § 附录 G 所定义的 PMUX 报文的起始处理。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

[⬆️ 返回目录](#-本章目录-table-of-contents)

---

<!-- 📄 Page 371 -->
---

<a id="sec-4-2-2-3-3-cont"></a>
### 非 Flit 模式接收器帧定界要求(续)


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

  - The next Token to be processed begins with the Symbol immediately following the last DW of the TLP, as determined by the TLP Length field.
    - Receivers must evaluate this Symbol and determine whether it is the first Symbol of an EDB Token and therefore whether the TLP is nullified. See the EDB Token requirements.
  - Receivers are permitted to check whether more than one STP Token is received in a single Symbol Time. If checked, receiving more than one STP Token in a single Symbol Time is a Framing Error.
- **When an EDB Token is received:**
  - If an EDB Token is received immediately following a TLP (there are no Symbols between the last Symbol of the TLP and the first Symbol of the EDB Token), receivers must inform the Data Link Layer that an EDB Token has been received. Receivers are permitted to inform the Data Link Layer that an EDB Token has been received after processing the first Symbol of the EDB Token or after processing any or all of the remaining Symbols of the EDB Token. Regardless of when they inform the Data Link Layer of a received EDB Token, Receivers must check all Symbols of the EDB Token. Receiving a Symbol that does not match the definition of an EDB Token is a Framing Error.
  - Receiving an EDB Token at any time other than immediately following a TLP is a Framing Error.
  - The next Token to be processed begins with the Symbol immediately following the EDB Token.
- **When an EDS Token is received in the last four Symbols of the Data Block across the Link:**
  - Receivers must stop processing the Data Stream.
  - Receiving an Ordered Set other than SKP, EIOS, or EIEOS in the Block following the EDS Token is a Framing Error.
  - If a SKP Ordered Set is received in the Block following the EDS Token, Receivers resume Data Stream processing with the first Symbol of the Data Block that follows the SKP Ordered Set unless a Framing Error has been detected.
- **When an SDP Token is received:**
  - The next Token to be processed begins with the Symbol immediately following the last Symbol of the DLLP.
  - Receivers are permitted to check whether more than one SDP Token is received in a single Symbol Time. If checked, receiving more than one SDP Token in a single Symbol Time is a Framing Error.
- **When an IDL Token is received:**
  - For a x1 Link, the next Token to be processed begins with the next Symbol received.
  - For a x2 Link, the next Token to be processed begins with the Symbol received in Lane 0 of the next Symbol Time. It is strongly recommended that Receivers check whether the Symbol received in Lane 1, if it did not receive IDL, after an IDL Token was received in Lane 0 is also IDL and report an error for Lane 1 in the Lane Error Status Register. If checked, receiving a Symbol other than IDL is a Framing Error.
  - For a x4 Link, the next Token to be processed begins with the Symbol received in Lane 0 of the next Symbol Time. It is strongly recommended that Receivers check whether the Symbols received in Lanes 1-3, after an IDL Token was received in Lane 0 are also IDL and report an error for the Lane(s) that did not receive IDL, in the Lane Error Status Register. If checked, receiving a Symbol other than IDL is a Framing Error.
  - For x8 and x16 Links, the next Token to be processed begins with the Symbol received in the next DW aligned Lane following the IDL Token. For example, if an IDL Token is received in Lane 4 of a x16 Link, the next Token location begins with Lane 8 of the same Symbol Time. However, if an IDL Token is received on Lane 4 of a x8 Link, the next Token location begins with Lane 0 of the following Symbol Time. It is strongly recommended that Receivers check whether the Symbols received between the IDL Token and the next Token location are also IDL and report an error for the Lane(s) that did not receive IDL, in the Lane Error Status Register. If checked, receiving a Symbol other than IDL is a Framing Error.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

  - 下一个待处理的 Token 从 TLP 最后一个 DW 之后紧接的 Symbol 开始,该 DW 由 TLP Length 字段确定。
    - 接收器必须评估该 Symbol,以确定它是否是 EDB Token 的第一个 Symbol,从而确定 TLP 是否被作废。参见 EDB Token 要求。
  - 允许接收器检查在单个 Symbol Time 中是否接收到多个 STP Token。如果检查,在单个 Symbol Time 中接收到多个 STP Token 即为帧错误。
- **当接收到 EDB Token 时:**
  - 如果在 TLP 之后立即接收到 EDB Token(即 TLP 的最后一个 Symbol 与 EDB Token 的第一个 Symbol 之间没有任何 Symbol),接收器必须通知数据链路层已接收到 EDB Token。允许接收器在处理 EDB Token 的第一个 Symbol 之后,或处理 EDB Token 其余 Symbol 中的任何一个或全部之后,通知数据链路层已接收到 EDB Token。无论接收器何时通知数据链路层已接收到 EDB Token,都必须检查 EDB Token 的所有 Symbol。接收到与 EDB Token 定义不匹配的 Symbol 即为帧错误。
  - 在 TLP 之后立即接收之外的其他任何时间接收到 EDB Token,即为帧错误。
  - 下一个待处理的 Token 从 EDB Token 之后紧接的 Symbol 开始。
- **当在数据块横跨链路的最后四个 Symbol 中接收到 EDS Token 时:**
  - 接收器必须停止处理数据流。
  - 在 EDS Token 之后的 Block 中接收到非 SKP、EIOS 或 EIEOS 的有序集即为帧错误。
  - 如果在 EDS Token 之后的 Block 中接收到 SKP 有序集,则接收器在 SKP 有序集之后的数据块的第一个 Symbol 处恢复数据流处理,除非已检测到帧错误。
- **当接收到 SDP Token 时:**
  - 下一个待处理的 Token 从 DLLP 最后一个 Symbol 之后紧接的 Symbol 开始。
  - 允许接收器检查在单个 Symbol Time 中是否接收到多个 SDP Token。如果检查,在单个 Symbol Time 中接收到多个 SDP Token 即为帧错误。
- **当接收到 IDL Token 时:**
  - 对于 x1 链路,下一个待处理的 Token 从下一个接收到的 Symbol 开始。
  - 对于 x2 链路,下一个待处理的 Token 从下一个 Symbol Time 的 Lane 0 中接收到的 Symbol 开始。强烈建议接收器检查在 Lane 0 接收到 IDL Token 之后,Lane 1 中接收到的 Symbol(若未接收到 IDL)是否也是 IDL,并在 Lane Error Status Register 中针对 Lane 1 报告错误。如果检查,接收到非 IDL 的 Symbol 即为帧错误。
  - 对于 x4 链路,下一个待处理的 Token 从下一个 Symbol Time 的 Lane 0 中接收到的 Symbol 开始。强烈建议接收器检查在 Lane 0 接收到 IDL Token 之后,Lanes 1-3 中接收到的 Symbol 是否也是 IDL,并在 Lane Error Status Register 中针对未接收到 IDL 的通道报告错误。如果检查,接收到非 IDL 的 Symbol 即为帧错误。
  - 对于 x8 和 x16 链路,下一个待处理的 Token 从 IDL Token 之后下一个 DW 对齐的通道中接收到的 Symbol 开始。例如,如果在 x16 链路的 Lane 4 接收到 IDL Token,则下一个 Token 位置从同一 Symbol Time 的 Lane 8 开始。但是,如果在 x8 链路的 Lane 4 接收到 IDL Token,则下一个 Token 位置从下一个 Symbol Time 的 Lane 0 开始。强烈建议接收器检查 IDL Token 与下一个 Token 位置之间接收到的 Symbol 是否也是 IDL,并在 Lane Error Status Register 中针对未接收到 IDL 的通道报告错误。如果检查,接收到非 IDL 的 Symbol 即为帧错误。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>


[⬆️ 返回目录](#-本章目录-table-of-contents)

---

<!-- 📄 Page 372 -->
---

<a id="sec-4-2-2-3-3-cont2"></a>
### 非 Flit 模式接收器帧定界要求(续 2)

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

**Note:** The only Tokens expected to be received in the same Symbol Time following an IDL Token are additional IDL Tokens or an EDS Token.

- While processing the Data Stream, Receivers must also check the Block type received by each Lane, after accounting for Lane-to-Lane de-skew, for the following conditions:
  - Receiving an Ordered Set Block on any Lane immediately following an SDS Ordered Set is a Framing Error.
  - Receiving a Block with an undefined Block type (a Sync Header of 11b or 00b) is a Framing Error. It is strongly recommended that Receivers of a multi-Lane Link report an error for any Lane that received the undefined Block type in the Lane Error Status register.
  - Receiving an Ordered Set Block on any Lane without receiving an EDS Token in the preceding Block is a Framing Error. For example, receiving a SKP Ordered Set without a preceding EDS Token is a Framing Error. In addition, receiving a SKP Ordered Set followed immediately by another Ordered Set Block (including another SKP Ordered Set) within a Data Stream is a Framing Error. It is strongly recommended that if the first Symbol of the Ordered Set is SKP, Receivers of a multi-Lane Link report an error for the Lane(s) in the Lane Error Status register if the received Symbol number 1 through 4N does not match the corresponding Symbol in § Table 4-52 or § Table 4-53.
  - Receiving a Data Block on any Lane when the previous block contained an EDS Token is a Framing Error. It is strongly recommended that Receivers of a multi-Lane Link report an error for the Lane(s) that received the Data Block in the Lane Error Status register.
  - Receivers are permitted to check for different Ordered Sets on different Lanes. If checked, receiving different Ordered Sets is a Framing Error. For example, if Lane 0 receives a SKP Ordered Set and Lane 1 receives an EIOS, it would be a Framing Error.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

**注:** 在 IDL Token 之后同一 Symbol Time 中预期接收到的 Token 仅为额外的 IDL Token 或一个 EDS Token。

- 在处理数据流期间,接收器还必须考虑通道间去偏斜(Lane-to-Lane de-skew)后,检查每个通道所接收的 Block 类型,条件如下:
  - 在 SDS 有序集之后,任何通道立即接收到有序集 Block 即为帧错误。
  - 接收到具有未定义 Block 类型的 Block(Sync Header 为 11b 或 00b)即为帧错误。强烈建议多通道链路的接收器在 Lane Error Status 寄存器中针对接收到未定义 Block 类型的任何通道报告错误。
  - 在任何通道上接收到有序集 Block,而前一个 Block 中未接收到 EDS Token,即为帧错误。例如,接收到 SKP 有序集而前面没有 EDS Token 即为帧错误。此外,在数据流中接收到 SKP 有序集后立即又接收到另一个有序集 Block(包括另一个 SKP 有序集)亦为帧错误。强烈建议当有序集的第一个 Symbol 为 SKP 时,如果接收到的 Symbol 1 到 4N 与 § 表 4-52 或 § 表 4-53 中对应的 Symbol 不匹配,则多通道链路的接收器在 Lane Error Status 寄存器中针对相应通道报告错误。
  - 在前一个 Block 包含 EDS Token 的情况下,任何通道接收到数据 Block 即为帧错误。强烈建议多通道链路的接收器在 Lane Error Status 寄存器中针对接收到数据 Block 的通道报告错误。
  - 允许接收器检查不同通道上是否接收到不同的有序集。如果检查,接收到不同的有序集即为帧错误。例如,如果 Lane 0 接收到 SKP 有序集而 Lane 1 接收到 EIOS,则为帧错误。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

[⬆️ 返回目录](#-本章目录-table-of-contents)

---

<a id="sec-4-2-2-3-4"></a>
## 4.2.2.3.4 Receiver Framing Requirements in Flit Mode | Flit 模式下的接收器帧定界要求


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

While processing the Data Stream, Receivers must check the Block type received by each Lane, after accounting for Lane-to-Lane de-skew, for the following conditions:

- Not receiving a SKP Ordered Set followed by a Data Block on any Lane immediately following an SDS Ordered Set is a Framing Error.
- Receiving a Block with an undefined Block type (a Sync Header of 11b or 00b) is a Framing Error. It is strongly recommended that Receivers of a multi-Lane Link report an error for any Lane that received the undefined Block type in the Lane Error Status register.
- For Lanes that are not entering or exiting the electrical idle state as part of L0p state, the following conditions result in a Framing Error. It is strongly recommended that Receivers of a multi-Lane Link report an error for any Lane that caused the Framing Error in the Lane Error Status register.
  - Receiving an Ordered Set Block on any Lane in an unscheduled Block boundary, as defined in § Section 4.2.3.1
  - Not receiving one of these three Ordered Sets of the appropriate length at the scheduled Block boundary: EIEOS, SKP Ordered Set, or EIOS
  - Receivers are permitted to check for different Ordered Sets on different Lanes, except as permitted for an L0p width transition. If checked, receiving different Ordered Sets is a Framing Error. For example, if Lane 0 receives a SKP Ordered Set and Lane 1 receives an EIOS and no L0p transition is expected, it would be a Framing Error.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

在处理数据流期间,接收器必须考虑通道间去偏斜后,检查每个通道所接收的 Block 类型,条件如下:

- 在 SDS 有序集之后,任何通道未接收到 SKP 有序集后接数据 Block,即为帧错误。
- 接收到具有未定义 Block 类型的 Block(Sync Header 为 11b 或 00b)即为帧错误。强烈建议多通道链路的接收器在 Lane Error Status 寄存器中针对接收到未定义 Block 类型的任何通道报告错误。
- 对于未作为 L0p 状态的一部分进入或退出电气空闲状态的通道,以下条件会导致帧错误。强烈建议多通道链路的接收器在 Lane Error Status 寄存器中针对导致帧错误的任何通道报告错误。
  - 在任何通道上,在未调度的 Block 边界接收到有序集 Block,如 § 4.2.3.1 中所定义。
  - 在调度的 Block 边界未接收到下列三种有序集中适当长度之一:EIEOS、SKP 有序集或 EIOS。
  - 允许接收器检查不同通道上是否接收到不同的有序集,L0p 宽度转换所允许的情况除外。如果检查,接收到不同的有序集即为帧错误。例如,如果 Lane 0 接收到 SKP 有序集而 Lane 1 接收到 EIOS,且未预期 L0p 转换,则为帧错误。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>


[⬆️ 返回目录](#-本章目录-table-of-contents)

---

<!-- 📄 Page 373 -->
---

<a id="sec-4-2-2-3-4-cont"></a>
### Flit 模式接收器帧定界要求与加扰 (续)

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

If a Receiver detects a Framing Error while processing the Data Stream, it must:

- Report a Receiver Error as described in § Section 4.2.5.8 .
- Stop processing the Data Stream. Processing of a new Data Stream is initiated when the next SDS Ordered Set is received as previously described.
- Initiate the error recovery process as described in § Section 4.2.5.8 . If the LTSSM state is L0, direct the LTSSM to Recovery. If the LTSSM state is Configuration.Complete or Configuration.Idle when the Framing Error is detected, the error recovery process is satisfied by either a transition from Configuration.Idle to Recovery.RcvrLock due to the specified timeout, or a transition to Recovery through L0. If the LTSSM state is Recovery.RcvrCfg or Recovery.Idle when the Framing Error is detected, the error recovery process is satisfied by either a transition from Recovery.Idle to Recovery.RcvrLock due to the specified timeout, or a directed transition from L0 to Recovery. If the LTSSM substate is either Recovery.RcvrLock or Configuration.Linkwidth.Start, the error recovery process is satisfied upon exit from these substates and no direction of the LTSSM to Recovery is required.

  - **Note:** The framing error recovery mechanism is not expected to directly cause any Data Link Layer initiated recovery action such as NAK.

Each Lane of the transmitter in a multi-Lane Link may implement a separate LFSR for scrambling. Each Lane of the receiver in a multi-Lane Link may implement a separate LFSR for descrambling. Implementations may choose to implement fewer LFSRs but must achieve the same functionality as independent LFSRs.

The LFSR uses the following polynomial: `G(X) = X23 + X21 + X16 + X8 + X5 + X2 + 1` and is demonstrated in § Figure 4-18.

The scrambling rules are as follows:

- The two bits of the Sync Header used in 8.0 GT/s, 16.0 GT/s, or 32.0 GT/s Data Rate are not scrambled and do not advance the LFSR.
- All 16 Symbols of an Electrical Idle Exit Ordered Set (EIEOS) bypass scrambling. Except during L0p, the scrambling LFSR is initialized after the last Symbol of an EIEOS is transmitted, and the descrambling LFSR is initialized after the last Symbol of an EIEOS is received.
- **TS1 and TS2 Ordered Sets for 8.0 GT/s, 16.0 GT/s, or 32.0 GT/s Data Rate:**
  - Symbol 0 of a TS1 or TS2 Ordered Set bypasses scrambling.
  - Symbols 1-13 are scrambled.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

如果接收器在处理数据流时检测到帧错误,则必须:

- 按 § 4.2.5.8 中所述报告接收器错误 (Receiver Error)。
- 停止处理数据流。当接收到下一个 SDS 有序集时,如前所述,将启动新数据流的处理。
- 按 § 4.2.5.8 中所述启动错误恢复流程。如果 LTSSM 状态为 L0,则将 LTSSM 引导至 Recovery。如果在检测到帧错误时 LTSSM 状态为 Configuration.Complete 或 Configuration.Idle,则错误恢复流程可由 Configuration.Idle 到 Recovery.RcvrLock 的超时触发的转换,或经由 L0 的 Recovery 转换来满足。如果在检测到帧错误时 LTSSM 状态为 Recovery.RcvrCfg 或 Recovery.Idle,则错误恢复流程可由 Recovery.Idle 到 Recovery.RcvrLock 的超时触发的转换,或从 L0 到 Recovery 的引导转换来满足。如果 LTSSM 子状态为 Recovery.RcvrLock 或 Configuration.Linkwidth.Start,则错误恢复流程在这些子状态退出时即得到满足,无需将 LTSSM 引导至 Recovery。

  - **注:** 帧错误恢复机制预期不会直接导致任何数据链路层发起的恢复动作(例如 NAK)。

多通道链路中发送器的每个通道可以实现单独的 LFSR 用于加扰 (Scrambling)。多通道链路中接收器的每个通道可以实现单独的 LFSR 用于解扰 (De-scrambling)。实现可以选择使用更少的 LFSR,但必须实现与独立 LFSR 相同的功能。

LFSR 使用以下多项式:`G(X) = X23 + X21 + X16 + X8 + X5 + X2 + 1`,如 § 图 4-18 所示。

加扰规则如下:

- 8.0 GT/s、16.0 GT/s 或 32.0 GT/s 数据速率下使用的 Sync Header 的 2 个比特不加扰,也不会推进 LFSR。
- 电气空闲退出有序集 (EIEOS) 的全部 16 个 Symbol 旁路加扰。在 L0p 期间除外,加扰 LFSR 在 EIEOS 最后一个 Symbol 发送之后初始化,解扰 LFSR 在 EIEOS 最后一个 Symbol 接收之后初始化。
- **8.0 GT/s、16.0 GT/s 或 32.0 GT/s 数据速率下的 TS1 和 TS2 有序集:**
  - TS1 或 TS2 有序集的 Symbol 0 旁路加扰。
  - Symbol 1-13 进行加扰。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

[⬆️ 返回目录](#-本章目录-table-of-contents)

---


---

<a id="sec-4-2-2-3-5"></a>
## 4.2.2.3.5 Recovery from Framing Errors in Non-Flit Mode and Flit Mode | 非 Flit 模式与 Flit 模式下的帧错误恢复

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

**IMPLEMENTATION NOTE:**
**TIME SPENT IN RECOVERY DUE TO DETECTION OF A FRAMING ERROR**

When using 128b/130b encoding, all Framing Errors require Link recovery. It is expected that implementations will require less than 1 microsecond to recover from a Framing Error as measured from the time that both Ports have entered the Recovery state.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

**实现注意事项：**
**检测到帧错误时恢复所耗费的时间**

当使用 128b/130b 编码时，所有帧错误都需要进行链路恢复。预期实现从双方端口进入 Recovery 状态起，恢复一个帧错误所需时间应小于 1 微秒。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

---

<a id="sec-4-2-2-4"></a>
## 4.2.2.4 Scrambling in Non-Flit Mode and Flit Mode | 非 Flit 模式与 Flit 模式下的加扰


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

◦ Symbols 14 and 15 bypass scrambling if required for DC Balance, but they are scrambled if not required for DC Balance.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

◦ 符号 14 和 15 在需要时为保持直流平衡（DC Balance）而旁路加扰，否则仍执行加扰。

</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>

• All 16 Symbols of a Fast Training Sequence (FTS) Ordered Set used in 8.0 GT/s, 16.0 GT/s, or 32.0 GT/s bypass scrambling.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

• 在 8.0 GT/s、16.0 GT/s 或 32.0 GT/s 下使用的快速训练序列（FTS, Fast Training Sequence）有序集的全部 16 个符号均旁路加扰。

</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>

• All 16 Symbols of a Start of Data Stream (SDS) Ordered Set bypass scrambling.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

• 数据流起始（SDS, Start of Data Stream）有序集的全部 16 个符号均旁路加扰。

</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>

• All 16 Symbols of an Electrical Idle Ordered Set (EIOS) bypass scrambling.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

• 电气空闲有序集（EIOS, Electrical Idle Ordered Set）的全部 16 个符号均旁路加扰。

</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>

• All Symbols of a SKP Ordered Set bypass scrambling.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

• SKP 有序集的全部符号均旁路加扰。

</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>

• Transmitters advance their LFSR for all Symbols of all Ordered Sets except for the SKP Ordered Set. The LFSR is not advanced for any Symbols of a SKP Ordered Set.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

• 发送器对所有有序集的全部符号推进其 LFSR（线性反馈移位寄存器），但 SKP 有序集除外。SKP 有序集的任何符号都不推进 LFSR。

</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>

• Receivers evaluate Ordered Sets to determine whether to advance their LFSR. If the Ordered Set is a SKP Ordered Set (see § Section 4.2.8), or an EIOS with L0p in the midst of a data stream then the LFSR is not advanced for any Symbol of the Ordered Set. Otherwise, the LFSR is advanced for all Symbols of the Ordered Set.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

• 接收器评估有序集以决定是否推进其 LFSR。若有序集是 SKP 有序集（参见 § 4.2.8 节），或数据流中处于 L0p 状态时的 EIOS，则该有序集的任何符号都不推进 LFSR；否则，对该有序集的所有符号推进 LFSR。

</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>

• In L0p, the scrambler on the Transmit and Receive sides associated with all the Lanes must advance whenever the scrambler associated with Lane 0 advances and does not advance whenever the scrambler associated Lane 0 does not advance. As a result, in L0p, the transmit or receipt of an EIEOS does not reset the scrambler associated with the Lane, if it is not Lane 0.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

• 在 L0p 状态下，与各 Lane 相关联的发送和接收侧加扰器，必须在 Lane 0 的加扰器推进时推进，在 Lane 0 的加扰器不推进时不推进。因此，在 L0p 状态下，对非 Lane 0 通道，发送或接收 EIEOS 不会复位该 Lane 的加扰器。

</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>

• All 16 Symbols of a Data Block are scrambled and advance the scrambler.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

• 数据块（Data Block）的全部 16 个符号都加扰并推进加扰器。

</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>

• For Symbols that need to be scrambled, the least significant bit is scrambled first and the most significant bit is scrambled last.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

• 对于需要加扰的符号，最低有效位先加扰，最高有效位最后加扰。

</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>

• The seed value of the LFSR is dependent on the Lane number Default Lane numbers are assigned in an implementation specific manner which is invariant to Link width and Lane reversal negotiation. These default numbers must be unique. For example, each Lane of a x16 Link must be assigned a unique Lane number from 0

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

• LFSR 的种子值依赖于 Lane 编号。默认的 Lane 编号以实现特定的方式分配，且不受链路宽度和 Lane 反转协商的影响。这些默认编号必须唯一。例如，x16 链路的每个 Lane 必须被分配一个 0 开始的唯一 Lane 编号。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>


**Table 4-X. LFSR Seed Values per Lane | 表 4-X. 各 Lane 的 LFSR 种子值**

| Lane | Seed   |
|------|--------|
| 0    | 1DBFBCh |
| 1    | 0607BBh |
| 2    | 1EC760h |
| 3    | 18C0DBh |
| 4    | 010F12h |
| 5    | 19CFC9h |
| 6    | 0277CEh |
| 7    | 1BB807h |

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

**IMPLEMENTATION NOTE:**
**ELASTIC BUFFER AND L0P**

For implementations that descramble prior to putting entries into the elastic buffer, one way to handle the scramblers advancing in the idle lane during L0p is as follows:

◦ The Lane which goes to electrical idle will tie its decision to advance the scrambler, accounting for sync hdr, if any, and SKP Ordered Set to Lane 0. Since the EIOS in the Lane coincides with SKP OS in Lane 0, the scrambler in the idle Lane should be synchronized with the Lane 0 on receipt of EIOS

◦ The TS1/TS2 Ordered Sets exchanged when trying to upsize the Link on L0p will be scrambled using the continually running LFSR.

◦ Depending on the implementation, any differences in scrambler synchronization on the receive side for lanes which have recently become active can be determined on the SKP Ordered Set boundary following block alignment and adjusted accordingly.

◦ The special scrambler rules for L0p above apply only to 128b/130b and 1b/1b encoding since the Ordered Sets are scrambled, and different Lanes can have different seeds/taps which must run continuously without being reset during the data stream. In contrast, 8b/10b encoding does not scramble Ordered Sets and all the scramblers across all Lanes will be reset during SKP Ordered Sets during the link width up-size.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

**实现注意事项：**
**弹性缓冲与 L0P**

对于在将数据送入弹性缓冲器之前先解扰的实现，处理 L0p 期间空闲 Lane 加扰器推进的一种方法如下：

◦ 进入电气空闲的 Lane 将其加扰器推进决策与 Lane 0 绑定，考虑同步头（如有）和 SKP 有序集。由于该 Lane 的 EIOS 与 Lane 0 的 SKP OS 同时出现，因此空闲 Lane 的加扰器应在收到 EIOS 时与 Lane 0 保持同步。

◦ 在 L0p 中尝试扩大链路时交换的 TS1/TS2 有序集将使用持续运行的 LFSR 进行加扰。

◦ 根据实现方式的不同，接收侧最近才激活的 Lane，其加扰器同步的任何差异可在块对齐后的 SKP 有序集边界上确定并做相应调整。

◦ 上述 L0p 的特殊加扰规则仅适用于 128b/130b 和 1b/1b 编码，因为这些编码对有序集加扰，不同 Lane 可具有不同的种子/抽头，且在数据流期间必须连续运行而不能被复位。相比之下，8b/10b 编码不对有序集加扰，且所有 Lane 的加扰器在链路宽度增加过程中的 SKP 有序集期间都会被复位。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

◦ The seed values for Lane number modulo 8 are:

[Lane / Seed table — see Table 4-X above]

• The seed value of the LFSR does not change while LinkUp=1. Link reconfiguration through the LTSSM Configuration state does not modify the initial Lane number assignment as long as LinkUp remains 1 (even though the Lane assignment may change during Configuration).

• Scrambling cannot be disabled in Configuration.Complete when using 128b/130b encoding.

• A Loopback Follower must not descramble or scramble the looped-back bit stream.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

◦ Lane 编号模 8 所对应的种子值如下：

[Lane / Seed 表格 — 参见上表 4-X]

• 在 LinkUp=1 期间，LFSR 的种子值不变。只要 LinkUp 保持 1，通过 LTSSM Configuration 状态进行的链路重配置不会修改初始 Lane 编号分配（即使 Lane 分配在 Configuration 期间可能发生变化）。

• 使用 128b/130b 编码时，不能在 Configuration.Complete 中关闭加扰。

• Loopback Follower 不得对回环比特流进行解扰或加扰。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>


<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

**IMPLEMENTATION NOTE:**
**SCRAMBLING PSEUDO-CODE**

The pseudo-code for the scrambler along with examples are provided in § Section C.2 of § Appendix C.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

**实现注意事项：**
**加扰伪代码**

加扰器的伪代码及示例见 § 附录 C 的 § C.2 节。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

<!-- 📄 Page 376 -->
---

> **Figure 4-18.** LFSR with Scrambling Polynomial in 8.0 GT/s and Above Data Rate
> <img src="figures/chapter_04/fig_0376_1_tight.png" width="700">

**Figure 4-18 | 图 4-18**: 8.0 GT/s 及以上数据速率下带加扰多项式的 LFSR (LFSR with Scrambling Polynomial in 8.0 GT/s and Above Data Rate)

---


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

**IMPLEMENTATION NOTE:**
**LFSR IMPLEMENTATION WITH A SHARED LFSR**

Implementations may choose to implement one LFSR and take different tap points as shown in § Figure 4-19, which is equivalent to the individual LFSR per-lane with different seeds, as shown in § Figure 4-18. It should also be noted that the tap equations of four Lanes are the XOR of the tap equations of two neighboring Lanes. For example, Lane 0 can be obtained by XORing the output of Lanes 1 and 7; Lane 2 is the XOR of Lanes 1 and 3; Lane 4 is the XOR of Lanes 3 and 5; and Lane 6 is the XOR of Lanes 5 and 7. This can be used to help reduce the gate count at the expense of potential delay due to the XOR results of the two Lanes.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

**实现注意事项：**
**采用共享 LFSR 的实现**

实现可选择只实现一个 LFSR，并取不同的抽头点（如图 4-19 所示），其效果等同于图 4-18 中按 Lane 使用不同种子的独立 LFSR。还需要注意，四个 Lane 的抽头方程等于其相邻两个 Lane 抽头方程的异或（XOR）。例如，Lane 0 可由 Lane 1 和 Lane 7 的输出异或得到；Lane 2 是 Lane 1 和 Lane 3 的异或；Lane 4 是 Lane 3 和 Lane 5 的异或；Lane 6 是 Lane 5 和 Lane 7 的异或。这可用来减少门数，代价是由于两个 Lane 的异或结果可能带来额外延迟。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>


---

<!-- 📄 Page 378 -->
---

> **Figure 4-19.** Alternate Implementation of the LFSR for Descrambling
> <img src="figures/chapter_04/fig_0378_1_tight.png" width="700">

**Figure 4-19 | 图 4-19**: 用于解扰的 LFSR 替代实现 (Alternate Implementation of the LFSR for Descrambling)

抽头方程（Tap Equations）:

- For i = 0, 8, 16, 24: Tap_Eqn_Lane_i = D9 ^ D13
- For i = 1, 9, 17, 25: Tap_Eqn_Lane_i = D1 ^ D13
- For i = 2, 10, 18, 26: Tap_Eqn_Lane_i = D13 ^ D22
- For i = 3, 11, 19, 27: Tap_Eqn_Lane_i = D1 ^ D22
- For i = 4, 12, 20, 28: Tap_Eqn_Lane_i = D3 ^ D22
- For i = 5, 13, 21, 29: Tap_Eqn_Lane_i = D1 ^ D3
- For i = 6, 14, 22, 30: Tap_Eqn_Lane_i = D3 ^ D9
- For i = 7, 15, 23, 31: Tap_Eqn_Lane_i = D1 ^ D9

Reset Value = 1.

---

<a id="sec-4-2-2-5"></a>
## 4.2.2.5 Precoding | 预编码 (Precoding)

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

A Receiver may request precoding from its transmitter for operating at data rates of 32.0 GT/s and higher. Precoding, when enabled at a Data Rate, applies to both Flit Mode and Non-Flit Mode at that data rate. The precoding rules are as follows:

• A Port or Pseudo-Port must request precoding on all configured Lanes of the Link. Behavior is undefined if precoding is requested on some Lanes but not others by a Port or Pseudo-Port.

• A Port or Pseudo-Port may request precoding independent of other Ports or Pseudo-Ports. For example, it is possible that precoding may be turned on only in the Upstream Port in the case with no Retimers in § Figure 4-61, or on all the Lanes in Tx(A) and Tx(E) in the two Retimer example in § Figure 4-61.

• Precoding is turned off for all data rates when the LTSSM is in the Detect state.

• If a precoding request for a data rate is to be made, it must be made prior to entering that data rate. A precoding request is made by setting the Transmitter Precode Request bit in the EQ TS2 or the 128b/130b EQ TS2 Ordered Sets prior to the transition to Recovery.Speed for the data rate at which the precoding will be turned on. Prior to the first Link speed negotiation to 32.0 GT/s since exiting Detect, the Transmitter Precode Request bit represents the precoding request for 32.0 GT/s when the Supported Link Speeds field in the EQ TS2 or the 128b/130b EQ TS2 Ordered sets is 32.0 GT/s or above. If the Link speed has been negotiated to 32.0 GT/s since exiting Detect, the Transmitter Precode Request bit represents the precoding request for the same data rate that the values in the Transmitter Preset field apply to. For each data rate of 32.0 GT/s or higher, the precoding request must be made independently.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

接收器可向其发送器请求预编码，以在 32.0 GT/s 及更高的数据速率下运行。预编码在某个数据速率下使能后，将同时适用于该数据速率下的 Flit 模式和非 Flit 模式。预编码规则如下：

• 端口或伪端口必须在链路的所有已配置 Lane 上请求预编码。如果一个端口或伪端口仅在部分 Lane 上请求预编码，则行为未定义。

• 端口或伪端口可独立于其他端口或伪端口请求预编码。例如，如图 4-61 中无 Retimer 的情况，可能仅在上游端口使能预编码；或者在图 4-61 中两个 Retimer 的示例中，Tx(A) 和 Tx(E) 的所有 Lane 都使能预编码。

• 当 LTSSM 处于 Detect 状态时，所有数据速率下都关闭预编码。

• 若要针对某一数据速率提出预编码请求，必须在进入该数据速率之前提出。预编码请求通过在进入该数据速率对应的 Recovery.Speed 之前，在 EQ TS2 或 128b/130b EQ TS2 有序集中设置 Transmitter Precode Request 位来实现。在退出 Detect 后第一次协商到 32.0 GT/s 之前，当 EQ TS2 或 128b/130b EQ TS2 有序集中的 Supported Link Speeds 字段为 32.0 GT/s 或更高时，Transmitter Precode Request 位表示对 32.0 GT/s 的预编码请求。如果自退出 Detect 后链路速度已经协商到 32.0 GT/s，则 Transmitter Precode Request 位表示与 Transmitter Preset 字段所适用的同一数据速率的预编码请求。针对 32.0 GT/s 及以上的每个数据速率，预编码请求必须独立提出。

</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>

• Each (pseudo) Port must store the precoding request along with the Tx Eq values from the most recent equalization procedure in each Lane. If the Link operates at 32.0 GT/s or higher data rate without performing equalization through the No Equalization Needed mechanism it negotiated in the TS1/TS2 Ordered Sets or modified TS1/TS2 Ordered Sets or the Link operates at the 32.0 GT/s or higher data rate in Polling.Compliance or Loopback, the precoding requests from the last equalization results that are being used must be enforced. If no equalization has ever been performed on the Link (prior to the current Link up), then precoding will not be turned on.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

• 每个（伪）端口必须将预编码请求连同各 Lane 最近一次均衡过程中得到的 Tx Eq 值一起存储。如果链路在 32.0 GT/s 或更高数据速率下运行时，未通过 TS1/TS2 有序集或修改版 TS1/TS2 有序集协商出的 "No Equalization Needed" 机制进行均衡；或链路在 Polling.Compliance 或 Loopback 状态下以 32.0 GT/s 或更高数据速率运行，则必须强制使用最近一次均衡结果中的预编码请求。若在当前 Link up 之前链路从未进行过均衡，则不会使能预编码。

</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>

• If the Transmitter Precode Request bit is Set to 1b in each of the received eight consecutive EQ TS2 or 128b/130b EQ TS2 Ordered Sets during Recovery.RcvrCfg prior to entry to Recovery.Speed, the Transmitter must turn on the precoding for the data rate at which the Link will operate on exit from Recovery.Speed if the data rate is 32.0 GT/s or higher and the precoding request was intended for the data rate of operation. If the precoding request applies to a speed other than the data rate at which the Link will operate on exit from Recovery.Speed, the received Transmitter Precode Request bit must be ignored and the Precoding setting must not be updated for any data rate. Once turned on, precoding will be in effect for that data rate until the Transmitter receives another set of eight consecutive EQ TS2 or 128b/130b EQ TS2 Ordered Sets with Transmitter Precode Request set to 0b during Recovery.RcvrCfg prior to entry to Recovery.Speed for the same data rate.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

• 若在进入 Recovery.Speed 之前的 Recovery.RcvrCfg 期间，连续收到的 8 个 EQ TS2 或 128b/130b EQ TS2 有序集中 Transmitter Precode Request 位均为 1b，则发送器必须在退出 Recovery.Speed 后的数据速率为 32.0 GT/s 或更高且该预编码请求正是针对该工作数据速率时，为该数据速率开启预编码。若预编码请求针对的是与退出 Recovery.Speed 后将运行的数据速率不同的速率，则必须忽略所收到的 Transmitter Precode Request 位，并且不得为任何数据速率更新预编码设置。预编码一旦开启，将在该数据速率下一直生效，直到发送器在进入同一数据速率的 Recovery.Speed 之前，于 Recovery.RcvrCfg 期间再次收到连续 8 个 Transmitter Precode Request 为 0b 的 EQ TS2 或 128b/130b EQ TS2 有序集为止。

</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>

• A Transmitter must not turn on precoding for any data rates lower than 32.0 GT/s.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

• 发送器不得为任何低于 32.0 GT/s 的数据速率开启预编码。

</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>

• In data rates of 32.0 GT/s or higher, a Transmitter must set the Transmitter Precoding On bit to 1b in the TS1 Ordered Sets that it transmits in Recovery if its precoding is on for the current data rate; else the bit must be set to 0b.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

• 在 32.0 GT/s 及更高的数据速率下，若发送器在当前数据速率已开启预编码，则其在 Recovery 中发送的 TS1 有序集中必须将 Transmitter Precoding On 位置 1b；否则该位必须置 0b。

</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>

• A Transmitter that has turned on precoding for the 32.0 GT/s data rate on Lane 0 must set the 32.0 GT/s Transmitter Precoding On bit to 1b in the 32.0 GT/s Status Register; else, it must set the bit to 0b. A Receiver that has requested, or will request, its link partner to turn on precoding at the 32.0 GT/s data rate must set the 32.0 GT/s Transmitter Precode Request to 1b in the 32.0 GT/s Status Register; else it must set the bit to 0b.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

• 在 Lane 0 上已为 32.0 GT/s 数据速率开启预编码的发送器，必须将 32.0 GT/s Status Register 中的 "32.0 GT/s Transmitter Precoding On" 位置 1b；否则必须置 0b。已请求或将要请求其链路伙伴在 32.0 GT/s 数据速率开启预编码的接收器，必须将 32.0 GT/s Status Register 中的 "32.0 GT/s Transmitter Precode Request" 位置 1b；否则必须置 0b。

</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>

• A Transmitter that has turned on precoding for the 64.0 GT/s data rate on Lane 0 must set the 64.0 GT/s Transmitter Precoding On bit to 1b in the 64.0 GT/s Status Register; else it must set the bit to 0b. A Receiver that has requested, or will request, its link partner to turn on precoding at the 64.0 GT/s data rate must set the 64.0 GT/s Transmitter Precode Request to 1b in the 64.0 GT/s Status Register; else it must set the bit to 0b.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

• 在 Lane 0 上已为 64.0 GT/s 数据速率开启预编码的发送器，必须将 64.0 GT/s Status Register 中的 "64.0 GT/s Transmitter Precoding On" 位置 1b；否则必须置 0b。已请求或将要请求其链路伙伴在 64.0 GT/s 数据速率开启预编码的接收器，必须将 64.0 GT/s Status Register 中的 "64.0 GT/s Transmitter Precode Request" 位置 1b；否则必须置 0b。

</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>

• See § Section 4.2.2.5.1 for 32.0 GT/s precoding requirements. See § Section 4.2.3.1.4 for 64.0 GT/s and above data rate precoding requirements.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

• 32.0 GT/s 预编码要求见 § 4.2.2.5.1 节。64.0 GT/s 及以上数据速率的预编码要求见 § 4.2.3.1.4 节。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

---

<!-- 📄 Page 380 -->
---

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

When precoding is on at 32.0 GT/s, the following rules apply (see § Figure 4-20):

• Only scrambled bits are precoded.

• The "previous bit" used for precoding is set to 1b on every block boundary and gets updated by the last scrambled and precoded bit transmitted within the current block boundary.

• For symbols that are scrambled, Receivers must first decode the precoded bits before sending them to the descrambler.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

在 32.0 GT/s 开启预编码时，遵循以下规则（参见 § 图 4-20）：

• 仅对加扰后的比特进行预编码。

• 用于预编码的"前一个比特"在每个块边界上被设为 1b，并随当前块边界内最后发送的已加扰且已预编码的比特而更新。

• 对于已加扰的符号，接收器必须先对预编码比特进行解码，然后再送入解扰器。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

> **Figure 4-20.** Precoding working the scrambler / de-scrambler
> <img src="figures/chapter_04/fig_0380_1_tight.png" width="700">

**Figure 4-20 | 图 4-20**: 预编码与加扰器/解扰器协同工作 (Precoding working the scrambler/de-scrambler)

- (a. Precoding on Tx side) — (a. 发送侧预编码)
- (b. Precoding on Rx side) — (b. 接收侧预编码)

---

<a id="sec-4-2-2-5-1"></a>
## 4.2.2.5.1 Precoding at 32.0 GT/s Data Rate | 32.0 GT/s 数据速率下的预编码


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

**IMPLEMENTATION NOTE:**
**PARITY IN THE SKP ORDERED SET WHEN PRECODING IS TURNED ON**

As per the rules of § Section 4.2.5.1 and § Section 4.2.8.2, when precoding is turned on, the parity in the SKP Ordered Sets should be calculated before precoding is applied on the Transmit side. Thus, the order in the Transmitter is:

1. scrambling,
2. followed by parity bit calculation,
3. followed by precoding for the scrambled bits.

Accordingly, in the Receiver, the order is:

1. precoding (if turned on by the Transmitter),
2. followed by parity bit calculation,
3. followed by descrambling.

The rationale for this order is that in a Link with one or two Retimers, different Link segments may have the precoding on or off. Let us consider an example system with one retimer between the Root Port and End Point to illustrate this. In the upstream direction, the End Point has precoding on in its Transmitter Lanes since the Retimer Receiver needs it but the Retimer to Root Port Link segment has the precoding off since the Root Port does not need precoding at its Receiver. Since the Retimer does not change the parity bit, the Root Port would get a parity error if the parity calculation was done by the Transmitter (of the End Point) after precoding.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

**实现注意事项：**
**预编码开启时 SKP 有序集中的奇偶校验位**

按照 § 4.2.5.1 节和 § 4.2.8.2 节的规则，当预编码开启时，SKP 有序集中的奇偶校验位应在发送侧应用预编码之前计算。因此，发送器中的顺序为：

1. 加扰；
2. 之后进行奇偶校验位计算；
3. 再对已加扰的比特进行预编码。

相应地，接收器中的顺序为：

1. 预编码（若发送器已开启）；
2. 之后进行奇偶校验位计算；
3. 再解扰。

采用此顺序的原因在于：当链路中含一个或两个 Retimer 时，不同的链路段可能独立开启或关闭预编码。考虑一个示例系统——根端口和端点之间有一个 Retimer 以说明此情况。在上游方向，端点在其发送器 Lane 上开启预编码，因为 Retimer 接收器需要它；而 Retimer 到根端口之间的链路段则关闭预编码，因为根端口的接收器不需要预编码。由于 Retimer 不会改变奇偶校验位，如果发送器（端点的）在预编码之后再计算奇偶校验位，那么根端口将得到奇偶校验错误。

</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>

**IMPLEMENTATION NOTE:**
**LOOPBACK LEAD'S BEHAVIOR IF PRECODING IS ON IN ANY LINK SEGMENT**

As per the rules of precoding mentioned in this section and § Section 4.2.7.4, a Loopback Lead operating at a data rate of 32.0 GT/s or higher should account for precoding to be on some link segments and off in other link segments. This is particularly relevant when the Loopback Follower transitions from sending TS1 Ordered Sets to looping back the bits. This is where the precoding on the receiver of the Loopback Lead may switch (between precoding on and off). The Loopback Lead is permitted to use implementation specific mechanisms to handle this scenario.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

**实现注意事项：**
**任一链路段开启预编码时 Loopback Lead 的行为**

按照本节及 § 4.2.7.4 节所述的预编码规则，工作于 32.0 GT/s 或更高数据速率的 Loopback Lead 应考虑不同链路段可能独立开启或关闭预编码的情况。当 Loopback Follower 从发送 TS1 有序集切换为对收到的比特进行回环时尤其需要关注：此时 Loopback Lead 接收器上的预编码状态可能会在开启和关闭之间切换。允许 Loopback Lead 使用实现特定机制处理该场景。

</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>

**IMPLEMENTATION NOTE:**
**TS1/TS2 ORDERED SETS WHEN PRECODING IS TURNED ON**

As per the rules in this section, when precoding is turned on, the 'previous bit' used for precoding is 1b for the first bit of Symbol 1 since Symbol 0 is not scrambled and the 'previous bit' gets set to 1b at the block boundary.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

**实现注意事项：**
**预编码开启时的 TS1/TS2 有序集**

根据本节规则，当预编码开启时，由于符号 0 不加扰，因此用于预编码的"前一个比特"在符号 1 的第 1 个比特处为 1b，且"前一个比特"在块边界被设为 1b。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>


---

<a id="sec-4-2-2-6"></a>
## 4.2.2.6 Loopback with 128b/130b Code in Non-Flit Mode and Flit Mode | 非 Flit 模式与 Flit 模式下使用 128b/130b 编码的回环

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

When using 128b/130b encoding, Loopback Leads must transmit Blocks with the defined 01b and 10b Sync Headers. However, they are not required to transmit an SDS Ordered Set when transitioning from Ordered Set Blocks to Data Blocks, nor are they required to transmit an EDS Token when transitioning from Data Blocks to Ordered Set Blocks. Leads must transmit SKP Ordered Sets periodically as defined in § Section 4.2.8, and they must be capable of processing received (looped-back) SKP Ordered Sets of varying length. Leads are permitted to transmit Electrical Idle Exit Ordered Sets (EIEOS) as defined in § Section 4.2.2.2.1. Leads are permitted to transmit any payload in Data Blocks and Ordered Set Blocks that they expect to be looped-back. If the Loopback Lead transmits an Ordered Set Block whose first symbol matches the first symbol of SKP OS, EIEOS, or EIOS, that Ordered Set Block must be a complete and valid SKP OS, EIEOS, or EIOS.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

使用 128b/130b 编码时，Loopback Lead 必须在所发送的块中使用规定的 01b 和 10b 同步头。但从有序集块过渡到数据块时，无需发送 SDS 有序集；从数据块过渡到有序集块时，也无需发送 EDS 标记。Lead 必须按 § 4.2.8 节的规定周期性发送 SKP 有序集，并且必须能处理长度可变的接收（已回环）SKP 有序集。允许 Lead 发送 § 4.2.2.2.1 节中规定的电气空闲退出有序集（EIEOS）。允许 Lead 在数据块和有序集块中发送其预期被回环的任何有效负载。如果 Loopback Lead 发送的有序集块的第 1 个符号与 SKP OS、EIEOS 或 EIOS 的第 1 个符号匹配，则该有序集块必须是完整且有效的 SKP OS、EIEOS 或 EIOS。

</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>

When using 128b/130b encoding, Loopback Followers must retransmit all bits received without modification, except for SKP Ordered Sets which can be adjusted as needed for clock compensation. If clock compensation is required, Followers must add or remove 4 SKP Symbols per Ordered Set. The modified SKP Ordered Set must meet the definition of § Section 4.2.8.2 (i.e., it must have between 4 to 20 SKP Symbols followed by the SKP_END Symbol and the three Symbols that follow it as transmitted by the Loopback Leads. If a Follower is unable to obtain Block alignment or it is misaligned, it may be unable to perform clock compensation and therefore unable to loop-back all bits received. In this case, it is permitted to add or remove Symbols as necessary to continue operation. Followers must not check for a received SDS Ordered Set when a transition from Ordered Set Blocks to Data Blocks is detected, and they must not check for a received EDS Token when a transition from Data Blocks to Ordered Set Blocks is detected.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

使用 128b/130b 编码时，Loopback Follower 必须将收到的所有比特原样回传（不做修改），但 SKP 有序集可按需调整以补偿时钟。若需要进行时钟补偿，Follower 必须在每个有序集中增加或删除 4 个 SKP 符号。修改后的 SKP 有序集必须符合 § 4.2.8.2 节的定义（即必须包含 4 至 20 个 SKP 符号，后接 SKP_END 符号以及由 Loopback Lead 发送的紧随其后的 3 个符号）。如果 Follower 无法获得块对齐或发生失对齐，则可能无法执行时钟补偿，从而无法回环所收到的全部比特。此时允许其按需增加或删除符号以维持运行。当检测到从有序集块到数据块的过渡时，Follower 不应检查收到的 SDS 有序集；检测到从数据块到有序集块的过渡时，也不应检查收到的 EDS 标记。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

---

<a id="sec-4-2-3"></a>
## 4.2.3 Flit Mode Operation | Flit 模式操作


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

Flit definition and Symbol placement is specified in this section.

Flits encoded with 8b/10b encoding follow the rules in § Section 4.2.1 with the following exception:

• Since the Flit Mode has its fixed TLP and DLP placement, the packet markers such as STP, SDP, END, EDB are not used.

Flits Encoded with 128b/130b encoding follow the rules in § Section 4.2.2 with the following exceptions:

• Since the Flit Mode has its fixed TLP and DLP placement, the Framing Tokens are not used.

1b/1b encoding is specified in Section 4.2.3.1.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

Flit 定义与符号放置由本节规定。

采用 8b/10b 编码的 Flit 遵循 § 4.2.1 节的规则，但有以下例外：

• 由于 Flit 模式具有固定的 TLP 和 DLP 放置，因此不使用 STP、SDP、END、EDB 等报文起始/结束标记。

采用 128b/130b 编码的 Flit 遵循 § 4.2.2 节的规则，但有以下例外：

• 由于 Flit 模式具有固定的 TLP 和 DLP 放置，因此不使用 Framing Tokens（帧标记）。

1b/1b 编码的规定见 § 4.2.3.1 节。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>


---

<a id="sec-4-2-3-1"></a>
## 4.2.3.1 1b/1b Encoding for 64.0 GT/s and higher Data Rates | 64.0 GT/s 及更高数据速率下的 1b/1b 编码

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

When the PCI Express Link is operating at 64.0 GT/s or higher Data Rates, it uses Flit Mode. A Symbol (8 bits) is the basic unit of transfer per Lane. PAM4 signaling is used for all Symbols, whether it belongs to a Data Stream or an Ordered Set. PAM4 operates on 2-bit aligned boundaries. A Symbol, represented as 'S7S6S5S4S3S2S1S0', is placed on a Lane starting with the voltage encoding of 'S1S0' and ending with 'S7S6'. An example placement of Flits (described later) or Ordered Set on a x4 Link is shown in § Figure 4-21 below.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

当 PCI Express 链路以 64.0 GT/s 或更高的数据速率运行时，使用 Flit 模式。符号（Symbol，8 比特）是每条 Lane 的基本传输单位。所有符号（无论属于数据流还是有序集）均使用 PAM4 信号。PAM4 在 2 比特对齐的边界上工作。一个符号记为 'S7S6S5S4S3S2S1S0'，在 Lane 上以 'S1S0' 的电压编码开始，以 'S7S6' 结束。x4 链路上 Flit（稍后描述）或有序集的放置示例如下方 § 图 4-21 所示。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

---

<!-- 📄 Page 383 -->
---

> **Figure 4-21.** Example of Symbol placement in a x4 Link with 1b/1b encoding
> <img src="figures/chapter_04/fig_0383_1_tight.png" width="700">

**Figure 4-21 | 图 4-21**: 使用 1b/1b 编码时 x4 链路上的符号放置示例 (Example of Symbol placement in a x4 Link with 1b/1b encoding)

- Lane 0 / Lane 1 / Lane 2 / Lane 3
- Symbol 0 of Flit or Symbol 0 of OS
- Symbol 1 of Flit or Symbol 0 of OS
- Symbol 2 of Flit or Symbol 0 of OS
- Symbol 3 of Flit or Symbol 0 of OS
- Flit X (256 B)
- Flit X+1 (256 B) Or Ordered Set
- Time (UI): 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7 ... 252, 253, 254, 255, 256, 257, 258 ...


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

A conceptual diagram of the Transmit side and Receive side is shown in § Figure 4-22 and § Figure 4-23 respectively. These diagrams are provided to explain the order of operations that must be performed on the Transmit and Receive side. An implementation may choose to operate on different widths depending on the design goals and constraints.

At a Flit level, on the Transmit side, CRC is first applied followed by FEC generation. After that, each Lane is allotted its Symbol in the byte interleaved fashion for the Flit. On a per Lane basis, Scrambling is performed, if required, followed by Gray Coding at a 2-bit aligned boundary, after which Precoding is performed at 2-bit aligned UI level, if enabled and required. Thus, in the Symbol above, Gray Coding and Precoding will be applied for the bits corresponding to 'S1S0', 'S3S2', …, 'S7S6'. As shown in the diagram below, the scrambled symbols of the TS1/TS2 Ordered sets also undergo the precoding logic (including bypass). All Symbols, whether they belong to a Flit (Data Stream) or scrambled Symbols of an Ordered Set or any pattern where explicitly mentioned (e.g., scrambled part of modified compliance pattern) undergo the Gray Coding and PAM4 encoding, whereas unscrambled Symbols of an Ordered Set or any pattern only undergo the PAM4 encoding (the gray coding and DC-balance is effectively taken care of through the bit definition). For the cases where parts of a pattern may be expressed as a repeated sequence of UIs, the Symbol here refers to the aligned 4 UI interval of that pattern.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

发送侧和接收侧的概念图分别见 § 图 4-22 和 § 图 4-23。这些图用于说明在发送侧和接收侧必须执行的操作顺序。实现可根据设计目标和约束选择不同的位宽运行。

在 Flit 级别，发送侧首先进行 CRC 计算，然后进行 FEC 生成。之后，每条 Lane 按字节交织方式获得 Flit 中对应的符号。在每条 Lane 上，如果需要，先进行加扰，然后在 2 比特对齐边界上进行格雷编码（Gray Coding），之后在 2 比特对齐的 UI 级别上按需进行预编码（若已使能）。因此，在上述符号中，'S1S0'、'S3S2'、…、'S7S6' 对应的比特都将经过格雷编码和预编码。如下图所示，TS1/TS2 有序集中已加扰的符号同样会经过预编码逻辑（包括旁路）。无论符号属于 Flit（数据流）、有序集的已加扰符号，还是任何明确说明需要加扰的码型（如修改版 Compliance Pattern 的加扰部分），都要经过格雷编码和 PAM4 编码；而有序集或任何码型的未加扰符号则只经过 PAM4 编码（格雷编码和直流平衡实际上已通过比特定义得到保证）。对于码型的某些部分可能表示为 UI 的重复序列的情况，此处的符号指的是该码型对齐的 4 UI 间隔。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>

**Figure 4-22 | 图 4-22**: 64.0 GT/s 下的发送侧 (Transmit side at 64.0 GT/s)

- Lane 0 ... Lane N-1
- Scrambling (1 bit) — 加扰（1 比特）
- Tx Precoding (2-bit aligned) — 发送预编码（2 比特对齐）
- TS0 OS or Precoding disabled — TS0 有序集或预编码关闭
- Gray Coding (2-bit aligned) — 格雷编码（2 比特对齐）
- Symbols of Ordered Sets or Patterns that are to be scrambled — 需要加扰的有序集或码型符号
- Symbols of Ordered Sets or Patterns that are not to be scrambled — 不需要加扰的有序集或码型符号
- PAM-4 voltage Conversion to 2-bit aligned — PAM-4 电压转换为 2 比特对齐
- Tx Pins — 发送引脚
- DLP (6 Bytes) — 数据链路负载（6 字节）
- CRC Generation (8B) — CRC 生成（8 字节）
- TLP (236 Bytes) — 事务层包（236 字节）
- FEC: ECC Bytes Generation (6B) — FEC：ECC 字节生成（6 字节）
- 242 Bytes / 250 Bytes / 256 Bytes
- Flit Level (Link Wide) – For Flit Mode in all Data Rates — Flit 级别（链路级）—— 适用于所有数据速率的 Flit 模式
- Symbol (8b) Level per Lane – For 64.0 GT/s and higher Data Rates — 每条 Lane 的符号（8 比特）级 —— 适用于 64.0 GT/s 及更高数据速率

The Receive side is similar to the Transmit side in the opposite direction. After the PAM4 voltage is converted to a 2-bit aligned quantity, it undergoes the Receive side precoding if applicable followed by the decoding of the Gray code, followed by descrambling on a single bit level, if applicable. The Data Stream (Flit) is aggregated across all Lanes and undergoes the FEC decode and correction followed by the CRC check before sending up the Transactions and Data Link Payload.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

接收侧与发送侧类似，只是方向相反。PAM4 电压被转换为 2 比特对齐的量后，若适用，先进行接收侧预编码，再进行格雷解码，然后按单比特级别解扰（若适用）。数据流（Flit）跨所有 Lane 聚合后，进行 FEC 解码与纠错，再进行 CRC 校验，最后将事务和数据链路负载向上层递交。

</td>
</tr>

- Lane 0 ... Lane N-1

- Rx Precoding (2-bit aligned) — 接收预编码（2 比特对齐）

PAM4 即四电平脉冲幅度调制（Pulse Amplitude Modulation 4-levels）。它是一种信号机制，在同一单位间隔（UI, Unit Interval）内编码 4 个电平（2 比特），形成 3 个眼图，如 § 图 4-24 所示。PAM4 仅在 64.0 GT/s 或更高的数据速率下部署。如 § 第 8 章 所述，PAM4 有助于应对信道损耗，因为在 64.0 GT/s 数据速率下其奈奎斯特频率与 32.0 GT/s 相同。四个电压电平 0、1、2、3 分别标称映射为 -400 mV、-133 mV、+133 mV 和 +400 mV，相应的格雷编码值按小端比特顺序分别为 00b、01b、11b 和 10b，如 § 图 4-24 所示。在设计满足直流平衡（DC

<a id="sec-4-2-3-1-1"></a>
## 4.2.3.1.1 PAM4 Signaling | PAM4（四电平脉冲幅度调制）信令

PAM4 stands for Pulse Amplitude Modulation 4-levels. It is a signaling mechanism where 4 levels (2 bits) are encoded in same Unit Interval (UI) resulting in 3 eyes, as shown in § Figure 4-24. It is deployed only for 64.0 GT/s or higher Data Rates. As described in § Chapter 8., PAM4 helps with the channel loss as it has the same Nyquist frequency as 32.0 GT/s for 64.0 GT/s Data Rate. The four voltage levels 0, 1, 2, and 3, nominally map to -400 mV, -133 mV, +133 mV and +400 mV respectively, and the Gray code encoded values of 00b, 01b, 11b, and 10b respectively, with the little-endian bit order, as shown in § Figure 4-24. The corresponding DC Balance values to be used when designing Ordered Sets to meet the DC balance needs is also shown in § Figure 4-24. The Reduced voltage levels (eye height or EH) and eye width (EW) increases susceptibility to errors. Gray coding is used to help minimize errors within a UI for the voltage levels.

> PAM4 即四电平脉冲幅度调制（Pulse Amplitude Modulation 4-levels）。它是一种信令机制，在同一单位间隔（UI）内编码 4 个电平（2 比特），形成 3 个眼图，如 § 图 4-24 所示。它仅用于 64.0 GT/s 及更高的数据速率。如 § 第 8 章 所述，PAM4 有助于应对信道损耗，因为对于 64.0 GT/s 数据速率，PAM4 与 32.0 GT/s 具有相同的奈奎斯特频率。四个电压电平 0、1、2、3 分别标称映射为 -400 mV、-133 mV、+133 mV 和 +400 mV，相应的格雷编码值分别为 00b、01b、11b、10b（采用小端比特顺序），如 § 图 4-24 所示。在设计有序集以满足直流平衡（DC Balance）需求时所使用的对应直流平衡值也在 § 图 4-24 中示出。降低的电压幅度（眼高 EH，即 Eye Height）和眼宽（EW，即 Eye Width）会增加误码敏感性。格雷编码（Gray Coding）用于在 UI 范围内尽量减少电压电平所产生的误码。

> **Figure 4-24.** PAM4 Signaling at UI level: Voltage levels, 2-bit encoding, and their corresponding DC balance values
> <img src="figures/chapter_04/fig_0385_1_tight.png" width="700">

**Figure 4-24 | 图 4-24**: UI 级别的 PAM4 信令：电压电平、2 比特编码及其对应的直流平衡值 (PAM4 Signaling at UI level: Voltage levels, 2-bit encoding, and their corresponding DC balance values)

<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

With PAM4 encoding, the bit error rate (BER) is expected to be significantly worse than the 10-12 BER target of the lower data rates (2.5, 5.0, 8.0, 16.0, and 32.0 GT/s). In addition, errors are expected to occur in bursts in a Lane and some amount of Lane to Lane correlation is also expected. The electrical spec parameters along with FBER (First Bit Error Rate) < 10-6 described in § Chapter 8. must be met to ensure probability of a Flit error after FEC to be less than 3×10-5.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

使用 PAM4 编码时，预期误码率（BER）将明显高于较低数据速率（2.5、5.0、8.0、16.0 和 32.0 GT/s）所对应的 10⁻¹² BER 目标。此外，预期错误会成串（burst）地出现在同一通道（Lane）上，并且不同通道之间也会存在一定程度的相关性。必须满足 § 第 8 章 中所描述的电气规范参数以及 FBER（First Bit Error Rate）< 10⁻⁶ 的要求，以保证 FEC（Forward Error Correction，前向纠错）之后的 Flit 错误概率小于 3×10⁻⁵。

</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>

With PAM4 encoding, the bit error rate (BER) is expected to be significantly worse than the 10-12 BER target of the lower data rates (2.5, 5.0, 8.0, 16.0, and 32.0 GT/s). In addition, errors are expected to occur in bursts in a Lane and some amount of Lane to Lane correlation is also expected. The electrical spec parameters along with FBER (First Bit Error Rate) < 10-6 described in § Chapter 8. must be met to ensure probability of a Flit error after FEC to be less than 3×10-5.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

使用 PAM4 编码时，预期误码率（BER）将明显高于较低数据速率（2.5、5.0、8.0、16.0 和 32.0 GT/s）所对应的 10⁻¹² BER 目标。此外，预期错误会成串（burst）地出现在同一通道（Lane）上，并且不同通道之间也会存在一定程度的相关性。必须满足 § 第 8 章 中所描述的电气规范参数以及 FBER（First Bit Error Rate）< 10⁻⁶ 的要求，以保证 FEC（Forward Error Correction，前向纠错）之后的 Flit 错误概率小于 3×10⁻⁵。

</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>

A Forward Error Correction (FEC) mechanism is used to deal with the high FBER in the Data Stream. Since FEC works on fixed sized code words, Flit (flow control unit) will be used for sending/receiving TLPs and DLLPs in a data stream. A low-overhead FEC will be used to keep the latency low. This will be augmented with a strong CRC at Flit level for high reliability. Link level retry will be deployed at the Flit level in Flit Mode. Ordered Sets will be protected through replication for Data Rates of 64.0 GT/s and above. Additionally, the precoding mechanism described below will be used for all scrambled bits to help minimize the number of errors in an error burst within a Lane.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

采用前向纠错（FEC, Forward Error Correction）机制来处理数据流中的高 FBER。由于 FEC 作用于固定大小的码字，Flit（流量控制单元）将用于在数据流中发送/接收 TLP（事务层包）和 DLLP（数据链路层包）。使用低开销的 FEC 以保持较低的延迟，并辅以 Flit 级别的强 CRC 以获得高可靠性。在 Flit 模式下，链路级别的重传（Retry）将以 Flit 为单位进行。对于 64.0 GT/s 及以上数据速率，有序集（Ordered Set）将通过复制（Replication）进行保护。此外，下文所述的预编码（Precoding）机制将作用于所有加扰比特，以减少通道内错误串中的错误数。

</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>

The scrambling mechanism in the 64.0 GT/s Data Rate is identical to that for the 8.0, 16.0, and 32.0 GT/s Data Rates and is already covered in § Section 4.2.2.4, except for the TS0 / TS1 / TS2 / SKP Ordered Set rules defined below. § Figure 4-25 demonstrates the scrambler on the Transmitter side. All Data Stream bits as well as some Ordered Set bits are scrambled.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

64.0 GT/s 数据速率下的加扰机制与 8.0、16.0 和 32.0 GT/s 数据速率下的加扰机制完全相同，已在 § 第 4.2.2.4 节 中涵盖，但下文定义的 TS0/TS1/TS2/SKP 有序集规则除外。§ 图 4-25 演示了发送器端的加扰器。所有数据流比特以及部分有序集比特都会被加扰。

</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>

- TS1 and TS2 Ordered Sets:
  - Symbols 0 and 8 bypass scrambling.
  - Symbols 1 through 6 and Symbols 9 through 14 are scrambled.
  - Symbols 7 and 15 bypass scrambling if required for DC Balance, but they are scrambled if not required for DC Balance.
- TS0 Ordered Sets:
  - Symbols 0 and 8 bypass scrambling
  - Symbols 1 through 6 and Symbols 9 through14 use NRZ-based scrambling
    - NRZ-based scrambling consists of scrambling all 8 bits and then, in place of gray-coding, forcing the even bit 2i to be identical to the odd bit (2i+1) [where 0≤i≤3]. This ensures that only voltage levels 0 and 3 are sent.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

- TS1 和 TS2 有序集：
  - 符号 0 和 8 旁路加扰。
  - 符号 1 至 6 以及符号 9 至 14 进行加扰。
  - 符号 7 和 15 在需要时为保持直流平衡（DC Balance）而旁路加扰，否则仍执行加扰。
- TS0 有序集：
  - 符号 0 和 8 旁路加扰
  - 符号 1 至 6 以及符号 9 至 14 使用基于 NRZ 的加扰
    - 基于 NRZ 的加扰首先对全部 8 比特加扰，然后以"令偶数位 2i 与奇数位 (2i+1) 相同 [其中 0≤i≤3]"代替格雷编码。这样可以保证只发送电压电平 0 和 3。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>

---

<a id="sec-4-2-3-1-2"></a>
## 4.2.3.1.2 1b/1b Scrambling | 1b/1b 加扰


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

- Symbols 7 and 15 bypass scrambling if required for DC Balance; else they carry even parity of Symbols 1 through 6 and Symbols 9 through 14 respectively with NRZ-scrambling
- All Symbols in TS0 are NRZ-based (i.e., each UI maps to voltage levels 0 or 3, encoded as 00b or 10b). Only TS0 Ordered Set is designed to be NRZ-based in 64.0 GT/s Data Rate.
- SKP Ordered Sets:
  - When processing Ordered Sets outside of a Data Stream, receivers use the 1b/1b SKP decode rules in § Section 4.2.3.1.5. When processing Ordered Sets inside a Data Stream, receivers use the 1b/1b SKP decode rules in § Section 4.2.3.2. If it is determined by the Receiver that the Ordered Set is a SKP Ordered Set, then the LFSR is not advanced for any Symbol of the Block, as specified in § Section 4.2.2.4.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

- 符号 7 和 15 在需要时为保持直流平衡（DC Balance）而旁路加扰；否则它们分别承载符号 1 至 6 和符号 9 至 14 的偶校验，并进行 NRZ 加扰
- TS0 中的所有符号均基于 NRZ（即每个 UI 映射为电压电平 0 或 3，编码为 00b 或 10b）。在 64.0 GT/s 数据速率下，仅 TS0 有序集被设计为基于 NRZ。
- SKP 有序集：
  - 在数据流之外处理有序集时，接收器使用 § 第 4.2.3.1.5 节 中的 1b/1b SKP 解码规则。在数据流之内处理有序集时，接收器使用 § 第 4.2.3.2 节 中的 1b/1b SKP 解码规则。如果接收器判定该有序集为 SKP 有序集，则按 § 第 4.2.2.4 节 中的规定，该块中任何符号对应的 LFSR 都不推进。

</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>

Only scrambled bits on a 2-bit aligned boundary are Gray coded, when both bits are scrambled. All bits in the Data Stream are scrambled and Gray coded. Only some Symbols of the TS1/TS2 Ordered Sets are scrambled – those are Gray coded, while the unscrambled symbols are not Gray coded. All TS0 Ordered Set Symbols to be Half Scrambled undergo NRZ-based scrambling, as described in § Section 4.2.3.1.2. § Figure 4-25 represents the sequence of Gray Coding, which is followed by Tx Precoding, when enabled, followed by the PAM4 voltage translation on the Tx side as well as the Rx side, considering an error En that may affect the nth 2-bit quantity on the channel.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

仅当 2 比特对齐边界上的两个比特均被加扰时，才对其进行格雷编码（Gray Coding）。数据流中的所有比特均加扰并执行格雷编码。TS1/TS2 有序集中只有部分符号被加扰——这些被加扰的符号执行格雷编码，而未加扰的符号不执行格雷编码。所有需"半加扰"的 TS0 有序集符号按 § 第 4.2.3.1.2 节 中所述进行基于 NRZ 的加扰。§ 图 4-25 表示格雷编码的顺序，其后（若启用）为发送器预编码（Tx Precoding），再其后为 Tx 端与 Rx 端的 PAM4 电压转换，并考虑可能影响通道上第 n 个 2 比特量的错误 En。

</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>

Gray Coding is only enabled in the PAM4 mode (at 64.0 GT/s and above Data Rates). Gray-coding works on aligned 2-bit quantities on a per-Lane basis. On the Transmit side, input Gn (Gn1Gn0) is transformed to output Pn (Pn1Pn0), using the Gray code encoding where the input 00b, 01b, 10b, 11b becomes 00b, 01b, 11b, and 10b respectively in the output. This can be represented by the equations: Pn1 = Gn1 and Pn0 = Gn1 ^ Gn0. The Receive side is identical and can be represented as: G′n1 = P′n1 and G′n0 = P′n1 ^ P′n0. § Table 4-6 demonstrates how a likely error of ±1 on voltage level at most affects one bit with Gray Coding.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

格雷编码仅在 PAM4 模式（即 64.0 GT/s 及以上数据速率）下启用。格雷编码按通道（per-Lane）作用于对齐的 2 比特量。在发送端，输入 Gn (Gn1Gn0) 经格雷编码变换为输出 Pn (Pn1Pn0)，即输入 00b、01b、10b、11b 分别对应输出 00b、01b、11b、10b。该变换可由公式表示为：Pn1 = Gn1，Pn0 = Gn1 ^ Gn0。接收端与此相同，可表示为：G′n1 = P′n1，G′n0 = P′n1 ^ P′n0。§ 表 4-6 演示了采用格雷编码时，电压电平上 ±1 的常见错误至多影响 1 比特。

</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>

Channel (Error En)

Symbols of Ordered Sets or patterns to be scrambled

Scrambling (1-bit)

Flit

Gray Coding (2-bit aligned)

Gn

TS0 OS or Precoding disabled

Tx Precoding (2-bit aligned)

Tn

Pn

PAM-4 voltage Level generation (2-bit aligned)

PAM-4 voltage Conversion to 2-bit aligned

Rn

Rx Precoding (2-bit aligned)

R′n

TS0 OS or Precoding disabled

Gray Coding (2-bit aligned)

Tn

Scrambling (1 bit level)

P′n

Flit

Symbols of Ordered Sets or Patterns that were scrambled

At these points, the mappings are:
00 = V0, 01 = V1, 10 = V2, 11 = V3

Symbols of Ordered Sets or Patterns that were not scrambled

Symbols or Ordered Sets or Patterns that are not to be scrambled

Figure 4-25 The Sequence of Gray Coding, Precoding, and PAM4 voltage translation on an aligned 2-bit boundary on a per Lane

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

通道（错误 En）

Rn

</td>
</tr>

<td>

**IMPLEMENTATION NOTE:**
**NRZ-BASED SCRAMBLING**

During NRZ-based scrambling, forcing the even bit 2i to be identical to the odd bit (2i+1) [where 0≤i≤3] can be performed after gray-coding or instead of gray-coding. The result will be the same either way.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

**实现注意事项：**
**基于 NRZ 的加扰**

在基于 NRZ 的加扰过程中，"令偶数位 2i 与奇数位 (2i+1) 相同 [其中 0≤i≤3]"这一操作可在格雷编码之后执行，也可代替格雷编码执行。两种方式的结果相同。

</td>
</tr>

<a id="sec-4-2-3-1-3"></a>
## 4.2.3.1.3 Gray Coding at 64.0 GT/s and Higher Data Rates | 64.0 GT/s 及更高数据速率下的格雷编码

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

**Table 4-6 Effect of +/-1 voltage level error on the wire for various PAM4 voltage levels – at most one bit flips with an error on a UI**

| Voltage Level | Resulting Voltage Level (Error of +1) | Resulting Voltage Level (Error of -1) |
|---|---|---|
| 0 | 1 | 0 (no error) |
| 1 | 2 | 0 |
| 2 | 3 | 1 |
| 3 | 3 (no error) | 2 |

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

**表 4-6 各种 PAM4 电压电平在通道上 ±1 电压电平错误的影响——UI 上至多翻转 1 比特**

| 电压电平 | 错误 +1 后电压电平 | 错误 -1 后电压电平 |
|---|---|---|
| 0 | 1 | 0（无错误）|
| 1 | 2 | 0 |
| 2 | 3 | 1 |
| 3 | 3（无错误）| 2 |

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
