<a id="sec-4-2-3-1-4"></a>
## 4.2.3.1.4 Precoding at 64.0 GT/s and Higher Data Rates | 64.0 GT/s 及更高数据速率下的预编码


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

Only scrambled bits on a 2-bit aligned boundary are precoded, when both bits are scrambled and precoding is enabled. Precoding is bypassed for all Symbols of TS0 Ordered Sets. Other than the precoding mechanism described here, all the precoding rules in § Section 4.2.2.5 apply to 1b/1b encoding with the exception of TS0 Ordered Set. § Figure 4-25 represents the Precoding on the Transmit as well as Receive side. On the Transmit side, the input is Pn and the output is Tn. Tn−1 represents the output from the precoder in the previous UI. When the prior UI was unscrambled and precoding is enabled, Tn−1 is set to 00b. The output Tn will be converted to the PAM4 voltage levels described above, when precoding is enabled. The channel may inject an error En (no error means En = 00b). On the Receive side, Rn = Tn + En. The input to the Precoding logic is R′n (Rn = Tn + en), where en is inclusive of the error on the wire En as well as any internal DFE propagated error. The output of the precoding logic is P′n, when precoding is enabled. The Truth Table for the Precoding function for the Transmit and Receive side is shown in § Table 4-7 and § Table 4-8. Precoding can help mitigate the number of errors in a burst.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

仅当 2 比特对齐边界上的两个比特均被加扰、且预编码（Precoding）启用时，才对其进行预编码。TS0 有序集的所有符号均旁路预编码。除了此处所述的预编码机制外，§ 第 4.2.2.5 节 中的所有预编码规则均适用于 1b/1b 编码（TS0 有序集除外）。§ 图 4-25 展示了发送端与接收端的预编码。在发送端，输入为 Pn，输出为 Tn。Tn−1 表示前一个 UI 中预编码器的输出。当前一 UI 未加扰且预编码启用时，Tn−1 被设置为 00b。当预编码启用时，输出 Tn 将被转换为前述 PAM4 电压电平。通道可能引入错误 En（无错误表示 En = 00b）。在接收端，Rn = Tn + En。预编码逻辑的输入为 R′n（Rn = Tn + en），其中 en 包含通道上的错误 En 以及任何内部 DFE 传播的错误。当预编码启用时，预编码逻辑的输出为 P′n。发送端与接收端预编码功能的真值表分别如 § 表 4-7 和 § 表 4-8 所示。预编码有助于减少错误串中的错误数。

</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>

**Table 4-7 Truth Table for Precoding on the Transmit side**

| Tn / Tn-1 | 00 | 01 | 11 | 10 |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 00 | 00 | 11 | 01 | 10 |
| 01 | 01 | 00 | 10 | 11 |
| 11 | 11 | 10 | 00 | 01 |
| 10 | 10 | 01 | 11 | 00 |

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

**表 4-7 发送端预编码真值表**

| Tn / Tn-1 | 00 | 01 | 11 | 10 |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 00 | 00 | 11 | 01 | 10 |
| 01 | 01 | 00 | 10 | 11 |
| 11 | 11 | 10 | 00 | 01 |
| 10 | 10 | 01 | 11 | 00 |

</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>

**Table 4-8 Truth Table for Precoding on the Receive side**

| P′n / R′n-1 | 00 | 01 | 11 | 10 |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 00 | 00 | 01 | 11 | 10 |
| 01 | 01 | 10 | 00 | 11 |
| 11 | 11 | 00 | 10 | 01 |
| 10 | 10 | 11 | 01 | 00 |

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

**表 4-8 接收端预编码真值表**

| P′n / R′n-1 | 00 | 01 | 11 | 10 |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 00 | 00 | 01 | 11 | 10 |
| 01 | 01 | 10 | 00 | 11 |
| 11 | 11 | 00 | 10 | 01 |
| 10 | 10 | 11 | 01 | 00 |

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>


---

<a id="sec-4-2-3-1-5"></a>
## 4.2.3.1.5 Ordered Set Blocks at 64.0 GT/s and Higher Data Rates | 64.0 GT/s 及更高数据速率下的有序集块

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

All Ordered Sets with the exception SKP Ordered Sets (i.e., TS0, TS1, TS2, EIOS, EIEOS and SDS) are 16 Bytes long on both the Transmit and the Receive side. SKP Ordered Sets are 40 Bytes long on the Transmit side and can be 24, 32, 40, 48 or 56 bytes long on the Receive side. Ordered Sets exhibit a greater degree of redundancy than the corresponding Ordered Sets at Data Rates lower than 64.0 GT/s. The redundancy is required due to the high FBER with correlated errors with PAM4 signaling. The Ordered Sets are described in detail in § Section 4.2.5.1, § Section 4.2.5.3, § Section 4.2.5.7, and § Section 4.2.8.3.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

除 SKP 有序集外，所有有序集（即 TS0、TS1、TS2、EIOS、EIEOS 和 SDS）在发送端与接收端均为 16 字节长。SKP 有序集在发送端为 40 字节长，在接收端可为 24、32、40、48 或 56 字节长。与低于 64.0 GT/s 的数据速率下对应的有序集相比，有序集表现出更高程度的冗余。该冗余是 PAM4 信令下高 FBER 且错误相关的需求。详细的有序集描述见 § 第 4.2.5.1 节、§ 第 4.2.5.3 节、§ 第 4.2.5.7 节 和 § 第 4.2.8.3 节。

</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>

While processing Ordered Sets outside of a Data Stream, the following rules apply for the EIEOS, SKP OS, and EIOS, after obtaining Block alignment:

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

在数据流之外处理有序集时，获得块对齐（Block Alignment）后，EIEOS、SKP OS 和 EIOS 适用以下规则：

</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>

- An EIEOS is considered to be received under the following conditions:
  - any 5 or more aligned and consecutive bytes in each of the first and last 8 bytes of a Block match the corresponding bytes of an EIEOS and
  - either Symbol 0 or Symbol 8 of the Block matches the corresponding byte of an EIEOS. A Receiver is permitted to ignore up to one bit mismatch for this comparison

While in Aligned phase, a Receiver must switch the block boundary by > 1 UI on an EIEOS Ordered Set or should shift the Block boundary by ≤ 1 UI only if it receives an EIEOS followed by a valid first symbol of an expected Ordered Set.

While searching for an EIEOS, the 8-byte boundary can begin in any aligned UI and the bits must be looked at prior to performing gray coding, precoding, or descrambling in the Receiver side.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

- 满足以下条件时认为已接收到 EIEOS：
  - 在一个块的前 8 字节和后 8 字节中，每一段均有 5 个或以上对齐且连续的字节与 EIEOS 的对应字节匹配；并且
  - 块的符号 0 或符号 8 与 EIEOS 的对应字节匹配。允许接收器在该比较中忽略至多 1 比特不匹配

在 Aligned 阶段，接收器在 EIEOS 有序集上必须将块边界切换 > 1 UI；或者仅当接收器在 EIEOS 之后接收到预期有序集的有效首符号时，才可将块边界移动 ≤ 1 UI。

在搜索 EIEOS 时，8 字节边界可以从任一对齐的 UI 开始，且必须在执行格雷编码、预编码或解扰之前查看这些比特。

</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>

- An EIOS is considered to be received under the following conditions:
  - any 5 or more aligned bytes of the first 8 bytes of a Block match the corresponding bytes of an EIOS and
  - either Symbol 0 or Symbol 8 of the Block matches the corresponding byte of an EIOS. When an EIOS is received, the Lane is ready to enter Electrical Idle irrespective of what is received in the last 7 Bytes of the Block.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

- 满足以下条件时认为已接收到 EIOS：
  - 块的前 8 字节中有任意 5 个或以上对齐字节与 EIOS 的对应字节匹配；并且
  - 块的符号 0 或符号 8 与 EIOS 的对应字节匹配。当接收到 EIOS 时，无论块的最后 7 字节接收到什么，该通道都已准备好进入电气空闲（Electrical Idle）。

</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>

- A SKP OS is considered to begin under the following conditions: (i) any 5 or more aligned bytes of the first 8 bytes of a Block match a SKP, and (ii) either Symbol 0 is a SKP or Symbol 8 of the Block is either a SKP or a SKP_END. It looks at each subsequent aligned 8 byte chunks and applies the following rules:
  - If any 5 or more aligned bytes match SKP_END or the current chunk is the fifth 8 byte chunk after the start of the SKP OS (i.e., current set is bytes 40 through 47 from the start of the SKP OS), the SKP OS will terminate after the next aligned 8 byte chunk.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

- 满足以下条件时认为 SKP OS 开始：(i) 块的前 8 字节中有任意 5 个或以上对齐字节与 SKP 匹配；并且 (ii) 符号 0 为 SKP，或者块的符号 8 为 SKP 或 SKP_END。然后对后续每个对齐的 8 字节块应用以下规则：
  - 如果任意 5 个或以上对齐字节与 SKP_END 匹配，或者当前块是 SKP OS 开始后的第 5 个 8 字节块（即当前集合为 SKP OS 起始后第 40 至 47 字节），则在下一个对齐的 8 字节块之后 SKP OS 终止。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

---

<a id="sec-4-2-3-1-6"></a>
## 4.2.3.1.6 Alignment at Block/Flit Level for 1b/1b Encoding | 1b/1b 编码下的块/Flit 级对齐


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

With 1b/1b encoding, Block alignment aligns with the Ordered Set boundary on a per-Lane basis. Once this is accomplished, the Flit-level alignment at the Link level occurs automatically with the Data Stream that starts at the end of the Control SKP Ordered Set that immediately follows the SDS Ordered Set sequence. Once the Flit alignment starts, it adjusts to the Flit boundary. When an Ordered Set is scheduled to be received and the received Ordered Set is a Control SKP Ordered Set, the Flit boundary is automatically adjusted to start at the conclusion of the Ordered Set.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

在 1b/1b 编码下，块对齐按通道（per-Lane）与有序集边界对齐。完成该对齐后，链路级的 Flit 级对齐将自动发生在数据流中——该数据流起始于紧接 SDS 有序集序列之后的控制 SKP 有序集的末尾。一旦 Flit 对齐开始，它便调整到 Flit 边界。当预期接收一个有序集且接收到的有序集是控制 SKP 有序集时，Flit 边界会自动调整，使其起始于该有序集的结束处。

</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>

The Block level alignment occurs with the EIEOS Ordered Set in Configuration or Recovery states. The EIEOS is a unique pattern that Receivers use to determine the start/end of the Ordered Set boundary in the received bit stream. Conceptually, Receivers can be in three different phases of alignment: Unaligned, Aligned, and Locked. These phases are defined to illustrate the required behavior, but are not meant to specify a required implementation.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

块级对齐在 Configuration 或 Recovery 状态下使用 EIEOS 有序集实现。EIEOS 是一种独特的码型，接收器使用它来确定所接收比特流中有序集边界的开始/结束。从概念上讲，接收器可处于三种不同的对齐阶段：Unaligned、Aligned 和 Locked。定义这些阶段是为了说明所需的行为，并非规定必须的实现方式。

</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>

**Unaligned Phase**

Receivers enter this phase after a period of Electrical Idle, such as when the data rate is changed to one that uses 1b/1b encoding or when they exit a low-power Link state, or if directed (by an implementation specific method). In this phase, Receivers monitor the received bit stream for a match against all 128 bits of the EIEOS bit pattern. When one is detected, they adjust their alignment to it and proceed to the Aligned Phase.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

**Unaligned 阶段**

接收器在经历一段电气空闲（Electrical Idle）后进入该阶段，例如当数据速率切换到使用 1b/1b 编码的速率时，或退出低功耗链路状态时，或被（通过实现特定的方法）指示进入该阶段时。在该阶段，接收器监视接收到的比特流，查找与 EIEOS 比特码型（全部 128 比特）完全匹配的模式。当检测到一个匹配时，它们将自己的对齐调整到该匹配处，并进入 Aligned 阶段。

</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>

**Aligned Phase**

Receivers monitor the received bit stream for the EIEOS OS and the received Blocks for a Start of Data Stream (SDS) Ordered Set. If an EIEOS bit pattern is detected on an alignment that does not match the current alignment and the LTSSM is in the Recovery.RcvrLock state, Receivers must adjust their alignment to the newly received EIEOS bit pattern. If an EIEOS bit pattern is detected on an alignment that does not match the current alignment, the LTSSM is in the Recovery.RcvrCfg state, and the subsequent Symbol matches the first Symbol of an expected Ordered Set, Receivers must adjust their alignment to the newly received EIEOS bit pattern. If an SDS Ordered Set is received, Receivers proceed to the Locked phase. The Data Stream starts after the SDS Ordered Set sequence is received, though Flits start after the Control SKP Ordered Set immediately succeeding the SDS Ordered Set sequence. The conclusion of the SKP Ordered Set immediately following an SDS Ordered Set sequence marks the start of the Flit boundary at the end of the Control SKP Ordered Set.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

**Aligned 阶段**

接收器监视接收到的比特流中是否存在 EIEOS OS，并监视接收到的块中是否存在数据流起始（SDS, Start of Data Stream）有序集。如果在与当前对齐不一致的对齐位置检测到 EIEOS 比特码型，且 LTSSM 处于 Recovery.RcvrLock 状态，则接收器必须将自身对齐调整到新接收到的 EIEOS 比特码型上。如果在与当前对齐不一致的对齐位置检测到 EIEOS 比特码型，且 LTSSM 处于 Recovery.RcvrCfg 状态，且后续符号与预期有序集的首符号匹配，则接收器必须将自身对齐调整到新接收到的 EIEOS 比特码型上。如果接收到 SDS 有序集，则接收器进入 Locked 阶段。数据流在 SDS 有序集序列接收完毕之后开始，而 Flit 在紧接 SDS 有序集序列之后的控制 SKP 有序集之后开始。紧随 SDS 有序集序列的 SKP 有序集的结束标志着该控制 SKP 有序集末尾处的 Flit 边界的开始。

</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>

**IMPLEMENTATION NOTE:**
**INFERRING ORDERED SETS WITHIN AND OUTSIDE OF DATA STREAMS**

Since most bytes of TS0/TS1/TS2 Ordered Sets are scrambled (except Symbols 0 and 8), one has to get an exact match in at least Symbol 0 or 8, in addition to the match in 5 Byte positions to infer the EIEOS, EIOS, or SKP OS, while not in a Data Stream. The rules for detection of TS0, TS1, TS2, and SDS Ordered Set appears with the description of the Ordered Set. Since SKP, EIEOS, and EIOS can appear within a Data Stream, there are slightly different rules for inferring those in a Data Stream than while not in a Data Stream, taking advantage of the fact that none of these Ordered Sets have any scrambling, to ensure higher fault tolerance during a Data Stream. Hence the rules for detection of EIOS, EIEOS and SKP OS are specified twice; in this section as well as in § Section 4.2.8.3.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

**实现注意事项：**
**在数据流内和数据流外推断有序集**

由于 TS0/TS1/TS2 有序集的大多数字节都被加扰（符号 0 和 8 除外），在非数据流状态下，除了 5 个字节位置的匹配外，还必须在符号 0 或 8 中至少获得一个完全匹配，才能推断为 EIEOS、EIOS 或 SKP OS。TS0、TS1、TS2 和 SDS 有序集的检测规则随各自的有序集描述给出。由于 SKP、EIEOS 和 EIOS 可出现在数据流内，因此在数据流内推断这些有序集的规则与在数据流外略有不同，这利用了这些有序集均不执行加扰这一事实，从而在数据流中获得更高的容错能力。因此，EIOS、EIEOS 和 SKP OS 的检测规则在本节以及 § 第 4.2.8.3 节 中各规定一次。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>


---

<a id="sec-4-2-3-2"></a>
## 4.2.3.2 Processing of Ordered Sets During Flit Mode Data Stream | Flit 模式数据流中有序集的处理

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

**Locked Phase**

Receivers must not adjust their Block or Flit alignment while in this phase. Data Blocks are expected to be received after the Control SKP Ordered Set following the SDS Ordered Set sequence, and adjusting the Block alignment would interfere with the processing of these Blocks. Receivers must return to the Unaligned or Aligned Phase if the Data Stream is terminated by the Link entering Recovery and/or a Framing Error is detected.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

**Locked 阶段**

处于该阶段时，接收器不得调整其块对齐或 Flit 对齐。预期在 SDS 有序集序列之后的控制 SKP 有序集之后接收数据块，调整块对齐会干扰对这些块的处理。如果数据流因链路进入 Recovery 或检测到帧错误而终止，则接收器必须返回 Unaligned 或 Aligned 阶段。

</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>

**Additional Requirements:**

- While in the Aligned Phase, Receivers must adjust their Block alignment, as necessary, when a Control SKP Ordered Set is received. See § Section 4.2.8 for more information on SKP Ordered Sets.
- While in the Locked phase, Receivers must adjust their Block and Flit alignment, as necessary, when a Control SKP Ordered Set is received.
- After any LTSSM transition to Recovery, Receivers must ignore all received TS Ordered Sets until they receive an EIEOS. Conceptually, receiving an EIEOS validates the Receiver's alignment and allows TS Ordered Set processing to proceed. If a received EIEOS initiates an LTSSM transition from L0 to Recovery, Receivers are permitted to process any TS Ordered Sets that follow the EIEOS or ignore them until another EIEOS is received after entering Recovery.
- Receivers are permitted to transition from the Locked phase to the Unaligned or Aligned phase as long as Data Stream processing is stopped.
- **Loopback Leads:** While in Loopback.Entry, Leads must be capable of adjusting their Receiver's Block/Flit alignment to received EIEOS bit patterns. While in Loopback.Active, Leads are permitted to transmit an EIEOS and adjust their Receiver's Block alignment to the looped back bit stream.
- **Loopback Followers:** While in Loopback.Entry, Followers must be capable of adjusting their Receiver's Block alignment to received EIEOS bit patterns. While in Loopback.Active, Followers must not adjust their Receiver's Block alignment, except at the scheduled Control SKP Ordered Set boundary. Conceptually, the Receiver is directed to the Locked phase when the Follower starts to loop back the received bit stream.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

**附加要求：**

- 在 Aligned 阶段，接收器必须在接收到控制 SKP 有序集时按需调整其块对齐。有关 SKP 有序集的更多信息，请参见 § 第 4.2.8 节。
- 在 Locked 阶段，接收器必须在接收到控制 SKP 有序集时按需调整其块对齐和 Flit 对齐。
- 在 LTSSM 转入 Recovery 之后，接收器必须忽略所有已接收的 TS 有序集，直至接收到 EIEOS。从概念上讲，接收到 EIEOS 即验证了接收器的对齐正确性，并允许继续处理 TS 有序集。如果接收到的 EIEOS 引发了 LTSSM 从 L0 进入 Recovery 的转移，则允许接收器处理该 EIEOS 之后紧随的 TS 有序集，也允许忽略它们，直至在进入 Recovery 之后再次接收到 EIEOS 为止。
- 在数据流处理已停止的前提下，允许接收器从 Locked 阶段转移到 Unaligned 或 Aligned 阶段。
- **Loopback Leader（回环主导方）**：在 Loopback.Entry 期间，Leader 必须能够将其接收器的块/Flit 对齐调整到接收到的 EIEOS 比特码型。在 Loopback.Active 期间，允许 Leader 发送一个 EIEOS，并将其接收器的块对齐调整到回环比特流上。
- **Loopback Follower（回环从属方）**：在 Loopback.Entry 期间，Follower 必须能够将其接收器的块对齐调整到接收到的 EIEOS 比特码型。在 Loopback.Active 期间，Follower 不得调整其接收器的块对齐，仅在预定的控制 SKP 有序集边界处例外。从概念上讲，当 Follower 开始回环所接收的比特流时，其接收器被引导进入 Locked 阶段。

</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>

Flit Mode does not employ the EDS ('End of Data Stream') token. Instead, a Data Stream can be terminated by either an EIOS (prior to entering a low power state, such as L1) or an EIEOS (upon entry into Recovery). SKP Ordered Sets appear at periodic intervals during the Data Stream (as defined in § Table 4-21. When necessary, the EIOS or EIEOS must be transmitted in place of the SKP Ordered Set, as defined in § Section 4.2.3.4.5. Therefore, these three Ordered Sets are defined such that one cannot alias to another even with a burst error.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

Flit 模式不使用 EDS（End of Data Stream）令牌。数据流的终止可通过 EIOS（在进入低功耗状态如 L1 之前）或 EIEOS（进入 Recovery 时）实现。SKP 有序集在数据流中按 § 表 4-21 中所定义的周期性间隔出现。必要时，必须按 § 第 4.2.3.4.5 节 中的规定在 SKP 有序集所在位置发送 EIOS 或 EIEOS。因此，这三种有序集被定义为即使在突发错误下也不会相互混叠（alias）。

</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>

Ordered Set processing during or at the end of a Data Stream must be performed as follows by the Receiver for 64.0 GT/s or higher Data Rates:

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

对于 64.0 GT/s 或更高数据速率，接收器在数据流内或数据流末尾的有序集处理必须按以下方式执行：

</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>

- Receiver checks the first 8 bytes of the Ordered Set in the place where an Ordered Set is expected to appear.
- Any 5 or more aligned bytes of the first aligned 8 byte chunk matches the corresponding bytes of either a SKP, EIOS, or EIEOS for the corresponding Ordered Set to be considered valid in each of the active Lanes. Failure to meet this requirement is considered a framing error and the Link must enter Recovery.
- If a SKP Ordered Set is inferred from the first 8 bytes, the Receiver looks at each subsequent aligned 8 byte chunks and applies the following rules:
  - If at least each of any 5 aligned bytes matches SKP_END or the current chunk is the fifth 8 byte chunk after the start of the SKP OS (i.e., current set is bytes 40 through 47), the SKP OS will terminate after the next aligned 8 byte chunk. At the conclusion of the SKP OS the Data Stream resumes, if the SKP OS terminated with the receipt of 5 aligned bytes of SKP_END. If the SKP OS is being terminated without receipt of 5 aligned bytes of SKP_END, a Framing Error occurs.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

- 接收器在预期出现有序集的位置检查有序集的前 8 字节。
- 在每个活动通道上，第一个对齐 8 字节块中任意 5 个或以上对齐字节与 SKP、EIOS 或 EIEOS 的对应字节匹配，对应有序集才视为有效。未满足此要求视为帧错误（Framing Error），链路必须进入 Recovery。
- 如果从前 8 字节推断为 SKP 有序集，则接收器检查后续每个对齐 8 字节块并应用以下规则：
  - 如果任意 5 个对齐字节与 SKP_END 匹配，或者当前块是 SKP OS 起始后的第 5 个 8 字节块（即当前集合为第 40 至 47 字节），则 SKP OS 将在下一个对齐 8 字节块之后终止。如果 SKP OS 终止时收到了 5 个对齐字节的 SKP_END，则数据流在 SKP OS 结束后恢复。如果 SKP OS 终止时未收到 5 个对齐字节的 SKP_END，则发生帧错误。

</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>

- Any of the following conditions are Framing Error even with the receipt of a proper OS on each of active Lanes and the Link must enter Recovery:
  - Receiving any combination of these ordered sets simultaneously on any two active Lanes: EIEOS and EIOS or EIEOS and SKP OS,
  - Receiving a SKP OS of unequal lengths across all Lanes
  - Receiving a combination of EIOS and SKP OS and any of the following conditions is true:
    - L0p has not been enabled in the Link
    - The set of Lanes receiving EIOS are not contiguous
    - The set of Lanes receiving SKP OS are not contiguous
    - The number of Lanes receiving SKP OS is not a valid link width (1, 2, 4, or 8)
    - Any of these Lanes is receiving neither SKP OS nor EIOS.
- If an EIEOS is inferred from the first 8 bytes on any active Lane, the Data Stream ends with the receipt of the EIEOS and the Link must enter Recovery when permitted by the LTSSM.
- If an EIOS is inferred from the first 8 bytes across any active Lanes, the Link prepares to enter the low-power state for which the negotiation has occurred (L0p or L1 or L2).
- Data stream continues on a reduced set of Lanes at the conclusion of the OS if all of the following conditions are true:
  - An EIOS is inferred from the first 8 bytes on some active Lanes,
  - The remaining active Lanes received the SKP OS on 1, 2, 4 or 8 Lanes, and
  - L0p has been enabled in the Link

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

- 即使在每个活动通道上均收到正确的 OS，以下任一情况仍视为帧错误，链路必须进入 Recovery：
  - 在任意两个活动通道上同时接收到以下组合之一：EIEOS 与 EIOS，或 EIEOS 与 SKP OS；
  - 跨所有通道接收到长度不一致的 SKP OS；
  - 同时接收到 EIOS 和 SKP OS 的组合，并满足以下任一条件：
    - 链路上未启用 L0p
    - 接收到 EIOS 的通道集合不连续
    - 接收到 SKP OS 的通道集合不连续
    - 接收到 SKP OS 的通道数不是有效的链路宽度（1、2、4 或 8）
    - 上述通道中有任一通道既未接收到 SKP OS 也未接收到 EIOS。
- 如果从任一活动通道的前 8 字节推断为 EIEOS，则数据流在收到 EIEOS 时结束，且当 LTSSM 允许时链路必须进入 Recovery。
- 如果跨任一活动通道的前 8 字节推断为 EIOS，则链路准备进入已协商好的低功耗状态（L0p、L1 或 L2）。
- 在 OS 结束后，如果同时满足以下所有条件，则数据流在缩减后的通道集合上继续：
  - 在部分活动通道的前 8 字节推断为 EIOS，
  - 其余活动通道在 1、2、4 或 8 个通道上接收到 SKP OS，并且
  - 链路上已启用 L0p

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

---

<a id="sec-4-2-3-3"></a>
## 4.2.3.3 Data Stream in Flit Mode | Flit 模式数据流


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

The flit size is 256 Bytes, allocated as follows:

- 236 Bytes for TLPs (Bytes 0 through 235 in the Flit, as shown in § Table 4-10 through § Table 4-14)
- 6 Bytes for Data Link Layer Payload (DLP in Bytes 236 through 241 in the Flit, shown as DLP0..6 in § Table 4-10 through § Table 4-14)
- 8 Bytes for CRC (Bytes 242 through 249 in the Flit, shown as CRC0..7 in § Table 4-10 through § Table 4-14), and
- 6 Bytes for ECC (Bytes 250 through 255 arranged as 3 groups of ECC0[0:1], ECC1[0:1], ECC2[0:1], as shown in § Table 4-10 through § Table 4-14).

A flit is interleaved across the Lanes in the Link in a Byte aligned fashion, as shown in § Table 4-10 through § Table 4-14. Each Byte corresponds to a Symbol in the 64.0 GT/s and above Data Rates, a Symbol within the Data Block with 128b/130b encoding, for 8.0 GT/s, 16.0 GT/s, and 32.0 GT/s; and gets converted to a 10b Symbol using the 8b/10b encoding for 2.5 GT/s and 5.0 GT/s Data Rates. Since the TLP and DLP have fixed allocation of bytes within each flit, the Port will schedule the packets of each type accordingly. The 8 Bytes of CRC protects the TLP and DLLP Bytes (and not the ECC bytes). The 6 Bytes of ECC protects the entire flit, including the CRC. Even though the ECC protection is needed only with the high FBER with the PAM4 signaling at 64.0 GT/s and higher Data Rates, it will be deployed for the lower Data Rates for consistency.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

Flit 大小为 256 字节，分配如下：

- 236 字节用于 TLP（Flit 中的字节 0 至 235，如 § 表 4-10 至 § 表 4-14 所示）
- 6 字节用于数据链路层载荷（DLP，Flit 中的字节 236 至 241，在 § 表 4-10 至 § 表 4-14 中以 DLP0..6 表示）
- 8 字节用于 CRC（Flit 中的字节 242 至 249，在 § 表 4-10 至 § 表 4-14 中以 CRC0..7 表示），以及
- 6 字节用于 ECC（Flit 中的字节 250 至 255，排列为 ECC0[0:1]、ECC1[0:1]、ECC2[0:1] 三组，如 § 表 4-10 至 § 表 4-14 所示）。

Flit 按字节对齐在链路的各通道之间交错排列，如 § 表 4-10 至 § 表 4-14 所示。在 64.0 GT/s 及以上数据速率下，每一字节对应一个符号（Symbol）；在 8.0 GT/s、16.0 GT/s 和 32.0 GT/s 数据速率下，每一字节对应 128b/130b 编码数据块内的一个符号；在 2.5 GT/s 和 5.0 GT/s 数据速率下，每一字节使用 8b/10b 编码转换为 10b 符号。由于 TLP 和 DLP 在每个 Flit 中具有固定的字节分配，端口将相应地调度各类型的包。8 字节 CRC 保护 TLP 和 DLLP 字节（不保护 ECC 字节）。6 字节 ECC 保护整个 Flit，包括 CRC。尽管 ECC 保护仅在 64.0 GT/s 及更高数据速率 PAM4 信令高 FBER 情况下才需要，但为保持一致性，也会在较低数据速率下使用。

</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>

On a Link layer retry, only the TLPs will be replayed, and not the DLP. Thus, the link layer payload in each flit (DLP) does not enter the Link Layer Retry Buffer (LLRB) at the Transmitter. The DLP fields in the retried flits will reflect the latest Data Link Layer Payload. Thus, a DLP can be lost and the same mechanism of replication/aggregation for DLLPs ensures that the data link layer messages gets delivered to the Link partner.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

在链路层重传时，仅 TLP 会被重放，DLP 不会被重放。因此，每个 Flit 中的链路层载荷（DLP）不进入发送器中的链路层重传缓冲（LLRB, Link Layer Retry Buffer）。被重传的 Flit 中的 DLP 字段将反映最新的数据链路层载荷。因此，DLP 可能丢失，而针对 DLLP 的复制/聚合机制可保证数据链路层消息仍能传递到链路对端。

</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>

The FEC is a 3-way interleaved ECC, with each ECC code capable of correcting a single Byte error. The interleaving is done so that a burst error of up to 16 bits in any Lane does not impact more than a Byte in each interleaved ECC code word. Each of the interleaved ECC has a different color. Thus, all blue colored Bytes [e.g., 0, 3, 6, 9,…, 231, 234, DLP1, DLP4, CRC1, CRC4, and CRC7] are part of ECC Group 0 and are covered by the 2 blue colored ECC0[0] and ECC0[1] bytes (which are placed in the last Symbol of the flit in Lanes 12 and 15 respectively).

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

FEC 采用 3 路交织的 ECC，每路 ECC 码可纠正 1 字节错误。进行交织后，任何通道上长达 16 比特的突发错误不会影响每个交织 ECC 码字中的超过 1 字节。每个交织 ECC 码对应不同的颜色。因此，所有蓝色字节 [例如 0、3、6、9、…、231、234、DLP1、DLP4、CRC1、CRC4 和 CRC7] 均属于 ECC 组 0，并由 2 个蓝色字节 ECC0[0] 和 ECC0[1] 进行覆盖（这两个字节分别位于 Flit 最后一个符号的通道 12 和 15 中）。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>


---

**Table 4-10 Flit Layout in a x16 Link | 表 4-10 x16 链路中的 Flit 布局**

> **Figure 4-26 Processing of Ordered Sets during or at the end of a Data Stream in Flit mode at 64.0 GT/s Data Rate**
> <img src="figures/chapter_04/fig_0393_1_tight.png" width="700">

| Description | Lane 0 | Lane 1 | Lane 2 | Lane 3 | Lane 4 | Lane 5 | Lane 6 | Lane 7 | Lane 8 | Lane 9 | Lane 10 | Lane 11 | Lane 12 | Lane 13 | Lane 14 | Lane 15 |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 128 UI TLP Bytes [0…223] | 0–15 | 16–31 | 32–47 | 48–63 | 64–79 | 80–95 | 96–111 | 112–127 | 128–143 | 144–159 | 160–175 | 176–191 | 192–207 | 208–223 | 224–239 | 240–255 |
| TLP, DLP (Bytes 224–235) | 224 | 225 | 226 | 227 | 228 | 229 | 230 | 231 | 232 | 233 | 234 | 235 | DLP0 | DLP1 | DLP2 | DLP3 |
| DLP, CRC, ECC | DLP4 | DLP5 | CRC0 | CRC1 | CRC2 | CRC3 | CRC4 | CRC5 | CRC6 | CRC7 | ECC1[0] | ECC2[0] | ECC0[0] | ECC1[1] | ECC2[1] | ECC0[1] |

**IMPLEMENTATION NOTE:** The first 236 Bytes (0..235) are for TLP(s), next 6 Bytes are for Data Link Layer Payload (DLP0..5), next 8 Bytes for CRC (CRC0..7) and the last 6 Bytes are for ECC (ecc 0..1). The FEC is a 3-way interleaved ECC, each capable of correcting a single Byte, with the interleaving shown in 3 colors.

**实现注意事项：** 前 236 字节（0..235）用于 TLP，接下来 6 字节用于数据链路层载荷（DLP0..5），再接下来 8 字节用于 CRC（CRC0..7），最后 6 字节用于 ECC（ecc 0..1）。FEC 采用 3 路交织 ECC，每路可纠正 1 字节错误，交织以 3 种颜色显示。

**Table 4-11 Flit interleaving in a x8 Link | 表 4-11 x8 链路中的 Flit 交织**

| Description | Lane 0 | Lane 1 | Lane 2 | Lane 3 | Lane 4 | Lane 5 | Lane 6 | Lane 7 |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 256 UI TLP Bytes [0…231] | 0–31 | 32–63 | 64–95 | 96–127 | 128–159 | 160–191 | 192–223 | 224–231 |
| TLP Bytes [231…235], DLP Bytes (232–235) | 232 | 233 | 234 | 235 | DLP0 | DLP1 | DLP2 | DLP3 |
| DLP, CRC Bytes | DLP4 | DLP5 | CRC0 | CRC1 | CRC2 | CRC3 | CRC4 | CRC5 |
| CRC, ECC Bytes | CRC6 | CRC7 | ECC1[0] | ECC2[0] | ECC0[0] | ECC1[1] | ECC2[1] | ECC0[1] |

**IMPLEMENTATION NOTE:** Note that the same set of Bytes form the same ECC group in the 3-way interleaved FEC as in a x16 Link. The first 236 Bytes (0..235) are for TLP(s), next 6 Bytes are for Data Link Layer Payload (DLP0..5), next 8 Bytes for CRC (CRC0..7) and the last 6 Bytes are for ECC (ecc 0..1). The FEC is a 3-way interleaved ECC, each capable of correcting a single Byte, with the interleaving shown in 3 colors.

**实现注意事项：** 注意与 x16 链路一样，相同的字节集合构成 3 路交织 FEC 中相同的 ECC 组。前 236 字节（0..235）用于 TLP，接下来 6 字节用于数据链路层载荷（DLP0..5），再接下来 8 字节用于 CRC（CRC0..7），最后 6 字节用于 ECC（ecc 0..1）。FEC 采用 3 路交织 ECC，每路可纠正 1 字节错误，交织以 3 种颜色显示。

---

**Table 4-12 Flit interleaving in a x4 Link | 表 4-12 x4 链路中的 Flit 交织**

| Description | Lane 0 | Lane 1 | Lane 2 | Lane 3 |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 512 UI TLP Bytes [0…235] | 0–63 | 64–127 | 128–191 | 192–235 |
| DLP, CRC, ECC Bytes | DLP0 | DLP1 | DLP2 | DLP3 |
| DLP, CRC, ECC Bytes | DLP4 | DLP5 | CRC0 | CRC1 |
| DLP, CRC, ECC Bytes | CRC2 | CRC3 | CRC4 | CRC5 |
| DLP, CRC, ECC Bytes | CRC6 | CRC7 | ECC1[0] ECC2[0] | ECC0[0] |
| DLP, CRC, ECC Bytes | ECC1[1] | ECC2[1] | ECC0[1] | – |

**IMPLEMENTATION NOTE:** Note that the same set of Bytes form the same ECC group in the 3-way interleaved FEC as in a x16/x8 Link. The first 236 Bytes (0..235) are for TLP(s), next 6 Bytes are for Data Link Layer Payload (DLP0..5), next 8 Bytes for CRC (CRC0..7) and the last 6 Bytes are for ECC (ecc 0..1). The FEC is a 3-way interleaved ECC, each capable of correcting a single Byte, with the interleaving shown in 3 colors.

**实现注意事项：** 注意与 x16/x8 链路一样，相同的字节集合构成 3 路交织 FEC 中相同的 ECC 组。前 236 字节（0..235）用于 TLP，接下来 6 字节用于数据链路层载荷（DLP0..5），再接下来 8 字节用于 CRC（CRC0..7），最后 6 字节用于 ECC（ecc 0..1）。FEC 采用 3 路交织 ECC，每路可纠正 1 字节错误，交织以 3 种颜色显示。

---

**Table 4-13 Flit interleaving in a x2 Link | 表 4-13 x2 链路中的 Flit 交织**

| Description | Lane 0 | Lane 1 |
|---|---|---|
| 1024 UI TLP Bytes [0…235] | 0–127 | 128–235 |
| DLP Bytes | DLP0 | DLP1 |
| DLP Bytes | DLP2 | DLP3 |
| DLP Bytes | DLP4 | DLP5 |
| CRC Bytes | CRC0 | CRC1 |
| CRC Bytes | CRC2 | CRC3 |
| CRC Bytes | CRC4 | CRC5 |
| CRC Bytes | CRC6 | CRC7 |
| ECC Bytes | ECC1[0] ECC2[0] | ECC0[0] ECC1[1] |
| ECC Bytes | ECC2[1] | ECC0[1] |

**IMPLEMENTATION NOTE:** Note that the same set of Bytes form the same ECC group in the 3-way interleaved FEC as in a x16/x8/x4 Link. The first 236 Bytes (0..235) are for TLP(s), next 6 Bytes are for Data Link Layer Payload (DLP0..5), next 8 Bytes for CRC (CRC0..7) and the last 6 Bytes are for ECC (ecc 0..1). The FEC is a 3-way interleaved ECC, each capable of correcting a single Byte, with the interleaving shown in 3 colors.

**实现注意事项：** 注意与 x16/x8/x4 链路一样，相同的字节集合构成 3 路交织 FEC 中相同的 ECC 组。前 236 字节（0..235）用于 TLP，接下来 6 字节用于数据链路层载荷（DLP0..5），再接下来 8 字节用于 CRC（CRC0..7），最后 6 字节用于 ECC（ecc 0..1）。FEC 采用 3 路交织 ECC，每路可纠正 1 字节错误，交织以 3 种颜色显示。

---

**Table 4-14 Flit arrangement in a x1 Link | 表 4-14 x1 链路中的 Flit 排列**

| Description | Lane 0 |
|---|---|
| 2048 UI TLP Bytes [0…235] | 0–235 |
| DLP Bytes | DLP0 |
| DLP Bytes | DLP1 |
| DLP Bytes | DLP2 |
| DLP Bytes | DLP3 |
| DLP Bytes | DLP4 |
| DLP Bytes | DLP5 |
| CRC Bytes | CRC0 |
| CRC Bytes | CRC1 |
| CRC Bytes | CRC2 |
| CRC Bytes | CRC3 |
| CRC Bytes | CRC4 |
| CRC Bytes | CRC5 |
| CRC Bytes | CRC6 |
| CRC Bytes | CRC7 |
| ECC Bytes | ECC1[0] |
| ECC Bytes | ECC2[0] |
| ECC Bytes | ECC0[0] |
| ECC Bytes | ECC1[1] |
| ECC Bytes | ECC2[1] |
| ECC Bytes | ECC0[1] |

**IMPLEMENTATION NOTE:** Note that the same set of Bytes form the same ECC group in the 3-way interleaved FEC as in a x16/x8/x4/x2 Link. The first 236 Bytes (0..235) are for TLP(s), next 6 Bytes are for Data Link Layer Payload (DLP0..5), next 8 Bytes for CRC (CRC0..7) and the last 6 Bytes are for ECC (ecc 0..1). The FEC is a 3-way interleaved ECC, each capable of correcting a single Byte, with the interleaving shown in 3 colors.

**实现注意事项：** 注意与 x16/x8/x4/x2 链路一样，相同的字节集合构成 3 路交织 FEC 中相同的 ECC 组。前 236 字节（0..235）用于 TLP，接下来 6 字节用于数据链路层载荷（DLP0..5），再接下来 8 字节用于 CRC（CRC0..7），最后 6 字节用于 ECC（ecc 0..1）。FEC 采用 3 路交织 ECC，每路可纠正 1 字节错误，交织以 3 种颜色显示。

<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

A conceptual representation of the processing of Symbols in the Flit Mode for the 64.0 GT/s Data Rate on the Transmit and the Receive side is demonstrated in § Figure 4-21, § Figure 4-22, and § Figure 4-25. A conceptual representation of the Flit Mode and Non-Flit Mode processing with 8b/10b encoding for 2.5 GT/s and 5.0 GT/s as well as with 128b/130b encoding for 8.0 GT/s, 16.0 GT/s, and 32.0 GT/s for the Transmit and Receive side is shown in and respectively.

**Figure 4-27 Flit Mode and Non-Flit Mode processing with 8b/10b and 128b/130b encoding on the Transmit side**

> **Figure 4-27 Flit Mode and Non-Flit Mode processing with 8b/10b and 128b/130b encoding on the Transmit side**
> <img src="figures/chapter_04/fig_0399_1_tight.png" width="700">

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

在发送端和接收端，64.0 GT/s 数据速率下 Flit 模式中符号处理的概念性表示见 § 图 4-21、§ 图 4-22 和 § 图 4-25。发送端和接收端在 2.5 GT/s 和 5.0 GT/s 下使用 8b/10b 编码、在 8.0 GT/s、16.0 GT/s 和 32.0 GT/s 下使用 128b/130b 编码时，Flit 模式与非 Flit 模式处理的概念性表示分别示于（发送）和（接收）。

**图 4-27 发送端 8b/10b 与 128b/130b 编码下的 Flit 模式与非 Flit 模式处理**


</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>

**Figure 4-28 Flit Mode and Non-Flit Mode processing with 8b/10b and 128b/130b encoding on the Receive side**

> **Figure 4-28 Flit Mode and Non-Flit Mode processing with 8b/10b and 128b/130b encoding on the Receive side**
> <img src="figures/chapter_04/fig_0400_1_tight.png" width="700">

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

**图 4-28 接收端 8b/10b 与 128b/130b 编码下的 Flit 模式与非 Flit 模式处理**


</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>

The TLP Bytes in the flit carry Transaction Layer TLPs. Since the Flit Mode does not support STP tokens, these TLP bytes must be populated by the Transaction Layer, irrespective of whether it has a TLP to send or not. A TLP may span across multiple flits depending on its length and placement. The following rules must be observed:

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

Flit 中的 TLP 字节承载事务层 TLP。由于 Flit 模式不支持 STP（Start of TLP）令牌，无论是否有 TLP 待发送，事务层都必须填充这些 TLP 字节。一个 TLP 可能会根据其长度与位置跨越多个 Flit。必须遵守以下规则：

</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>

- When the transaction layer does not have a TLP to send, it sends a NOP TLP (1DW). Once a NOP TLP is scheduled to be sent, NOP TLPs must be scheduled till the next 4DW aligned boundary within the Flit or the Flit boundary, whichever is earlier.
- NOP TLPs do not consume any credits.
- The TLPs in Flit Mode have information in predetermined position to determine the length of the TLPs, including the case where TLP prefix is used. See § Chapter 2. for details.
- The following rules apply for the non-NOP TLPs in bytes 0 through 127 of the Flit (i.e., the TLP bytes within the first Flit half) and bytes 128 through 235 of the Flit (i.e., the TLP bytes within the second Flit half):
  - No more than 8 TLPs, including partial TLPs.
    Receivers are permitted to check this rule.
    - If checked, this is logged as a Data Link Protocol Error in the receiving Port (see § Section 6.2).
- If a TLP at the end of a Flit gets poisoned or nullified through Flit_Status, and if that TLP extends to subsequent Flits, each of those Flits must also be poisoned or nullified through Flit_Status so that the poisoned or nullified Flit_Status is set in the Flit where the TLP ends. A Receiver is permitted to only look at the poison bit in the Flit where the TLP ends.
- A TLP that gets nullified through the Flit_Status field in the DLP Bytes described below is ignored by the Receiver if the Flit is valid, but the credits must be released.
- A TLP that gets EDB'ed or Poisoned through the Flit_Status field in the DLP Bytes described below must be succeeded by only NOP TLPs through the end of the Flit.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

- 当事务层没有 TLP 待发送时，它发送一个 NOP TLP（1DW）。一旦计划发送 NOP TLP，则必须持续安排 NOP TLP 直至 Flit 内下一个 4DW 对齐边界或 Flit 边界，以先到者为准。
- NOP TLP 不消耗任何信用（Credit）。
- Flit 模式中的 TLP 在预定位置包含用于确定 TLP 长度（包括使用 TLP 前缀的情况）的信息。详见 § 第 2 章。
- 以下规则适用于 Flit 的字节 0 至 127（即第一个 Flit 半部分内的 TLP 字节）以及字节 128 至 235（即第二个 Flit 半部分内的 TLP 字节）中的非 NOP TLP：
  - 包括部分 TLP 在内，TLP 总数不超过 8 个。
    允许接收器检查此规则。
    - 如果检查，记录为接收端口中的数据链路协议错误（Data Link Protocol Error）（见 § 第 6.2 节）。
- 如果 Flit 末尾的 TLP 通过 Flit_Status 被标记为中毒（Poisoned）或作废（Nullified），且该 TLP 延伸到后续 Flit，则这些后续 Flit 也必须通过 Flit_Status 标记为中毒或作废，以使中毒或作废的 Flit_Status 设置在 TLP 结束的 Flit 中。允许接收器仅查看 TLP 结束的 Flit 中的中毒位（Poison bit）。
- 如果 TLP 通过下文所述 DLP 字节中的 Flit_Status 字段被作废（Nullified），则当 Flit 有效时，接收器忽略该 TLP，但必须释放相应的信用。
- 如果 TLP 通过下文所述 DLP 字节中的 Flit_Status 字段被标记为 EDB 或中毒（Poisoned），则在该 Flit 结束之前，其后必须仅接 NOP TLP。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

<a id="sec-4-2-3-4"></a>
## 4.2.3.4 Bytes in Flit Layout | Flit 布局中的字节

<a id="sec-4-2-3-4-1"></a>
## 4.2.3.4.1 TLP Bytes in Flit | Flit 中的 TLP 字节

<!-- 📄 Page 401 -->
---

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

**IMPLEMENTATION NOTE:**
**HANDLING TLPS SPANNING MULTIPLE FLITS**

When a TLP spans multiple Flits, it may be interrupted by SKPs, NOP Flits, transitions through Recovery and/or other unavoidable scenarios. Transmitters should attempt to avoid this interruption whenever possible for optimal bandwidth and latency and Receivers must be able to deal with scenarios where the TLP may be interrupted.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

**实现注意事项：**
**处理跨多个 Flit 的 TLP**

当一个 TLP 跨越多个 Flit 时，它可能会被 SKP（跳过有序集）、NOP Flit、Recovery 状态切换和/或其他不可避免的情况中断。为了获得最佳的带宽 (Bandwidth) 和延迟 (Latency)，发送器 (Transmitter) 应尽可能避免此类中断；而接收器 (Receiver) 必须能够处理 TLP 可能被中断的场景。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

---

<!-- 📄 Page 402 -->
---


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

**Example:** § Table 4-15 shows an example of TLP placement in the Flit Mode for a x16 Link. For narrower Link widths, similar arrangements will exist subject to the rules mentioned above.

- This flit starts with a continuation of the remaining 2 DWs from TLP 19 (the 3rd DW of Hdr and 1 DW of Data).
- TLP 20 (4DW Hdr and 1DW Data) immediately starts in Lane 8 and ends in Lane 11 in the following Symbol.
- Since the Transmitter has nothing to send, it sends NOP sending till the 4DW aligned boundary, which is in Lane 15.
- TLPs 22, and 23 are scheduled without any intervening NOP.
- After TLP 23, the Transmitter did not have anything to send and sends 7 NOPs, aligned to a 4DW boundary till TLP 24 is ready, which continues till the flit boundary for TLP Bytes.
- After that we have 6 Bytes of DLP, 8 Bytes of CRC covering the 236 Bytes of TLP and 6 Bytes of DLP in the flit.
- Then we have the 3 sets of interleaved ECC, 2 bytes each, covering the entire 256B flit.

It should be noted that every Byte of each TLP as well the DLP is covered by one of the 3 ECC groups and each TLP/DLP is a member of all 3 ECC groups, and as indicated in the color code.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

**示例：** § 表 4-15 展示了在 x16 链路 (Link) 的 Flit 模式 (Flit Mode) 中 TLP 放置的示例。对于更窄的链路宽度，类似的布局将根据上述规则存在。

- 该 Flit 起始于 TLP 19 剩余的 2 个 DW（包头 (Hdr) 的第 3 个 DW 和 1 个数据 DW）的延续。
- TLP 20（4 DW 包头 + 1 DW 数据）紧接着从通道 (Lane) 8 开始，并在下一个 Symbol 的通道 11 结束。
- 由于发送器没有内容可发送，它发送 NOP 直到 4 DW 对齐边界，即通道 15。
- TLP 22 和 TLP 23 在调度时没有插入任何 NOP。
- 在 TLP 23 之后，发送器没有内容可发送，于是发送 7 个 NOP，对齐到 4 DW 边界，直到 TLP 24 就绪为止，TLP 24 一直延续到 TLP 字节 (TLP Bytes) 的 Flit 边界。
- 之后，Flit 中包含 6 字节的 DLP、覆盖 236 字节 TLP 的 8 字节 CRC 以及 6 字节的 DLP。
- 然后是 3 组交织的 ECC，每组 2 字节，覆盖整个 256 字节的 Flit。

值得注意的是，每个 TLP 的每个字节以及 DLP 均被 3 个 ECC 组中的某一个所覆盖，每个 TLP/DLP 同时属于全部 3 个 ECC 组，如色码所示。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>


**Table 4-15 Example TLP Placement in Flit Mode on a x16 Link | 表 4-15 Flit 模式下 x16 链路的 TLP 放置示例**

| Description | Lane 0 | Lane 1 | Lane 2 | Lane 3 | Lane 4 | Lane 5 | Lane 6 | Lane 7 | Lane 8 | Lane 9 | Lane 10 | Lane 11 | Lane 12 | Lane 13 | Lane 14 | Lane 15 |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 128 UI | TLP Bytes [0…223] | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | |
| … TLP 19 → | H2 | H2 | H2 | H2 | D0 D0 D0 D0 | H0 H0 | H0 | H0 | H1 | H1 | H1 | H1 | | | | |
| ← TLP 20 … | H2 | H2 | H2 | H2 | H3 | H3 | H3 | H3 D0 D0 | D0 | D0 | | | | | | |
| … TLP 20 → | NOP | H0 | H0 H0 H0 | H1 | H1 | H1 | H1 | H2 | H2 | H2 | H2 | H3 | H3 | H3 | H3 | |
| ← TLP 21 … | D0 | D0 D0 D0 | D1 | D1 | D1 | D1 | | | | | | | | | | |
| … TLP 21 → | NOP | H0 | H0 H0 H0 | H1 | H1 | H1 | H1 | H2 | H2 | H2 | H2 | H0 | H0 | H0 | H0 | |
| ← TLP 22 → | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | |
| ← TLP 23 … | H1 | H1 | H1 | H1 | H2 | H2 | H2 | H2 | H3 | H3 | H3 | H3 | D0 | D0 | D0 | D0 |
| … TLP 23 … | D1 | D1 | D1 | D1 | D2 | D2 | D2 | D2 | D3 | D3 | D3 | D3 | D4 | D4 | D4 | D4 |
| … TLP 23 → | D5 | D5 | D5 | D5 | | | | | | | | | | | | |
| | NOP | NOP | H0 | H0 H0 H0 | H1 | H1 | H1 | H1 | H2 | H2 | H2 | H2 | D0 | D0 | D0 | D0 |
| ← TLP 24 … | D1 | D1 | D1 | D1 | D2 | D2 | D2 | D2 | D3 | D3 | D3 | D3 | D4 | D4 | D4 | D4 |
| … TLP 24 … | D5 | D5 | D5 | D5 | D6 | D6 | D6 | D6 | D7 | D7 | D7 | D7 | D8 | D8 | D8 | D8 |
| … TLP 24 … | D9 | D9 | D9 | D9 | D10 D10 D10 D10 | D11 D11 | D11 | D11 | D12 | D12 | D12 | D12 | | | | |

---

<!-- 📄 Page 403 -->
---

| Description | Lane 0 | Lane 1 | Lane 2 | Lane 3 | Lane 4 | Lane 5 | Lane 6 | Lane 7 | Lane 8 | Lane 9 | Lane 10 | Lane 11 | Lane 12 | Lane 13 | Lane 14 | Lane 15 |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| … TLP 24 … | D13 D13 D13 D13 | D14 D14 D14 D14 | D15 D15 | D15 | D15 | D16 | D16 | D16 | D16 | | | | | | | |
| TLP, DLP | D17 D17 D17 D17 | D18 D18 D18 D18 | D19 D19 | D19 | D19 | DLP 0 | DLP 1 | DLP 2 | DLP 3 | | | | | | | |
| … TLP 24 → | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | |
| ← DLP … | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | |
| DLP, CRC, ECC | DLP 4 | DLP 5 | CRC 0 | CRC 1 | CRC 2 | CRC 3 | CRC 4 | CRC 5 | CRC 6 | CRC 7 | ECC1 [0] | ECC2 [0] | ECC0 [0] | ECC1 [1] | ECC2 [1] | ECC0 [1] |
| | … DLP → | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | |
| | ← CRC → | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | |
| | ← ECC → | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | |

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

In every flit, 6 bytes are allotted to carry the information carried by the Data Link Layer Packets (DLLPs) described in § Chapter 3. While the DLP bytes are designed to reuse the DLLP mechanism and formats for the most part, some optimizations have been made to effectively carry the Ack/Nak flit along with an optimized credit release (Optimized_Update_FC, defined below) or a traditional DLLP. An encoding has been added in the DLP to provide Update_FC credits for PRH, PRD, and NPRH within one 32 bit payload of DLP. The following table summarizes the DLP encodings.

Three Flit Types have been defined in § Table 4-16, along with their intended usages. They all have the same CRC and FEC mechanism. The distinction is made for ease of reference based on the TLP and DLP Bytes they carry. An IDLE Flit uses the sequence number of 0 along with the appropriate Replay Command (00b) as described in § Table 4-16. IDLE Flits are only sent after the Control SKP Ordered Set following the SDS Ordered Set Sequence with 128b/130b and 1b/1b encoding. For 8b/10b encoding, an IDLE Flit will be sent at the conclusion of the TS2 Ordered Set (or after a SKP OS following the last TS2 Ordered Set, depending on the SKP insertion interval). With 8b/10b encoding, an Ordered Set vs. a Flit is distinguished the lack of a COM at the end of a SKP OS. IDLE Flits continue to be sent until a Flit other than an IDLE Flit must be sent. Devices that advertise infinite credits across all FC/VC are permitted to send NOP DLLP if there is no DLLP to be sent in dlp 2, 3, 4, and 5.

A valid Flit is one that passes the CRC check after performing the FEC correction, if required, and none of the ECC groups in the FEC are reporting an uncorrectable error (by ECC), as described in § Section 4.2.3.4.4, and the Flit Usage as well as the Flit Status fields do not have a Reserved encoding defined below.

An FEC-correctable Flit is a valid Flit that passes the CRC check after performing the FEC correction.

An FEC uncorrectable error is an error in a Flit that is detected by the CRC after FEC correction or 'uncorrectable error' reported by an ECC group in the FEC which may result in a NAK and a replay.

An invalid Flit is a Flit that is not a valid Flit.

A valid non-IDLE Flit is a valid Flit that is either a NOP Flit or a Payload Flit. All subsequent Flits after the first valid non-IDLE Flit are non-IDLE Flits while the Link is in L0.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

在每个 Flit 中，分配了 6 字节用于承载第 3 章所述数据链路层包 (DLLP, Data Link Layer Packet) 中携带的信息。虽然 DLP 字节在设计上大部分复用了 DLLP 的机制和格式，但进行了一些优化，以便有效地承载 Ack/Nak Flit 以及优化的信用 (Credit) 释放 (Optimized_Update_FC，下文定义) 或传统的 DLLP。在 DLP 中新增了一种编码 (Encoding)，用于在 DLP 的一个 32 位有效载荷内提供 PRH、PRD 和 NPRH 的 Update_FC 信用。如下表总结了 DLP 编码。

§ 表 4-16 定义了三种 Flit 类型及其预期用途。它们均使用相同的 CRC 和 FEC 机制。区分依据是它们承载的 TLP 和 DLP 字节，以便于引用。IDLE Flit 使用序列号 0 以及 § 表 4-16 所述的相应 Replay Command (00b)。IDLE Flit 仅在 SDS 有序集序列之后的控制 SKP 有序集之后发送，且使用 128b/130b 编码和 1b/1b 编码。对于 8b/10b 编码，IDLE Flit 将在 TS2 有序集结束时发送（或在最后一个 TS2 有序集之后的 SKP OS 之后发送，具体取决于 SKP 插入间隔）。在使用 8b/10b 编码时，有序集与 Flit 的区别在于 SKP OS 末尾是否存在 COM。IDLE Flit 会持续发送，直到必须发送非 IDLE Flit 为止。通告所有 FC/VC 拥有无限信用的设备，如果 dlp 2、3、4、5 中没有 DLLP 可发送，则允许发送 NOP DLLP。

有效 Flit 是指在执行 FEC 校正（如果需要）后通过 CRC 校验，并且 FEC 中的所有 ECC 组均未报告不可纠正错误（ECC 层面）的 Flit，如 § 第 4.2.3.4.4 节所述，且 Flit Usage 字段和 Flit Status 字段未使用下文定义的保留编码 (Reserved encoding)。

FEC 可纠正 Flit 是指在执行 FEC 校正后通过 CRC 校验的有效 Flit。

FEC 不可纠正错误是指在 FEC 校正后由 CRC 检测到的 Flit 错误，或 FEC 中某个 ECC 组报告的"不可纠正错误"，可能导致 NAK 和重放 (Replay)。

无效 Flit 是指不是有效 Flit 的 Flit。

有效非 IDLE Flit 是指 NOP Flit 或 Payload Flit 中的有效 Flit。当链路 (Link) 处于 L0 状态时，第一个有效非 IDLE Flit 之后的所有后续 Flit 都是非 IDLE Flit。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

<a id="sec-4-2-3-4-2"></a>
## 4.2.3.4.2 DLP Bytes in Flit | Flit 中的 DLP 字节

---

<!-- 📄 Page 404 -->
---

**Figure 4-29 DLP Byte to Bit Number Assignment | 图 4-29 DLP 字节到位号分配**

> **Figure 4-29.** DLP Byte to Bit Number Assignment
> <img src="figures/chapter_04/fig_0404_1_tight.png" width="700">

| Bit Range | DLP Byte |
|---|---|
| 00b 0–7 | DLP 5 |
| 8–15 | DLP 4 |
| 16–23 | DLP 3 |
| 24–31 | DLP 2 |
| 01b 0–7 | DLP 5 |
| 8–15 | DLP 4 |
| 16–23 | DLP 3 |
| 24–31 | DLP 2 |
| 32–39 | DLP 1 |
| 40–47 | DLP 0 |
| Others | ... |

**Figure 4-30 DLP Bit usage | 图 4-30 DLP 位使用**

> **Figure 4-30.** DLP Bit usage
> <img src="figures/chapter_04/fig_0404_2_tight.png" width="700">

| Bit Range | Field |
|---|---|
| 0–31 | DLLP Payload (DLLP 有效载荷) |
| 32–41 | Sequence Number (序列号) |
| 42–43 | Replay Command (重放命令) |
| 44 | Type of DLLP Payload (DLLP 有效载荷类型) |
| 45 | Prior Flit was Payload (前一个 Flit 是 Payload Flit) |
| 46–47 | Flit Usage (Flit 用途) |

**Table 4-16 Flit Types | 表 4-16 Flit 类型**

| Flit Type | TLP Bytes | DLP Bytes 0,1 | DLP Bytes 2..5 |
|---|---|---|---|
| **IDLE Flit** | Transmitters must place 00h in all 236 bytes. (发送器必须在全部 236 字节中放置 00h。) | Transmitters must place 00h in both bytes. (发送器必须在两个字节中均放置 00h。) | NOP2 DLLP (see § Figure 3-8) |
| **NOP Flit** | All 236 bytes are Reserved. (全部 236 字节保留。) | Flit Usage = 00b<br>Flit Sequence Number⁶⁰ = NEXT_TX_FLIT_SEQ_NUM - 1 if Replay Command is 00b<br>Other Fields = Any valid encoding | Any valid encoding |
| **Payload Flit** | TLP content, packed per rules described in § Section 4.2.3.4.1. A portion of at least one non-NOP TLP in the 236 Bytes (TLP 内容，按 § 第 4.2.3.4.1 节所述规则打包。在 236 字节中至少包含一个非 NOP TLP 的一部分) | Flit Usage = 01b<br>Flit Sequence Number != 00b if Replay Command is 00b<br>Other Fields = Any valid encoding | Any valid encoding |

**Table 4-17 DLP Bytes in the Flit | 表 4-17 Flit 中的 DLP 字节**

| Field | Location | Encoding |
|---|---|---|
| Flit Usage | DLP0: Bits 7:6 | **00b** IDLE Flit or NOP Flit<br>**01b** Payload Flit<br>**Others** Reserved |
| Prior Flit was Payload | DLP0: Bit 5 | **0b** Prior flit was a NOP Flit or IDLE Flit (前一个 Flit 是 NOP Flit 或 IDLE Flit)<br>**1b** Prior flit was a Payload Flit (前一个 Flit 是 Payload Flit) |
| Type of DLLP Payload | DLP0: Bit 4 | **0b** DLLP Payload in DLP 2..5 (DLP 2..5 中为 DLLP 有效载荷)<br>**1b** Optimized_Update_FC or Flit_Marker in DLP 2..5 (DLP 2..5 中为 Optimized_Update_FC 或 Flit_Marker) |
| Replay Command [1:0] | DLP0: Bits 3:2 | **00b** The Flit Sequence Number included in the Flit is an Explicit Flit Sequence Number of the transmitted Flit. (Flit 中包含的 Flit 序列号是所发送 Flit 的显式 Flit 序列号。)<br>**01b** Ack of Flit Sequence Number from Receiver. The included Flit Sequence Number indicates the Flit Sequence Number of the last valid Flit received. (来自接收器的 Flit 序列号确认。所包含的 Flit 序列号表示接收到的最后一个有效 Flit 的 Flit 序列号。)<br>**10b** Nak requesting a Replay of all unacknowledged Flits. The included Flit Sequence Number indicates the Flit Sequence Number of the last valid Flit received. (Nak 请求重放所有未确认的 Flit。所包含的 Flit 序列号表示接收到的最后一个有效 Flit 的 Flit 序列号。)<br>**11b** Nak requesting a Replay of a single Flit (Flit Sequence Number + 1). The included Flit Sequence Number indicates the Flit Sequence Number of the last valid Flit received. (Nak 请求重放单个 Flit（Flit 序列号 + 1）。所包含的 Flit 序列号表示接收到的最后一个有效 Flit 的 Flit 序列号。) |
| Flit Sequence Number [9:0] | { DLP0: Bits 1:0, DLP1: Bits 7:0 } | 10-bit sequence number applied to Flits. See Flit Sequence Number Rules. (应用于 Flit 的 10 位序列号。参见 Flit 序列号规则。)<br>DLP0[1:0] contains Flit Sequence Number[9:8] and DLP1[7:0] has Flit Sequence Number[7:0]. |
| DLLP Payload | { DLP2, DLP3, DLP4, DLP5 } Bits 31:0 | DLP2 is 31:24<br>DLP3 is 23:16<br>DLP4 is 15:8<br>DLP5 is 7:0<br>Type of DLLP Payload is 0b → Regular DLLP Payload (see § Chapter 3.)<br>Type of DLLP Payload is 1b and bit 31 = 0b → Optimized_Update_FC (see § Table 4-18)<br>Type of DLLP Payload is 1b and bit 31 = 1b → Flit_Marker (see § Table 4-19) |

> In the Table above, a Transmitter must not use the Reserved encodings. (在上表中，发送器不得使用保留编码。)

> 60. A NOP Flit does not consume a Flit Sequence Number. (NOP Flit 不消耗 Flit 序列号。)

**Figure 4-31 Optimized_Update_FC | 图 4-31 Optimized_Update_FC**

> **Figure 4-31.** Optimized_Update_FC
> <img src="figures/chapter_04/fig_0405_1_tight.png" width="700">

| Bit Range | Field |
|---|---|
| 0–11 | Shared Posted DataFC (共享 Posted 数据流控) |
| 12–19 | Shared Posted HdrFC (共享 Posted 包头流控) |
| 20–27 | Shared Non-Posted HdrFC (共享 Non-Posted 包头流控) |
| 28–30 | VC (虚通道) |
| 31 | Optimized Update FC Indicator (Optimized Update FC 指示) |

**Table 4-18 Optimized_Update_FC | 表 4-18 Optimized_Update_FC**

| Bit Location | Description |
|---|---|
| 31 | Optimized Update FC Indicator — Must be "0" |
| 30:28 | VC |
| 27:20 | Shared Non-Posted HdrFC – Shared credits on this VC (此 VC 上的共享信用) |
| 19:12 | Shared Posted HdrFC – Shared credits on this VC |
| 11:0 | Shared Posted DataFC – Shared credits on this VC |

<!-- 📄 Page 406 -->
---

**Figure 4-32 Flit_Marker | 图 4-32 Flit_Marker**

> **Figure 4-32.** Flit_Marker
> <img src="figures/chapter_04/fig_0406_1_tight.png" width="700">

| Bit Range | Field |
|---|---|
| 0–27 | **00b** Reserved<br>**01b** PTM Message contained in this Flit (此 Flit 中包含 PTM 消息)<br>**10b** Flit_Status<br>**11b** Flit_Marker Indicator |
| 28 | PTM Message contained in this Flit |
| 29–30 | Flit_Status |
| 31 | Flit_Marker Indicator |

**Table 4-19 Flit_Marker | 表 4-19 Flit_Marker**

| Bit Location | Description |
|---|---|
| 31 | Flit_Marker Indicator — Must be "1" |
| 30:29 | Flit_Status - Indicates the validity of the Last TLP in this Flit. Encoding is: (指示此 Flit 中最后一个 TLP 的有效性。编码为：)<br>**00b** No special information - No TLPs that end in this Flit are Nullified or Poisoned. (无特殊信息 - 在此 Flit 中结束的 TLP 均未被作废 (Nullified) 或中毒 (Poisoned)。)<br>TLPs that starts in this Flit and ends in a subsequent Flit are marked in that Flit. (在此 Flit 中开始并在后续 Flit 中结束的 TLP 在该 Flit 中标记。)<br>**01b** Last TLP Nullified - Last TLP ending in current Flit is Nullified (最后一个 TLP 作废 - 当前 Flit 中结束的最后一个 TLP 被作废)<br>This mechanism replaces the EDB Mechanism in Non-Flit Mode framing. (此机制替代了非 Flit 模式 (Non-Flit Mode) 帧中的 EDB 机制。)<br>**10b** Last TLP Poisoned - Last TLP ending in the current Flit is Poisoned (最后一个 TLP 中毒 - 当前 Flit 中结束的最后一个 TLP 被中毒)<br>See § Section 2.7.2 regarding how this mechanism relates to the Transaction Layer EP bit mechanism.<br>**11b** Reserved |
| 28 | PTM Message contained in this Flit - In Flit Mode, timestamps for Precision Time Measurement messages are measured at the Flit level rather than at the TLP level. This bit indicates that this Flit contains the last symbol of a non-nullified PTM Message. (在 Flit 模式下，精确定时测量 (Precision Time Measurement) 消息的时间戳在 Flit 级别而非 TLP 级别测量。该位指示此 Flit 包含非作废 PTM 消息的最后一个符号。)<br>Nullified PTM Messages do not set this bit. (作废的 PTM 消息不设置此位。)<br>Poisoned PTM Messages are not permitted. (不允许中毒的 PTM 消息。) |


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

For the DLLP Payload in the above table, the Optimized_Update_FC is used to transmit performance critical NPRH, PRH, and PRD credits in 4 Bytes. For debug as well as ease of use by debug tools such as Logic Analyzers, devices must send at least one DLLP every 10 μs with an Update_FC DLLP per VC with the scaled credit information. It is strongly recommended that a Transmitter cycles through the VCs with finite non-0 credits in the Optimized_Update_FC as long as there is a credit to be released in the corresponding VC.

Receivers are permitted to check for the following Flit errors:

- Reserved value of Flit Usage
- Reserved encoding of Flit_Status field in a Flit_Marker
- Invalid sequence number (see § Section 4.2.3.4.2.1.4).
- Violations of the TLP packing rules as defined in § Section 4.2.3.4.1

If checked, these Flit errors are logged as a Data Link Protocol Error in the receiving Port (see § Section 6.2).

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

对于上表中的 DLLP 有效载荷，Optimized_Update_FC 用于在 4 字节内传输性能关键的 NPRH、PRH 和 PRD 信用 (Credit)。为了调试以及方便逻辑分析仪等调试工具的使用，设备必须每 10 μs 至少发送一个 DLLP，并为每个 VC 发送一个包含缩放信用信息的 Update_FC DLLP。强烈建议发送器在 Optimized_Update_FC 中循环遍历具有有限非零信用的 VC，只要相应 VC 中有待释放的信用。

允许接收器 (Receiver) 检查以下 Flit 错误：

- Flit Usage 的保留值
- Flit_Marker 中 Flit_Status 字段的保留编码
- 无效的序列号 (参见 § 第 4.2.3.4.2.1.4 节)
- 违反 § 第 4.2.3.4.1 节定义的 TLP 打包规则

如果进行检查，这些 Flit 错误将作为数据链路协议错误记录在接收端口 (Port) 中（参见 § 第 6.2 节）。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>

<!-- 📄 Page 407 -->
---

<a id="sec-4-2-3-4-2-1"></a>
## 4.2.3.4.2.1 Flit Sequence Number and Retry Mechanism | Flit 序列号与重传机制

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

**Term Definitions**

**Explicit Sequence Number Flit:** A Payload Flit or NOP Flit with Replay Command 00b.

**Ack Flit:** A Flit with Replay Command 01b.

**Standard Nak Flit:** A Flit with Replay Command 10b.

**Selective Nak Flit:** A Flit with Replay Command 11b.

**Nak Flit:** A Flit with either Replay Command 10b or 11b.

**Standard Nak:** A Nak that requests a Replay of all unacknowledged Flits.

**Selective Nak:** A Nak that requests a Replay of a specific Flit.

**Standard Replay:** A Replay of all unacknowledged Flits in the TX Retry Buffer.

**Selective Replay:** A Replay of a specific Flit from the TX Retry Buffer.

**TX Retry Buffer:** The buffer which stores information for transmitted Flits until the Flit has been acknowledged by the Link partner.⁶¹

**RX Retry Buffer:** The buffer which stores information for received Flits until the Flit has been consumed by the Receiver.⁶²

**Nak Ignore Window:** A time window in which received Nak Flits are ignored for a specific Flit Sequence Number.
- The Nak Ignore Window is started when a Payload Explicit Sequence Number Flit is sent with Flit Sequence Number = NAK_IGNORE_FLIT_SEQ_NUM + 1 and NAK_IGNORE_FLIT_SEQ_NUM != 0.⁶³

**Flags and Counters**

**NEXT_TX_FLIT_SEQ_NUM:** 10-bit unsigned counter that tracks the Flit Sequence Number for transmitted Flits.
- Set to 001h in DL_Inactive state.
- Behavior defined in NEXT_TX_FLIT_SEQ_NUM Rules.
- Usage defined in § Section 4.2.3.4.2.1.2, § Section 4.2.3.4.2.1.3, § Section 4.2.3.4.2.1.4, and § Section 4.2.3.4.2.1.6.

**TX_ACKNAK_FLIT_SEQ_NUM:** Stores the 10-bit Sequence Number to be transmitted in the next Ack Flit or Nak Flit.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

**术语定义**

**显式序列号 Flit (Explicit Sequence Number Flit)：** Replay Command 为 00b 的 Payload Flit 或 NOP Flit。

**Ack Flit：** Replay Command 为 01b 的 Flit。

**标准 Nak Flit (Standard Nak Flit)：** Replay Command 为 10b 的 Flit。

**选择性 Nak Flit (Selective Nak Flit)：** Replay Command 为 11b 的 Flit。

**Nak Flit：** Replay Command 为 10b 或 11b 的 Flit。

**标准 Nak (Standard Nak)：** 请求重放所有未确认 Flit 的 Nak。

**选择性 Nak (Selective Nak)：** 请求重放特定 Flit 的 Nak。

**标准重放 (Standard Replay)：** 重放 TX Retry Buffer (发送重试缓冲) 中所有未确认的 Flit。

**选择性重放 (Selective Replay)：** 从 TX Retry Buffer 中重放特定的 Flit。

**TX Retry Buffer (发送重试缓冲)：** 用于存储已发送 Flit 信息的缓冲器，直到该 Flit 获得链路对端的确认。⁶¹

**RX Retry Buffer (接收重试缓冲)：** 用于存储已接收 Flit 信息的缓冲器，直到该 Flit 被接收器使用。⁶²

**Nak Ignore Window (Nak 忽略窗口)：** 在此时间窗口内，针对特定 Flit 序列号接收到的 Nak Flit 会被忽略。
- 当发送 Flit Sequence Number = NAK_IGNORE_FLIT_SEQ_NUM + 1 且 NAK_IGNORE_FLIT_SEQ_NUM != 0 的 Payload 显式序列号 Flit 时，启动 Nak 忽略窗口。⁶³

**标志与计数器**

**NEXT_TX_FLIT_SEQ_NUM：** 10 位无符号计数器，用于跟踪已发送 Flit 的 Flit 序列号。
- 在 DL_Inactive 状态下设置为 001h。
- 行为定义见 NEXT_TX_FLIT_SEQ_NUM 规则。
- 使用定义见 § 第 4.2.3.4.2.1.2 节、§ 第 4.2.3.4.2.1.3 节、§ 第 4.2.3.4.2.1.4 节及 § 第 4.2.3.4.2.1.6 节。

**TX_ACKNAK_FLIT_SEQ_NUM：** 存储下一个 Ack Flit 或 Nak Flit 中要发送的 10 位序列号。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

---

<!-- 📄 Page 408 -->
---


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

- Set to 1023 in DL_Inactive state.
- Usage defined in § Section 4.2.3.4.2.1.2, § Section 4.2.3.4.2.1.3, and § Section 4.2.3.4.2.1.5.

**CONSECUTIVE_TX_EXPLICIT_SEQ_NUM_FLITS:** 2-bit unsigned counter which tracks how many consecutive Explicit Sequence Number Flits have been sent by the Transmitter.
- Set to 00b IDLE Flit Handshake Phase.
- Behavior defined in CONSECUTIVE_TX_EXPLICIT_SEQ_NUM_FLITS Rules
- Usage defined in § Section 4.2.3.4.2.1.2 and § Section 4.2.3.4.2.1.3.

**CONSECUTIVE_TX_NAK_FLITS:** 3-bit unsigned counter which tracks how many consecutive Nak Flits have been sent by the Transmitter.
- Set to 000b in the IDLE Flit Handshake Phase.
- This counter saturates at 111b and does not roll over to 000b.
- Behavior defined in CONSECUTIVE_TX_NAK_FLITS Rules.
- Usage defined in § Section 4.2.3.4.2.1.3 and § Section 4.2.3.4.2.1.7.

**NEXT_EXPECTED_RX_FLIT_SEQ_NUM:** 10-bit unsigned counter that stores the expected Flit Sequence Number of the next valid non-IDLE non-duplicate Flit to be received.
- Set to 001h in DL_Inactive state.
- Behavior and usage defined in § Section 4.2.3.4.2.1.5.

**IMPLICIT_RX_FLIT_SEQ_NUM:** 10-bit unsigned counter that tracks the implicit Flit Sequence Number of received Flits.
- Set to 000h in DL_Inactive state.
- Behavior defined in IMPLICIT_RX_FLIT_SEQ_NUM Rules.
- Usage defined in § Section 4.2.3.4.2.1.5.

**ACKD_FLIT_SEQ_NUM:** Stores the 10-bit Flit Sequence Number received in the most recently processed valid Ack Flit or Nak Flit.
- Set to 3FFh in DL_Inactive state.
- Behavior defined in § Section 4.2.3.4.2.1.4.
- Usage defined in § Section 4.2.3.4.2.1.4 and § Section 4.2.3.4.2.1.6.

**NON_IDLE_EXPLICIT_SEQ_NUM_FLIT_RCVD:** Flag to indicate that a Non-IDLE Explicit Sequence Number Flit has been received since the last entry to IDLE Flit Handshake Phase.
- Set to 1 when first Non-IDLE Explicit Sequence Number Flit is received after last entry to IDLE Flit Handshake Phase.
  - This must be updated before evaluating Ack, Nak, and Discard Rules in § Section 4.2.3.4.2.1.5.
- Cleared on entry to IDLE Flit Handshake Phase.
- Usage defined in IMPLICIT_RX_FLIT_SEQ_NUM Rules.

**REPLAY_SCHEDULED:** Flag to indicate that a Flit has been scheduled to be replayed from the TX Retry Buffer.
- Cleared when in DL_Inactive state.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

- 在 DL_Inactive 状态下设置为 1023。
- 使用定义见 § 第 4.2.3.4.2.1.2 节、§ 第 4.2.3.4.2.1.3 节及 § 第 4.2.3.4.2.1.5 节。

**CONSECUTIVE_TX_EXPLICIT_SEQ_NUM_FLITS：** 2 位无符号计数器，用于跟踪发送器已发送的连续显式序列号 Flit 的数量。
- 在 IDLE Flit 握手阶段设置为 00b。
- 行为定义见 CONSECUTIVE_TX_EXPLICIT_SEQ_NUM_FLITS 规则。
- 使用定义见 § 第 4.2.3.4.2.1.2 节及 § 第 4.2.3.4.2.1.3 节。

**CONSECUTIVE_TX_NAK_FLITS：** 3 位无符号计数器，用于跟踪发送器已发送的连续 Nak Flit 的数量。
- 在 IDLE Flit 握手阶段设置为 000b。
- 此计数器饱和值为 111b，不会回绕至 000b。
- 行为定义见 CONSECUTIVE_TX_NAK_FLITS 规则。
- 使用定义见 § 第 4.2.3.4.2.1.3 节及 § 第 4.2.3.4.2.1.7 节。

**NEXT_EXPECTED_RX_FLIT_SEQ_NUM：** 10 位无符号计数器，存储下一个预期接收的有效非 IDLE 且非重复 Flit 的 Flit 序列号。
- 在 DL_Inactive 状态下设置为 001h。
- 行为与使用定义见 § 第 4.2.3.4.2.1.5 节。

**IMPLICIT_RX_FLIT_SEQ_NUM：** 10 位无符号计数器，用于跟踪已接收 Flit 的隐式 Flit 序列号。
- 在 DL_Inactive 状态下设置为 000h。
- 行为定义见 IMPLICIT_RX_FLIT_SEQ_NUM 规则。
- 使用定义见 § 第 4.2.3.4.2.1.5 节。

**ACKD_FLIT_SEQ_NUM：** 存储最近处理的有效 Ack Flit 或 Nak Flit 中接收到的 10 位 Flit 序列号。
- 在 DL_Inactive 状态下设置为 3FFh。
- 行为定义见 § 第 4.2.3.4.2.1.4 节。
- 使用定义见 § 第 4.2.3.4.2.1.4 节及 § 第 4.2.3.4.2.1.6 节。

**NON_IDLE_EXPLICIT_SEQ_NUM_FLIT_RCVD：** 标志，指示自上次进入 IDLE Flit 握手阶段以来是否已接收到非 IDLE 显式序列号 Flit。
- 在 IDLE Flit 握手阶段后接收到第一个非 IDLE 显式序列号 Flit 时设置为 1。
  - 在评估 § 第 4.2.3.4.2.1.5 节中的 Ack、Nak 和 Discard 规则之前必须更新此标志。
- 在进入 IDLE Flit 握手阶段时清除。
- 使用定义见 IMPLICIT_RX_FLIT_SEQ_NUM 规则。

**REPLAY_SCHEDULED：** 标志，指示已调度从 TX Retry Buffer 重放一个 Flit。
- 在 DL_Inactive 状态下清除。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>

<!-- 📄 Page 409 -->
---

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

- Behavior defined in § Section 4.2.3.4.2.1.6.
- Usage defined in § Section 4.2.3.4.2.1.2, § Section 4.2.3.4.2.1.6, and § Section 4.2.3.4.2.1.7.

**REPLAY_SCHEDULED_TYPE:** Indicates whether a Standard Replay or a Selective Replay has been scheduled.
- If REPLAY_SCHEDULED is 1b:
  - REPLAY_SCHEDULED_TYPE 0b indicates a Standard Replay.
    - REPLAY_SCHEDULED_TYPE of 0b will be referred to as STANDARD_REPLAY.
  - REPLAY_SCHEDULED_TYPE 1b indicates a Selective Replay.
    - REPLAY_SCHEDULED_TYPE of 1b will be referred to as SELECTIVE_REPLAY.
- Behavior defined in § Section 4.2.3.4.2.1.6.
- Usage defined in § Section 4.2.3.4.2.1.6 and § Section 4.2.3.4.2.1.7.

**REPLAY_IN_PROGRESS:** Flag to indicate that the Transmitter is sending Flits from the TX Retry Buffer.
- Cleared when in DL_Inactive state.
- Behavior defined in REPLAY_IN_PROGRESS Rules.
- Usage defined in § Section 4.2.3.4.2.1.2, § Section 4.2.3.4.2.1.6, and § Section 4.2.3.4.2.1.7.

**TX_REPLAY_FLIT_SEQ_NUM:** Stores the 10-bit Flit Sequence Number of the first Flit to be replayed from the TX Retry Buffer.
- Set to 000h in DL_Inactive state.
- Behavior defined in § Section 4.2.3.4.2.1.6.
- Usage defined in § Section 4.2.3.4.2.1.4, § Section 4.2.3.4.2.1.6, and § Section 4.2.3.4.2.1.7.

**FLIT_REPLAY_NUM:** 3-bit unsigned counter that tracks the number of times that a Replay has been initiated without making forward progress.
- Set to 000b in DL_Inactive state.
- Behavior defined in § Section 4.2.3.4.2.1.4 and § Section 4.2.3.4.2.1.7.
- Usage defined in § Section 4.2.3.4.2.1.7.

**REPLAY_TIMEOUT_FLIT_COUNT:** 11-bit unsigned counter that counts the number of Payload Flits and NOP Flits sent since the last Ack of an outstanding Flit.
- Set to 000h in DL_Inactive state.
- This counter saturates at 7FFh and does not roll over to 000h.
- Behavior defined in REPLAY_TIMEOUT_FLIT_COUNT Rules
- Usage defined in § Section 4.2.3.4.2.1.6.

**NAK_SCHEDULED:** Flag to indicate that a Nak Flit has been scheduled for transmission.
- Cleared when in DL_Inactive state.
- Behavior defined in § Section 4.2.3.4.2.1.5.
- Usage defined in § Section 4.2.3.4.2.1.2, § Section 4.2.3.4.2.1.3, and § Section 4.2.3.4.2.1.5.

**NAK_SCHEDULED_TYPE:** Indicates whether a Standard Nak Flit or a Selective Nak Flit should be sent.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

- 行为定义见 § 第 4.2.3.4.2.1.6 节。
- 使用定义见 § 第 4.2.3.4.2.1.2 节、§ 第 4.2.3.4.2.1.6 节及 § 第 4.2.3.4.2.1.7 节。

**REPLAY_SCHEDULED_TYPE：** 指示已调度的是标准重放 (Standard Replay) 还是选择性重放 (Selective Replay)。
- 如果 REPLAY_SCHEDULED 为 1b：
  - REPLAY_SCHEDULED_TYPE 0b 指示标准重放。
    - REPLAY_SCHEDULED_TYPE 为 0b 时将称为 STANDARD_REPLAY。
  - REPLAY_SCHEDULED_TYPE 1b 指示选择性重放。
    - REPLAY_SCHEDULED_TYPE 为 1b 时将称为 SELECTIVE_REPLAY。
- 行为定义见 § 第 4.2.3.4.2.1.6 节。
- 使用定义见 § 第 4.2.3.4.2.1.6 节及 § 第 4.2.3.4.2.1.7 节。

**REPLAY_IN_PROGRESS：** 标志，指示发送器正在从 TX Retry Buffer 发送 Flit。
- 在 DL_Inactive 状态下清除。
- 行为定义见 REPLAY_IN_PROGRESS 规则。
- 使用定义见 § 第 4.2.3.4.2.1.2 节、§ 第 4.2.3.4.2.1.6 节及 § 第 4.2.3.4.2.1.7 节。

**TX_REPLAY_FLIT_SEQ_NUM：** 存储从 TX Retry Buffer 重放的第一个 Flit 的 10 位 Flit 序列号。
- 在 DL_Inactive 状态下设置为 000h。
- 行为定义见 § 第 4.2.3.4.2.1.6 节。
- 使用定义见 § 第 4.2.3.4.2.1.4 节、§ 第 4.2.3.4.2.1.6 节及 § 第 4.2.3.4.2.1.7 节。

**FLIT_REPLAY_NUM：** 3 位无符号计数器，用于跟踪在未取得前向进展的情况下启动重放的次数。
- 在 DL_Inactive 状态下设置为 000b。
- 行为定义见 § 第 4.2.3.4.2.1.4 节及 § 第 4.2.3.4.2.1.7 节。
- 使用定义见 § 第 4.2.3.4.2.1.7 节。

**REPLAY_TIMEOUT_FLIT_COUNT：** 11 位无符号计数器，对自上次确认未完成 Flit 以来已发送的 Payload Flit 和 NOP Flit 的数量进行计数。
- 在 DL_Inactive 状态下设置为 000h。
- 此计数器饱和值为 7FFh，不会回绕至 000h。
- 行为定义见 REPLAY_TIMEOUT_FLIT_COUNT 规则。
- 使用定义见 § 第 4.2.3.4.2.1.6 节。

**NAK_SCHEDULED：** 标志，指示已调度一个 Nak Flit 用于发送。
- 在 DL_Inactive 状态下清除。
- 行为定义见 § 第 4.2.3.4.2.1.5 节。
- 使用定义见 § 第 4.2.3.4.2.1.2 节、§ 第 4.2.3.4.2.1.3 节及 § 第 4.2.3.4.2.1.5 节。

**NAK_SCHEDULED_TYPE：** 指示应发送标准 Nak Flit 还是选择性 Nak Flit。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

<!-- 📄 Page 410 -->
---


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

- Set to 0b when in DL_Inactive state.
- If NAK_SCHEDULED is 1b,
  - NAK_SCHEDULED_TYPE 0b indicates a Standard Nak Flit.
    - NAK_SCHEDULED_TYPE of 0b will be referred to as STANDARD_NAK.
  - NAK_SCHEDULED_TYPE 1b indicates a Selective Nak Flit.
    - NAK_SCHEDULED_TYPE of 1b will be referred to as SELECTIVE_NAK.
- Behavior defined in § Section 4.2.3.4.2.1.5.
- Usage defined in § Section 4.2.3.4.2.1.2, § Section 4.2.3.4.2.1.3, and § Section 4.2.3.4.2.1.5.

**NAK_WITHDRAWAL_ALLOWED:** Flag that indicates an invalid Flit was received but should not be Nak'd if the subsequent received Flit has Prior Flit was Payload set to 0b (indicating that the invalid Flit was a NOP Flit).
- Cleared when in DL_Inactive state.
- Behavior and usage defined in § Section 4.2.3.4.2.1.5.

**NAK_IGNORE_FLIT_SEQ_NUM:** Stores the 10-bit Flit Sequence Number to be ignored during the Nak Ignore Window.
- Set to 000h when in DL_Inactive state.
- Behavior defined in § Section 4.2.3.4.2.1.4 and § Section 4.2.3.4.2.1.6
- Usage defined in § Section 4.2.3.4.2.1.6.

**NEXT_RX_FLIT_SEQ_NUM_TO_STORE:** Stores the 10-bit Flit Sequence Number of the next Flit to be stored in the RX Retry Buffer.
- Cleared when in DL_Inactive state.
- Behavior and usage defined in § Section 4.2.3.4.2.1.5.

**RX_RETRY_BUFFER_OVERFLOW:** Flag to indicate that a Flit was unable to be stored in the RX Retry Buffer due to the buffer becoming full before a Selective Replay was received for an outstanding Selective Nak.
- Cleared when in DL_Inactive state.
- Behavior and usage defined in § Section 4.2.3.4.2.1.5.

**RX_RETRY_BUFFER_LAST_FLIT_SEQ_NUM:** Stores the 10-bit Flit Sequence Number of the last Flit stored in the RX Retry Buffer if the RX Retry Buffer overflows.
- Cleared when in DL_Inactive state.
- Behavior and usage defined in § Section 4.2.3.4.2.1.5.

**MAX_UNACKNOWLEDGED_FLITS:** Maximum number of unacknowledged Flits outstanding.
- Set to the lesser of:
  - Number of Flits that can be stored in the TX Retry Buffer.
  - 511 Flits
- Usage defined in § Section 4.2.3.4.2.1.4, § Section 4.2.3.4.2.1.5, and § Section 4.2.3.4.2.1.6.

**General Rules**

**Flit Sequence Number Rules:**

- The valid range of Flit Sequence Numbers for Payload Flits is 1-1023.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

- 在 DL_Inactive 状态下设置为 0b。
- 如果 NAK_SCHEDULED 为 1b：
  - NAK_SCHEDULED_TYPE 0b 指示标准 Nak Flit。
    - NAK_SCHEDULED_TYPE 为 0b 时将称为 STANDARD_NAK。
  - NAK_SCHEDULED_TYPE 1b 指示选择性 Nak Flit。
    - NAK_SCHEDULED_TYPE 为 1b 时将称为 SELECTIVE_NAK。
- 行为定义见 § 第 4.2.3.4.2.1.5 节。
- 使用定义见 § 第 4.2.3.4.2.1.2 节、§ 第 4.2.3.4.2.1.3 节及 § 第 4.2.3.4.2.1.5 节。

**NAK_WITHDRAWAL_ALLOWED：** 标志，指示已收到无效 Flit，但如果后续收到的 Flit 的"前一个 Flit 是 Payload Flit"字段为 0b（指示该无效 Flit 是 NOP Flit），则不应发起 Nak。
- 在 DL_Inactive 状态下清除。
- 行为与使用定义见 § 第 4.2.3.4.2.1.5 节。

**NAK_IGNORE_FLIT_SEQ_NUM：** 存储在 Nak 忽略窗口期间应忽略的 10 位 Flit 序列号。
- 在 DL_Inactive 状态下设置为 000h。
- 行为定义见 § 第 4.2.3.4.2.1.4 节及 § 第 4.2.3.4.2.1.6 节。
- 使用定义见 § 第 4.2.3.4.2.1.6 节。

**NEXT_RX_FLIT_SEQ_NUM_TO_STORE：** 存储下一个要存储到 RX Retry Buffer 中的 Flit 的 10 位 Flit 序列号。
- 在 DL_Inactive 状态下清除。
- 行为与使用定义见 § 第 4.2.3.4.2.1.5 节。

**RX_RETRY_BUFFER_OVERFLOW：** 标志，指示由于在收到未完成的选择性 Nak 的选择性重放之前缓冲器已满，导致 Flit 无法存储到 RX Retry Buffer。
- 在 DL_Inactive 状态下清除。
- 行为与使用定义见 § 第 4.2.3.4.2.1.5 节。

**RX_RETRY_BUFFER_LAST_FLIT_SEQ_NUM：** 如果 RX Retry Buffer 溢出，存储最后存入 RX Retry Buffer 的 Flit 的 10 位 Flit 序列号。
- 在 DL_Inactive 状态下清除。
- 行为与使用定义见 § 第 4.2.3.4.2.1.5 节。

**MAX_UNACKNOWLEDGED_FLITS：** 允许存在的最大未确认 Flit 数。
- 设置为以下两者的较小值：
  - TX Retry Buffer 可存储的 Flit 数量。
  - 511 个 Flit。
- 使用定义见 § 第 4.2.3.4.2.1.4 节、§ 第 4.2.3.4.2.1.5 节及 § 第 4.2.3.4.2.1.6 节。

**通用规则**

**Flit 序列号规则：**

- Payload Flit 的 Flit 序列号有效范围为 1–1023。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>


---

<!-- 📄 Page 411 -->
---

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

- Flit Sequence Number 0 is only used for IDLE Flits.
- NOP Flits do not consume a Flit Sequence Number.
  - A NOP Flit that is also an Explicit Sequence Number Flit sets Flit Sequence Number to NEXT_TX_FLIT_SEQ_NUM - 1.
    - If no Payload Flits have been transmitted, Flit Sequence Number 1023 is used.
- All Flit Sequence Number counters will roll-over from 1023 to 1.
- Whenever text in this section refers to adding to, subtracting from, incrementing, or decrementing a sequence number, it is implied that sequence number rollover rules stated above will be followed.
- Two transmitted or received Flits are considered consecutive, even if they are separated by a SKP OS.
- The Nak Ignore Window should be the lesser of:
  - Measured Ack/Nak Latency of the Link + 1 maximum sized SKP Ordered Set + 2 Flits
  - 300ns
- It is strongly recommended that an Ack or Nak for a received Flit is transmitted within the following times of receiving the Flit on the receiver pins. The following times apply unless the transmitter is sending an Ordered Set. The measurement is from the first bit of the received Flit on the receiver pins to the first bit of the associated Ack Flit or Nak Flit on the transmitter pins. § Section 4.2.3.4.2.1.5 describes when an Ack or Nak should be scheduled.
  - x16: 50ns
  - x8: 52ns
  - x4: 56ns
  - x2: 64ns
  - x1: 80ns

**Transmitter Rules**

- The Transmitter must store the following information of transmitted Payload Flits in the TX Retry Buffer:
  - TLP Bytes associated with non-NOP TLPs
  - Flit Status
  - Flit Sequence Number

**MAX_UNACKNOWLEDGED_FLITS Outstanding:**

- If (NEXT_TX_FLIT_SEQ_NUM - ACKD_FLIT_SEQ_NUM) mod 1023 > MAX_UNACKNOWLEDGED_FLITS:
  - The Transmitter must not accept any new TLPs from the Transaction Layer.
  - The Transmitter must send a NOP Flit if REPLAY_SCHEDULED is 0b and REPLAY_IN_PROGRESS is 0b.

**REPLAY_IN_PROGRESS Rules:**

- If REPLAY_IN_PROGRESS is 1b:
  - The Transmitter must not accept any new TLPs from the Transaction Layer.
  - REPLAY_IN_PROGRESS is cleared if either of the following conditions are true:
    - REPLAY_SCHEDULED_TYPE is STANDARD_REPLAY and all unacknowledged Flits in the TX Retry Buffer have been transmitted.
    - REPLAY_SCHEDULED_TYPE is SELECTIVE_REPLAY and Flit with Flit Sequence Number equal to TX_REPLAY_FLIT_SEQ_NUM has been transmitted.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

- Flit 序列号 0 仅用于 IDLE Flit。
- NOP Flit 不消耗 Flit 序列号。
  - 同时也是显式序列号 Flit 的 NOP Flit 将 Flit 序列号设置为 NEXT_TX_FLIT_SEQ_NUM - 1。
    - 如果尚未发送任何 Payload Flit，则使用 Flit 序列号 1023。
- 所有 Flit 序列号计数器将从 1023 回绕至 1。
- 当本节文本涉及对序列号进行加、减、递增或递减操作时，意味着将遵循上述序列号回绕规则。
- 即使两个已发送或已接收的 Flit 之间被 SKP OS 分隔，它们仍被视为连续的。
- Nak 忽略窗口应取以下两者的较小值：
  - 测量得到的链路 Ack/Nak 延迟 + 1 个最大尺寸的 SKP 有序集 + 2 个 Flit
  - 300 ns
- 强烈建议对已接收 Flit 的 Ack 或 Nak 在以下时间内从接收器引脚接收 Flit 之后发送。除非发送器正在发送有序集，否则以下时间适用。测量从接收器引脚上接收 Flit 的第一位开始，到发送器引脚上相关 Ack Flit 或 Nak Flit 的第一位结束。§ 第 4.2.3.4.2.1.5 节描述了何时应调度 Ack 或 Nak。
  - x16: 50 ns
  - x8: 52 ns
  - x4: 56 ns
  - x2: 64 ns
  - x1: 80 ns

**发送器规则**

- 发送器必须将已发送 Payload Flit 的以下信息存储到 TX Retry Buffer 中：
  - 与非 NOP TLP 关联的 TLP 字节 (TLP Bytes)
  - Flit Status
  - Flit 序列号

**MAX_UNACKNOWLEDGED_FLITS 超限：**

- 如果 (NEXT_TX_FLIT_SEQ_NUM - ACKD_FLIT_SEQ_NUM) mod 1023 > MAX_UNACKNOWLEDGED_FLITS：
  - 发送器不得从事务层 (Transaction Layer) 接受任何新的 TLP。
  - 如果 REPLAY_SCHEDULED 为 0b 且 REPLAY_IN_PROGRESS 为 0b，则发送器必须发送一个 NOP Flit。

**REPLAY_IN_PROGRESS 规则：**

- 如果 REPLAY_IN_PROGRESS 为 1b：
  - 发送器不得从事务层接受任何新的 TLP。
  - 在以下任一条件成立时清除 REPLAY_IN_PROGRESS：
    - REPLAY_SCHEDULED_TYPE 为 STANDARD_REPLAY，且 TX Retry Buffer 中所有未确认的 Flit 都已发送。
    - REPLAY_SCHEDULED_TYPE 为 SELECTIVE_REPLAY，且 Flit 序列号等于 TX_REPLAY_FLIT_SEQ_NUM 的 Flit 已发送。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
