<a id="sec-4-2-5-7"></a>
## 4.2.5.7 Start of Data Stream Ordered Set (SDS Ordered Set) | 数据流起始有序集 (SDS 有序集)


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

The Start of Data Stream (SDS) Ordered Set, described in § Table 4-41, § Table 4-42, and § Table 4-43 is defined only for 128b/130b encoding and 1b/1b encoding. It is transmitted in the Configuration.Idle, Recovery.Idle, and Tx_L0s.FTS LTSSM states to define the transition from Ordered Set Blocks to a Data Stream, and Loopback Leads are permitted to transmit it as described in § Section 4.2.2.6. It must not be transmitted at any other time. While not in the Loopback state, the Block following an SDS Ordered Set must be a Data Block in Non-Flit Mode, and the first Symbol of that Data Block is the first Symbol of the Data Stream. In 1b/1b encoding, the Transmitter must send two back to back SDS when the conditions to send an SDS are met. The SDS Ordered Set in 1b/1b encoding must be on an aligned 128b (16B) boundary. An SDS Ordered Set sequence refers to either the single SDS Ordered set with 128b/130b encoding or the two back to back SDS Ordered Sets with 1b/1b encoding. In Flit Mode, with 128b/130b encoding and 1b/1b encoding, a Control SKP Ordered Set must be sent after the SDS Ordered Set. The first Symbol of the Data Stream starts immediately after the Control SKP Ordered Set. With 1b/1b encoding, a Receiver considers an SDS sequence valid if four good B1_C6_C6_C6 (4B) sets are received, at least two of which are in an even 4 byte aligned position (i.e., bytes 0-3, 8-11).

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

数据流起始 (Start of Data Stream, SDS) 有序集（详见 § 表 4-41、§ 表 4-42 和 § 表 4-43）仅针对 128b/130b 编码 (Encoding) 和 1b/1b 编码定义。它在 Configuration.Idle、Recovery.Idle 和 Tx_L0s.FTS 等 LTSSM 状态中发送，用于标识从有序集块 (Ordered Set Block) 向数据流 (Data Stream) 的过渡，Loopback 主导端 (Loopback Lead) 可按 § 第 4.2.2.6 节所述发送该有序集。除此之外的其他任何时刻均不得发送。在非 Loopback 状态下，SDS 有序集之后的块必须是非 Flit 模式 (Non-Flit Mode) 下的数据块 (Data Block)，该数据块的第一个 Symbol 即为数据流的第一个 Symbol。在 1b/1b 编码下，当满足发送 SDS 的条件时，发送器 (Transmitter) 必须发送两个背靠背的 SDS。1b/1b 编码下的 SDS 有序集必须位于对齐的 128b (16B) 边界上。SDS 有序集序列 (SDS Ordered Set sequence) 是指 128b/130b 编码下的单个 SDS 有序集，或 1b/1b 编码下的两个背靠背 SDS 有序集。在 Flit 模式 (Flit Mode) 下，无论采用 128b/130b 还是 1b/1b 编码，都必须在 SDS 有序集之后发送一个控制 SKP (Control SKP) 有序集。数据流的第一个 Symbol 在控制 SKP 有序集之后立即开始。在 1b/1b 编码下，接收器 (Receiver) 在接收到四个良好的 B1_C6_C6_C6 (4B) 序列（其中至少两个位于偶数 4 字节对齐位置，即字节 0-3、8-11）时，即认为该 SDS 序列有效。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>


**Table 4-41 SDS Ordered Set (for 8.0 GT/s and 16.0 GT/s Data Rate) | 表 4-41 SDS 有序集 (适用于 8.0 GT/s 和 16.0 GT/s 数据速率)**

| Symbol Number | Value | Description |
|---------------|-------|-------------|
| 0 | E1h | SDS Ordered Set Identifier |
| 1-15 | 55h | Body of SDS Ordered Set |

**Table 4-42 SDS Ordered Set (for 32.0 GT/s) | 表 4-42 SDS 有序集 (适用于 32.0 GT/s)**

| Symbol Number | Value | Description |
|---------------|-------|-------------|
| 0 | E1h | SDS Ordered Set Identifier |
| 1-15 | 87h | Body of SDS Ordered Set |

**Table 4-43 SDS Ordered Set (for 64.0 GT/s) | 表 4-43 SDS 有序集 (适用于 64.0 GT/s)**

| Symbol Number | Value | Description |
|---------------|-------|-------------|
| 0, 4, 8, 12 | B1h | SDS Ordered Set Identifier |
| 1-3, 5-7, 9-11, 13-15 | C6h | Body of SDS Ordered Set |

<a id="sec-4-2-5-8"></a>
## 4.2.5.8 Link Error Recovery | 链路错误恢复

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

- Link Errors, when operating with 8b/10b encoding are:
  - 8b/10b decode errors, Non-Flit Mode framing related errors defined in § Section 4.2.1.2.1, loss of Symbol lock, Elasticity Buffer Overflow/Underflow, or loss of Lane-to-Lane de-skew.
  - 8b/10b decode errors must be checked and must trigger a Receiver Error in specified LTSSM states (see § Table 4-48), which is a reported error associated with the Port (see § Section 6.2). Triggering a

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

- 在使用 8b/10b 编码 (Encoding) 时的链路错误 (Link Error) 包括：
  - 8b/10b 解码错误、§ 第 4.2.1.2.1 节中定义的非 Flit 模式 (Non-Flit Mode) 帧相关错误、Symbol 锁定丢失、弹性缓冲区 (Elasticity Buffer) 上溢/下溢或通道间去偏斜 (Lane-to-Lane De-skew) 丢失。
  - 必须检查 8b/10b 解码错误，并在指定的 LTSSM 状态（见 § 表 4-48）下触发接收器错误 (Receiver Error)，这是一项与端口 (Port) 关联的已报告错误 (参见 § 第 6.2 节)。是否针对 § 第 4.2.1.2.1 节中定义的

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

---

<!-- 📄 Page 485 -->
---


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

Receiver Error on any or all of Non-Flit Mode framing related errors defined in § Section 4.2.1.2.1, loss of Symbol Lock, Lane De-skew Error, and Elasticity Buffer Overflow/Underflow is optional.

- Link Errors, when operating with 128b/130b encoding, are:
  - Framing Errors, loss of Block Alignment, Elasticity Buffer Overflow/Underflow, or loss of Lane-to-Lane de-skew.
  - Framing errors must be checked and trigger a Receiver Error in the LTSSM states specified in § Table 4-48. The Receiver Error is a reported error associated with the Port (see § Section 6.2). Triggering a Receiver Error on any of all of loss of Block Alignment, Elasticity Buffer Overflow/Underflow, and loss of Lane-to-Lane de-skew is optional.

- Link Errors, when operating with 1b/1b encoding, are:
  - Framing Errors, Elasticity Buffer Overflow/Underflow, or loss of Lane-to-Lane de-skew.
  - Framing errors must be checked and trigger a Receiver Error in the LTSSM states specified in § Table 4-48. The Receiver Error is a reported error associated with the Port (see § Section 6.2). Triggering a Receiver Error on any of all of Elasticity Buffer Overflow/Underflow, and loss of Lane-to-Lane de-skew is optional.

- On a configured Link, which is in L0, error recovery will at a minimum be managed in a Layer above the Physical Layer (as described in § Section 3.6) by directing the Link to transition to Recovery.
  - Note: Link Errors may also result in the Physical Layer initiating an LTSSM state transition from L0 to Recovery.

- All LTSSM states other than L0 make progress<sup>72</sup> when Link Errors occur.
  - When operating with 8b/10b encoding, Link Errors that occur in LTSSM states other than L0 must not result in the Physical Layer initiating an LTSSM state transition.
  - When operating with 128b/130b encoding and not processing a Data Stream, Link Errors that occur in LTSSM states other than L0 must not result in the Physical Layer initiating an LTSSM state transition.

- When operating with 8b/10b encoding, if a Lane detects an implementation specific number of 8b/10b errors, Symbol lock must be verified or re-established as soon as possible.<sup>73</sup>

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

非 Flit 模式帧相关错误、Symbol 锁定丢失、通道去偏斜错误 (Lane De-skew Error) 以及弹性缓冲区上溢/下溢中的任意一项或全部触发接收器错误是可选的。

- 在使用 128b/130b 编码时的链路错误包括：
  - 帧错误 (Framing Error)、块对齐 (Block Alignment) 丢失、弹性缓冲区上溢/下溢或通道间去偏斜丢失。
  - 必须检查帧错误，并在 § 表 4-48 指定的 LTSSM 状态中触发接收器错误。该接收器错误是一项与端口关联的已报告错误（参见 § 第 6.2 节）。是否针对块对齐丢失、弹性缓冲区上溢/下溢以及通道间去偏斜丢失中的任意一项或全部触发接收器错误是可选的。

- 在使用 1b/1b 编码时的链路错误包括：
  - 帧错误、弹性缓冲区上溢/下溢或通道间去偏斜丢失。
  - 必须检查帧错误，并在 § 表 4-48 指定的 LTSSM 状态中触发接收器错误。该接收器错误是一项与端口关联的已报告错误（参见 § 第 6.2 节）。是否针对弹性缓冲区上溢/下溢以及通道间去偏斜丢失中的任意一项或全部触发接收器错误是可选的。

- 在已配置且处于 L0 状态的链路上，错误恢复至少由物理层 (Physical Layer) 之上的层（如 § 第 3.6 节所述）通过指示链路过渡到 Recovery 来管理。
  - 注：链路错误也可能导致物理层发起从 L0 到 Recovery 的 LTSSM 状态过渡。

- 除 L0 以外的所有 LTSSM 状态在发生链路错误时都应继续推进 (make progress)<sup>72</sup>。
  - 在使用 8b/10b 编码时，发生在 L0 之外 LTSSM 状态中的链路错误不得导致物理层发起 LTSSM 状态过渡。
  - 在使用 128b/130b 编码且未处理数据流 (Data Stream) 时，发生在 L0 之外 LTSSM 状态中的链路错误不得导致物理层发起 LTSSM 状态过渡。

- 在使用 8b/10b 编码时，如果某个通道检测到实现所指定数量的 8b/10b 错误，则必须尽快验证或重新建立 Symbol 锁定。<sup>73</sup>

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>


<a id="sec-4-2-5-9"></a>
## 4.2.5.9 Reset | 复位

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

Reset is described from a system point of view in § Section 6.6.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

复位的系统级描述见 § 第 6.6 节。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

<a id="sec-4-2-5-9-1"></a>
## 4.2.5.9.1 Fundamental Reset | 基本复位

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

When Fundamental Reset is asserted:

- The Receiver terminations are required to meet ZRX-HIGH-IMP-DC-POS and ZRX-HIGH-IMP-DC-NEG (see § Table 8-12).
- The Transmitter is required only to meet ITX-SHORT (see § Table 8-7).
- The Transmitter holds a constant DC common mode voltage.<sup>74</sup>

When Fundamental Reset is deasserted:

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

当基本复位 (Fundamental Reset) 有效时：

- 接收器 (Receiver) 端接必须满足 ZRX-HIGH-IMP-DC-POS 与 ZRX-HIGH-IMP-DC-NEG（参见 § 表 8-12）。
- 发送器 (Transmitter) 只需满足 ITX-SHORT（参见 § 表 8-7）。
- 发送器维持恒定的 DC 共模电压。<sup>74</sup>

当基本复位撤销时：

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

<sup>72</sup> 在本上下文中，"推进" (progress) 定义为 LTSSM 不会无限期停留在某一状态中，但 Detect 或 Disabled 状态可能例外。
<sup>73</sup> 验证和重新建立 Symbol 锁定的方法由实现自行决定。
<sup>74</sup> 所驱动的共模不要求满足 L0 与 Electrical Idle 之间的 DC 共模绝对差 (VTX-CM-DC-ACTIVE-IDLE-DELTA) 规范（参见 § 表 8-7）。

---

<!-- 📄 Page 486 -->
---

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

- The Port LTSSM (see § Section 4.2.6) is initialized (see § Section 6.6.1 for additional requirements).

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

- 端口 (Port) 的 LTSSM (链路训练与状态机, Link Training and Status State Machine)（参见 § 第 4.2.6 节）被初始化（更多要求见 § 第 6.6.1 节）。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

<a id="sec-4-2-5-9-2"></a>
## 4.2.5.9.2 Hot Reset | 热复位

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

Hot Reset is a protocol reset defined in § Section 4.2.6.12.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

热复位 (Hot Reset) 是 § 第 4.2.6.12 节定义的协议复位。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

<a id="sec-4-2-5-10"></a>
## 4.2.5.10 Link Data Rate Negotiation | 链路数据速率协商

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

All devices are required to start Link initialization using a 2.5 GT/s data rate on each Lane. A field in the training sequence Ordered Set (see § Section 4.2.5.1) is used to advertise all supported data rates. The Link trains to L0 initially in 2.5 GT/s data rate after which a data rate change occurs by going through the Recovery state.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

所有设备在每条通道 (Lane) 上都必须以 2.5 GT/s 的数据速率启动链路初始化。训练序列有序集 (TS, Training Sequence Ordered Set)（参见 § 第 4.2.5.1 节）中的一个字段用于通告 (Advertise) 所有受支持的数据速率。链路首先以 2.5 GT/s 数据速率训练至 L0 状态，之后通过进入 Recovery 状态完成数据速率变更。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

<a id="sec-4-2-5-11"></a>
## 4.2.5.11 Link Width and Lane Sequence Negotiation | 链路宽度与通道序列协商


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

PCI Express Links must consist of 1, 2, 4, 8, or 16 Lanes in parallel, referred to as x1, x2, x4, x8, and x16 Links, respectively. All Lanes within a Link must simultaneously transmit data based on the same frequency with a skew between Lanes not to exceed LTX-SKEW (§ Table 8-6). The negotiation process is described as a sequence of steps.

The negotiation establishes values for Link number and Lane number for each Lane that is part of a valid Link; each Lane that is not part of a valid Link exits the negotiation to become a separate Link or remains in Electrical Idle.

During Link width and Lane number negotiation, the two communicating Ports must accommodate the maximum allowed Lane-to-Lane skew as specified by LRX-SKEW in § Table 8-12.

Optional Link negotiation behaviors include Lane reversal, variable width Links, splitting of Ports into multiple Links and the configuration of a crosslink.

Other specifications may impose other rules and restrictions that must be comprehended by components compliant to those other specifications; it is the intent of this specification to comprehend interoperability for a broad range of component capabilities.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

PCI Express 链路 (Link) 必须由 1、2、4、8 或 16 条并行通道 (Lane) 组成，分别称为 x1、x2、x4、x8 和 x16 链路。同一链路内的所有通道必须以同一频率同时发送数据，通道间的偏斜不得超过 LTX-SKEW（§ 表 8-6）。协商过程以一系列步骤描述。

协商为属于有效链路的每条通道建立链路号和通道号；不属于有效链路的每条通道退出协商，成为独立链路或保持电气空闲 (Electrical Idle)。

在链路宽度和通道号协商期间，通信的两个端口必须容忍 § 表 8-12 中 LRX-SKEW 所规定的最大允许通道间偏斜。

可选的链路协商行为包括通道反转 (Lane Reversal)、可变宽度链路、将端口拆分为多个链路以及交叉链路 (Crosslink) 的配置。

其他规范可能会施加其组件必须遵守的其他规则与限制；本规范旨在涵盖广泛组件能力的互操作性。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>


<a id="sec-4-2-5-11-1"></a>
## 4.2.5.11.1 Required and Optional Port Behavior | 端口的必需与可选行为

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

- The ability for a xN Port to form a xN Link as well as a x1 Link (where N can be 16, 8, 4, 2, and 1) is required.
  - Designers must connect Ports between two different components in a way that allows those components to meet the above requirement. If the Ports between components are connected in ways that are not consistent with intended usage as defined by the component's Port descriptions/data sheets, behavior is undefined.
- The ability for a xN Port to form any Link width between N and 1 is optional.
  - An example of this behavior includes a x16 Port which can only configure into only one Link, but the width of the Link can be configured to be x8, x4, x2 as well as the required widths of x16 and x1.
- The ability to split a Port into two or more Links is optional.
  - An example of this behavior would be a x16 Port that may be able to configure two x8 Links, four x4 Links, or 16 x1 Links.
- Support for Lane reversal is optional.
  - If implemented, Lane reversal must be done for both the Transmitter and Receiver of a given Port for a multi-Lane Link.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

- 要求 xN 端口 (Port) 既能组成 xN 链路，也能组成 x1 链路（其中 N 可以是 16、8、4、2 和 1）。
  - 设计者必须以使两个不同组件均能满足上述要求的方式互连其端口。如果组件之间的端口连接方式与组件端口描述/数据手册中定义的预期用途不一致，则行为是未定义的。
- xN 端口能组成 N 到 1 之间任意宽度链路的能力是可选的。
  - 此行为的一个示例：x16 端口只能配置为一条链路，但该链路的宽度可配置为 x8、x4、x2 以及必需的 x16 和 x1。
- 将一个端口拆分为两条或多条链路的能力是可选的。
  - 此行为的一个示例：x16 端口可配置为两条 x8 链路、四条 x4 链路或 16 条 x1 链路。
- 对通道反转 (Lane Reversal) 的支持是可选的。
  - 如果实现通道反转，则对于多通道链路的给定端口，其发送器 (Transmitter) 和接收器 (Receiver) 都必须执行通道反转。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

---

<!-- 📄 Page 487 -->
---


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

  - An example of Lane reversal consists of Lane 0 of an Upstream Port attached to Lane N-1 of a Downstream Port where either the Downstream or Upstream device may reverse the Lane order to configure a xN Link.

Support for formation of a crosslink is optional. In this context, a Downstream Port connected to a Downstream Port or an Upstream Port connected to an Upstream Port is a crosslink.

Current and future electromechanical and/or form factor specifications may require the implementation of some optional features listed above. Component designers must read the specifications for the systems that the component(s) they are designing will used in to ensure compliance to those specifications.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

  - 通道反转的一个示例：上游端口 (Upstream Port) 的通道 0 与下游端口 (Downstream Port) 的通道 N-1 相连接，其中下游或上游设备均可反转通道顺序以配置 xN 链路。

对交叉链路 (Crosslink) 形成能力支持是可选的。在本规范中，下游端口与下游端口相连、或上游端口与上游端口相连称为交叉链路。

当前及未来的电气机械和/或外形规格 (Form Factor) 规范可能要求实现上述某些可选特性。组件设计者必须阅读其设计组件将要使用之系统的相关规范，以确保符合这些规范。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>


<a id="sec-4-2-5-12"></a>
## 4.2.5.12 Lane-to-Lane De-skew | 通道间去偏斜

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

The Receiver must compensate for the allowable skew between all Lanes within a multi-Lane Link (see § Table 8-7 and § Table 8-12) before delivering the data and control to the Data Link Layer.

When using 8b/10b encoding, an unambiguous Lane-to-Lane de-skew mechanism may use one or more of the following:

- The COM Symbol of a received TS1 or TS2 Ordered Set
- The COM Symbol of a received Electrical Idle Exit Ordered Set
- The COM Symbol of the first received SKP Ordered Set after an FTS sequence
- The COM Symbol of a received SKP Ordered Set during a training sequence when not using SRIS.

When using 128b/130b encoding, an unambiguous Lane-to-Lane de-skew mechanism may use one or more of the following:

- A received SDS Ordered Set
- A received Electrical Idle Exit Ordered Set except when exiting L0s
- The first received Electrical Idle Exit Ordered Set after an FTS Ordered Set when exiting L0s
- When operating at 8.0 GT/s, a received SKP Ordered Set
- When operating at a data rate of 16.0 GT/s or higher, the first received SKP Ordered Set after an FTS sequence
- When operating at a data rate of 16.0 GT/s or higher, a received SKP Ordered Set except when:
  - exiting a training sequence or
  - two SKP Ordered Sets are separated by an EDS

When using 1b/1b encoding, an unambiguous Lane-to-Lane de-skew mechanism may use one or more of the following:

- A received SDS Ordered Set
- A received Electrical Idle Exit Ordered Set
- A received Control SKP Ordered Set

Other de-skew mechanisms may also be employed, provided they are unambiguous. Lane-to-Lane de-skew must be performed during Configuration, Recovery, and L0s in the LTSSM.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

接收器 (Receiver) 必须在将数据和控制信息交付给数据链路层 (Data Link Layer) 之前，补偿多通道链路内所有通道之间所允许的偏斜（参见 § 表 8-7 和 § 表 8-12）。

在使用 8b/10b 编码 (Encoding) 时，可使用以下一项或多项作为无歧义的通道间去偏斜 (Lane-to-Lane De-skew) 机制：

- 接收到的 TS1 或 TS2 有序集 (Ordered Set) 的 COM Symbol
- 接收到的电气空闲退出有序集 (Electrical Idle Exit Ordered Set) 的 COM Symbol
- FTS 序列之后第一个接收到的 SKP 有序集的 COM Symbol
- 不使用 SRIS 时训练序列期间所接收到的 SKP 有序集的 COM Symbol。

在使用 128b/130b 编码时，可使用以下一项或多项作为无歧义的通道间去偏斜机制：

- 接收到的 SDS 有序集
- 接收到的电气空闲退出有序集（退出 L0s 时除外）
- 退出 L0s 时 FTS 有序集之后的第一个接收到的电气空闲退出有序集
- 在 8.0 GT/s 数据速率下运行时，接收到的 SKP 有序集
- 在 16.0 GT/s 或更高数据速率下运行时，FTS 序列之后第一个接收到的 SKP 有序集
- 在 16.0 GT/s 或更高数据速率下运行时，接收到的 SKP 有序集，但以下情况除外：
  - 正在退出训练序列；或
  - 两个 SKP 有序集之间被 EDS 隔开。

在使用 1b/1b 编码时，可使用以下一项或多项作为无歧义的通道间去偏斜机制：

- 接收到的 SDS 有序集
- 接收到的电气空闲退出有序集
- 接收到的控制 SKP (Control SKP) 有序集

只要是无歧义的机制，也可采用其他去偏斜方案。通道间去偏斜必须在 LTSSM 的 Configuration、Recovery 和 L0s 状态中执行。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

---

<!-- 📄 Page 488 -->
---

<a id="sec-4-2-5-13"></a>
## 4.2.5.13 Lane vs. Link Training | 通道训练与链路训练


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

The Link initialization process builds unassociated Lanes of a Port into associated Lanes that form a Link. For Lanes to configure properly into a desired Link, the TS1 and TS2 Ordered Sets must have the appropriate fields (Symbol 3, 4, and 5) set to the same values on all Lanes.

Links are formed at the conclusion of Configuration.

- If the optional behavior of a Port being able to configure multiple Links is employed, the following observations can be made:
  - A separate LTSSM is needed for each separate Link that is desired to be configured by any given Port.
  - The LTSSM Rules are written for configuring one Link. The decision to configure Links in a serial fashion or parallel is implementation specific.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

链路初始化过程将一个端口 (Port) 的未关联通道 (Lane) 组合为形成链路的关联通道。为了使通道正确配置为所需的链路，TS1 和 TS2 有序集 (Ordered Set) 必须在所有通道上将相应字段（Symbol 3、4 和 5）设置为相同的值。

链路在 Configuration 结束时形成。

- 如果采用端口能够配置多条链路的可选行为，则可得出以下观察：
  - 任何一个给定端口要配置的多条独立链路，每条都需要一个独立的 LTSSM (链路训练与状态机, Link Training and Status State Machine)。
  - LTSSM 规则是为配置一条链路而编写的。串行或并行配置链路的决定由实现自行决定。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>


<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

The LTSSM states are illustrated in § Figure 4-49. These states are described in following sections.

All timeout values specified for the Link Training and Status state machine (LTSSM) are minus 0 seconds and plus 50% unless explicitly stated otherwise. All timeout values must be set to the specified values after Fundamental Reset. All counter values must be set to the specified values after Fundamental Reset.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

LTSSM 状态如 § 图 4-49 所示。这些状态将在后续章节中描述。

除非另有明确说明，链路训练与状态机 (LTSSM, Link Training and Status State Machine) 所规定的所有超时值均允许负 0 秒、正 50% 的偏差。所有超时值必须在基本复位 (Fundamental Reset) 之后被设置为规定值。所有计数值也必须在基本复位之后被设置为规定值。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

**IMPLEMENTATION NOTE:**
**UNAMBIGUOUS LANE-TO-LANE DE-SKEW:**

The max skew at 2.5 GT/s that a receiver must be able to de-skew is 20 ns. A nominal SKP Ordered Set (i.e., one that does not have SKP Symbols added or removed by a Retimer (重定时器)) is 4 Symbols long, or 16 ns, at 2.5 GT/s. Generally SKP Ordered Sets are transmitted such that they are well spaced out, and no particular care is needed to use them for de-skew (i.e., they provide an unambiguous mechanism). If back-to-back SKP Ordered Sets are transmitted, an implementation that simply looks for the COM of the SKP Ordered Set to occur on each Lane at the same point in time may fail. When exiting L0s a transmitter may send back-to-back SKP Ordered Sets after the last FTS Ordered Set of the Fast Training Sequence. De-skew must be obtained in L0s, therefore the implementation must comprehend back-to-back SKP Ordered Sets when performing de-skew in this case.

Exceptions to the unambiguous mechanism in § Section 4.2.5.12 occur because back-to-back Ordered Sets might be sent (i.e., EIEOS might be sent back-to-back when exiting L0s when using 128b/130b encoding.) EIEOS can still be used for de-skew in this case, however the implementation must comprehend back-to-back EIEOS when performing de-skew.

When operating at a data rate of 16.0 GT/s or higher, a transmitter may send back-to-back SKP Ordered Sets at the end of a Training Sequence (e.g., TS2 Ordered Set, SKP Ordered Set, SKP Ordered Set, SDS Ordered Set). Implementations that choose to use SKP Ordered Sets for de-skew in this case are recommended to recognize that the back-to-back SKP Ordered Sets are different (i.e., Standard SKP Ordered Set followed by Control SKP Ordered Set).

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

**实现注意事项：**
**无歧义的通道间去偏斜 (Lane-to-Lane De-skew)：**

在 2.5 GT/s 下，接收器必须能够补偿的最大偏斜为 20 ns。标称 SKP 有序集（即未被 Retimer (重定时器) 添加或删除 SKP Symbol 的有序集）在 2.5 GT/s 下长度为 4 个 Symbol，即 16 ns。通常，SKP 有序集以充分间隔的方式发送，无需特别处理即可用于去偏斜（即它们提供了一种无歧义的机制）。如果发送的是背靠背的 SKP 有序集，那么仅查找每条通道上同一时刻出现 SKP 有序集 COM 的简单实现可能会失败。在退出 L0s 时，发送器可能在快速训练序列的最后一个 FTS 有序集之后发送背靠背的 SKP 有序集。此时必须完成 L0s 中的去偏斜，因此实现在去偏斜时必须考虑背靠背 SKP 有序集的情况。

§ 第 4.2.5.12 节中无歧义机制的例外情况发生于可能发送背靠背有序集时（即在使用 128b/130b 编码退出 L0s 时，EIEOS 可能背靠背发送）。在这种情况下 EIEOS 仍可用于去偏斜，但实现在去偏斜时必须考虑背靠背的 EIEOS。

当数据速率为 16.0 GT/s 或更高时，发送器可能在训练序列结束时发送背靠背的 SKP 有序集（例如：TS2 有序集、SKP 有序集、SKP 有序集、SDS 有序集）。在此情况下，选择使用 SKP 有序集进行去偏斜的实现应能识别这两种背靠背 SKP 有序集之间的差异（即标准 SKP 有序集后跟随控制 SKP 有序集）。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>


---


---

<a id="sec-4-2-6"></a>
## 4.2.6 Link Training and Status State Machine (LTSSM) Descriptions | 链路训练与状态机 (LTSSM) 描述

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

<!-- 📄 Page 489 -->
---

The purpose of this state is to detect when a far end termination is present.
The Port transmits training Ordered Sets and responds to the received training Ordered Sets. In this state, bit lock and
Symbol lock are established and Lane polarity is configured.
The Polling state includes Polling.Compliance (see § Section 4.2.7.2.2 ). This state is intended for use with test equipment
used to assess if the Transmitter and the interconnect present in the device under test setup is compliant with the
voltage and timing specifications in § Table 8-6, § Table 8-7, and § Table 8-12.
The Polling.Compliance state also includes a simplified inter-operability testing scheme that is intended to be
performed using a wide array of test and measurement equipment (i.e., pattern generator, oscilloscope, BERT, etc.). This
portion of the Polling.Compliance state is logically entered by at least one component asserting the Compliance Receive
bit (bit 4 in Symbol 5 of TS1) while not asserting the Loopback bit (bit 2 in Symbol 5 of TS1) upon entering Polling.Active.
The ability to set the Compliance Receive bit is implementation specific. A provision for changing data rates to that
indicated by the highest common transmitted and received Data Rate Identifiers (Symbol 4 of TS1) is also included to
make this behavior scalable to various data rates.
In Configuration, both the Transmitter and Receiver are sending and receiving data at the negotiated data rate. The
Lanes of a Port configure into a Link through a width and Lane negotiation sequence. Also, Lane-to-Lane de-skew must
occur, scrambling can be disabled if permitted, the N_FTS is set, and the Disabled or Loopback states can be entered.
In Recovery, both the Transmitter and Receiver are sending and receiving data using the configured Link and Lane
number as well as the previously supported data rate(s). Recovery allows a configured Link to change the data rate of
operation if desired, re-establish bit lock, Symbol lock or Block alignment, and Lane-to-Lane de-skew. Recovery is also
used to set a new N_FTS value and enter the Loopback, Disabled, Hot Reset, and Configuration states.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

<!-- 📄 Page 489 -->
---

该状态的目的是检测是否存在远端终结。
端口发送训练有序集 (Ordered Sets) 并对收到的训练有序集进行响应。在此状态下,建立位锁定 (bit lock) 和符号锁定 (Symbol lock),并完成通道 (Lane) 极性配置。
Polling 状态包含 Polling.Compliance(参见 § Section 4.2.7.2.2 )。该状态供测试设备使用,用于评估被测设备设置中的发送器 (Transmitter) 和互连是否符合 § Table 8-6、§ Table 8-7 和 § Table 8-12 中的电压与时序规范。
Polling.Compliance 状态还包含一种简化的互操作性测试方案,旨在使用各种测试和测量设备(例如码型发生器、示波器、BERT 等)执行。当至少一个组件在进入 Polling.Active 时置位 Compliance Receive 位(TS1 的 Symbol 5 中的第 4 位)且未置位 Loopback 位(TS1 的 Symbol 5 中的第 2 位)时,逻辑上进入 Polling.Compliance 状态的这一部分。
置位 Compliance Receive 位的能力是实现特定的。还包含将数据速率更改为由最高公共发送和接收的 Data Rate Identifiers(TS1 的 Symbol 4)所指示的值的机制,以使此行为可扩展到各种数据速率。
在 Configuration 中,发送器和接收器均以协商的数据速率收发数据。端口的各个通道通过宽度和通道协商序列配置为链路 (Link)。此外,必须完成通道间去偏斜 (Lane-to-Lane de-skew),可在允许时禁用加扰 (scrambling),设置 N_FTS,并可进入 Disabled 或 Loopback 状态。
在 Recovery 中,发送器和接收器均使用已配置的链路号和通道号以及先前支持的数据速率收发数据。Recovery 允许已配置的链路根据需要更改工作数据速率,重新建立位锁定、符号锁定或块对齐 (Block alignment),并完成通道间去偏斜。Recovery 还用于设置新的 N_FTS 值,并进入 Loopback、Disabled、Hot Reset 和 Configuration 状态。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

---

<a id="sec-4-2-6-2"></a>
## 4.2.6.2 Polling Overview | Polling 概述

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

IMPLEMENTATION NOTE:
USE OF POLLING.COMPLIANCE
Polling.Compliance is intended for a compliance test environment and not entered during normal operation and
cannot be disabled for any reason. Polling.Compliance is entered based on the physical system environment or
configuration register access mechanism as described in § Section 4.2.7.2.1 . Any other mechanism that causes a
Transmitter to output the compliance pattern is implementation specific and is beyond the scope of this
specification.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

**实现说明 (IMPLEMENTATION NOTE):**
**POLLING.COMPLIANCE 的使用 (USE OF POLLING.COMPLIANCE)**
Polling.Compliance 用于一致性测试环境,正常运行期间不会进入,且出于任何原因均不能被禁用。Polling.Compliance 根据物理系统环境或配置寄存器访问机制进入,详见 § Section 4.2.7.2.1。任何其他导致发送器输出一致性码型的机制均为实现特定的,不在本规范范围内。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

<a id="sec-4-2-6-5"></a>
## 4.2.6.5 L0 Overview | L0 概述

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

<!-- 📄 Page 490 -->
---

L0 is the normal operational state where data and control packets can be transmitted and received. All power
management states are entered from this state.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

<!-- 📄 Page 490 -->
---

L0 是正常工作状态,可发送和接收数据包与控制包。所有电源管理状态均从该状态进入。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

---

<a id="sec-4-2-6-6"></a>
## 4.2.6.6 L0s Overview | L0s 概述


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

<!-- 📄 Page 490 -->
---

L0s is intended as a power savings state.
- L0s is not supported in Flit Mode Mode and hardware must ignore the value of the L0s Enable bit for a Link
operating in FM,
- L0s is not supported on a Link that contains Retimers, or
- L0s is not supported when operating with separate reference clocks with independent Spread Spectrum
Clocking (SSC) (see § Section 4.2.8 and § Section 4.3.9 ).
L0s allows a Link to quickly enter and recover from a power conservation state without going through Recovery.
The entry to L0s occurs after receiving an EIOS.
The exit from L0s to L0 must re-establish bit lock, Symbol lock or Block alignment, and Lane-to-Lane de-skew.
A Transmitter and Receiver Lane pair on a Port are not required to both be in L0s simultaneously.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

<!-- 📄 Page 490 -->
---

L0s 旨在作为一种节能状态。
- 在 Flit 模式 (Flit Mode, FM) 中不支持 L0s,对于在 FM 中运行的链路 (Link),硬件必须忽略 L0s Enable 位的值。
- 在包含 Retimer (重定时器) 的链路上不支持 L0s。
- 在使用具有独立扩频时钟 (Spread Spectrum Clocking, SSC) 的独立参考时钟运行时,不支持 L0s(参见 § Section 4.2.8 和 § Section 4.3.9 )。
L0s 允许链路快速进入和退出节能状态,无需经过 Recovery。
进入 L0s 发生在收到 EIOS 之后。
从 L0s 退出到 L0 必须重新建立位锁定、符号锁定或块对齐,以及通道间去偏斜。
端口上的发送器与接收器通道对无需同时处于 L0s。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>


---

<a id="sec-4-2-6-7"></a>
## 4.2.6.7 L0p Overview | L0p 概述

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

<!-- 📄 Page 490 -->
---

L0p is a part of the L0 state and intended as a power savings state. L0p is supported only in Flit Mode, for all data rates.
L0p support is optional but strongly recommended for Ports. L0p support is mandatory for Pseudo-Ports (Retimers) that
support Flit Mode. L0p enables a Link to have some Lanes active while the remaining Lanes will be in electrical idle state.
With Flit Mode, Link Upconfigure, which performs dynamic link width adjustment through the state transition L0 →
Recovery → Configuration → L0, is not supported.
L0p is symmetric in width. All legal widths (x1, x2, x4, x8, x16) up to the configured Link width must be supported by
Ports that support L0p. When the Link is in L0p, at least one Lane in each direction must be active. L0p support is
negotiated only during Configuration.Complete state when LinkUp=0b, as described in § Section 4.2.7 .
Device Port Functions indicate support for L0p by Setting L0p Supported and by supplying a Port L0p Exit Latency value.
A Port Function for which L0p Enable is set to 1b, Hardware Autonomous Width Disable is set to 0b, and Target Link Width is set
to 111b, is permitted to initiate L0p requests with no architected software intervention by sending Link Management
DLLPs (see § Section 4.2.6.7.1 ) to its Link Partner. System software can direct a Port Function to initiate an L0p Link
Width change by sending to that Function a CfgWr TLP to set L0p Enable to 1b and Target Link Width to a value from 000b
through 100b. It is strongly recommended that system software leave Target Link Width unchanged (i.e., 111b) other than
for usage cases such as test or debug.
If the link is L0p capable and the Link width has been changed by entering the Configuration state, the following rules
apply:
1. On entry to Recovery the Link will revert to its configured Link width on the last entry to L0
2. L0p cannot be used to turn on Lanes that were turned off in Configuration state.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

<!-- 📄 Page 490 -->
---

L0p 是 L0 状态的一部分,旨在作为一种节能状态。L0p 仅在 Flit 模式的所有数据速率下受支持。
L0p 支持对端口是可选的,但强烈建议支持。L0p 支持对支持 Flit 模式的伪端口 (Pseudo-Ports,Retimer) 是强制的。L0p 使链路能够使部分通道保持活动状态,而其余通道处于电气空闲 (electrical idle) 状态。
在 Flit 模式下,不通过 L0 → Recovery → Configuration → L0 的状态转换执行动态链路宽度调整的 Link Upconfigure 不受支持。
L0p 在宽度上是对称的。支持 L0p 的端口必须支持已配置链路宽度以内的所有合法宽度(x1、x2、x4、x8、x16)。当链路处于 L0p 时,每个方向上至少必须有一个通道处于活动状态。如 § Section 4.2.7 所述,L0p 支持仅在 LinkUp=0b 时的 Configuration.Complete 状态期间协商。
设备端口功能 (Device Port Functions) 通过置位 L0p Supported 并提供 Port L0p Exit Latency 值来表明对 L0p 的支持。
对于 L0p Enable 置 1b、Hardware Autonomous Width Disable 置 0b 且 Target Link Width 置 111b 的端口功能,允许通过向其链路伙伴 (Link Partner) 发送 Link Management DLLP(参见 § Section 4.2.6.7.1 )来发起 L0p 请求,而无需架构化软件的干预。系统软件可通过向该功能发送 CfgWr TLP,将 L0p Enable 置 1b、Target Link Width 置为 000b 至 100b 之间的值,从而指示端口功能发起 L0p 链路宽度变更。强烈建议系统软件将 Target Link Width 保持不变(即 111b),测试或调试等使用场景除外。
如果链路支持 L0p 且链路宽度已通过进入 Configuration 状态而改变,则适用以下规则:
1. 在进入 Recovery 时,链路将在最近一次进入 L0 时恢复到其已配置的链路宽度。
2. L0p 不能用于开启在 Configuration 状态中关闭的通道。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

---

<a id="sec-4-2-6-7-1"></a>
## 4.2.6.7.1 L0p Request/Response Rules | L0p 请求/响应规则


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

<!-- 📄 Page 491 -->
---

With L0p, any Lane that goes to electrical idle must be in electrical idle for a minimum time of TTX-IDLE-MIN. The DC
common mode voltage in TX during electrical idle must be within specification. The Receiver needs to wait a minimum
of TTX-IDLE-MIN to start looking for Electrical Idle Exit.
Once L0p Is enabled, any Port can request a Link width change by sending up to three identical Link Management DLLPs
within five consecutive Flits. Two Flits are considered consecutive even if they are separated by an Ordered Set. This can
be either up-size to increase the number of active Lanes or down-size to reduce the number of active Lanes. The Link
partner must either Ack or Nak each L0p request on a valid Flit, unless it is required to ignore the request. A Port must
respond to an L0p request within the following time interval of receiving a valid Flit with the request: 4 μs if 8b/10b
encoding is used, 1 μs otherwise. Time is measured from the last bit of the Flit with the request on the ingress package
pin to the first bit of the Flit with the response on the egress package pin. A Port that has requested a Link width change
but has not received a response within the follwing time interval of issuing the request must either re-request the
identical Link width change or abandon the request: 8 μs if 8b/10b encoding is used, 4 μs otherwise. If identical L0p
requests are received within five consecutive Flits, the same response must be sent. After responding to a request, the
Link partner must consider the request to be abandoned if one of the following conditions are true:
- On an up-size request:
  - the requester did not initiate the Link width upsize within 16 μs after the Flit with the Ack was
  transmitted AND
  - the requester did not re-send the up-size request within 8 μs after the Flit with the Ack was
  transmitted
- On a down-size request with an Ack response:
  - the requester did not resend the Link width downsize request within 4 μs after the Flit with the Ack
  was transmitted, AND
  - the requester did not initiate the Link width downsize within 16 μs after the Flit with the Ack
  was transmitted and the Link Partner did not down-size the Link by sending an EIOSQ
- On a down-size request with a Nak response:
  - the requester did not resend the Link width down-size request within 4 μs after the Flit with the Nak
  was transmitted

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

<!-- 📄 Page 491 -->
---

对于 L0p,任何进入电气空闲的通道必须保持电气空闲至少 TTX-IDLE-MIN 时间。电气空闲期间 TX 的 DC 共模电压必须在规范范围内。接收器需要至少等待 TTX-IDLE-MIN 后才能开始检测 Electrical Idle Exit。
一旦使能 L0p,任何端口都可以通过在五个连续的 Flit 内发送最多三个相同的 Link Management DLLP 来请求链路宽度变更。即使两个 Flit 被有序集 (Ordered Set) 分隔,它们也被视为连续的。这可以是增加活动通道数量的 up-size,也可以是减少活动通道数量的 down-size。除非要求忽略请求,否则链路伙伴必须在有效的 Flit 上对每个 L0p 请求进行 Ack 或 Nak。端口必须在收到包含请求的有效 Flit 后的以下时间间隔内响应 L0p 请求:若使用 8b/10b 编码 (8b/10b 编码),则为 4 μs,否则为 1 μs。时间从入口封装引脚上包含请求的 Flit 的最后一位测量到出口封装引脚上包含响应的 Flit 的第一位。已请求链路宽度变更但在发出请求后以下时间间隔内未收到响应的端口必须重新请求相同的链路宽度变更或放弃该请求:若使用 8b/10b 编码,则为 8 μs,否则为 4 μs。如果在五个连续的 Flit 内收到相同的 L0p 请求,则必须发送相同的响应。响应请求后,链路伙伴必须在以下任一条件成立时认为该请求已被放弃:
- 对于 up-size 请求:
  - 请求者在发送包含 Ack 的 Flit 后 16 μs 内未发起链路宽度增大,并且
  - 请求者在发送包含 Ack 的 Flit 后 8 μs 内未重新发送 up-size 请求
- 对于带有 Ack 响应的 down-size 请求:
  - 请求者在发送包含 Ack 的 Flit 后 4 μs 内未重新发送链路宽度缩小请求,并且
  - 请求者在发送包含 Ack 的 Flit 后 16 μs 内未发起链路宽度缩小,且链路伙伴未通过发送 EIOSQ 来缩小链路
- 对于带有 Nak 响应的 down-size 请求:
  - 请求者在发送包含 Nak 的 Flit 后 4 μs 内未重新发送链路宽度缩小请求

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>


---

<a id="sec-4-2-6-7-2"></a>
## 4.2.6.7.2 L0p Ack/Nak Rules | L0p Ack/Nak 规则

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

<!-- 📄 Page 491 -->
---

The following rules must be followed for L0p:
- If the Hardware Autonomous Width Disable bit in the Link Control register is set to 1b or if the L0p Enable bit is set
to 0b, a Port:
  - Must not request any down-size.
  - Must initiate an up-size request to fully configured Link width if the Link is not at the fully configured
  width.
- L0p Ack and Nak Rules:
  - A Port is permitted to Nak an L0p Request for a lower width than the current width if L0p.Priority is
  not set.
  - If a Port is requesting link width down-size due to thermal throttling or reliability reasons, then it
  should set L0p.Priority in the L0p Request. The Link partner must Ack priority L0p Requests if it is not
  requesting an L0p width change and it is not going to request an L0p width change in the next Flit. If

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

<!-- 📄 Page 491 -->
---

L0p 必须遵循以下规则:
- 如果 Link Control 寄存器中的 Hardware Autonomous Width Disable 位置 1b,或 L0p Enable 位置 0b,则端口:
  - 不得请求任何 down-size。
  - 如果链路未处于完全配置的宽度,则必须发起 up-size 请求以达到完全配置的链路宽度。
- L0p Ack 和 Nak 规则:
  - 如果 L0p.Priority 未置位,允许端口对请求宽度低于当前宽度的 L0p 请求进行 Nak。
  - 如果端口因热节流 (thermal throttling) 或可靠性原因而请求链路宽度 down-size,则应在 L0p 请求中置位 L0p.Priority。链路伙伴必须对优先级 L0p 请求进行 Ack,前提是它未请求 L0p 宽度变更且在下一个 Flit 中也不会请求 L0p 宽度变更。如果

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

---

<a id="sec-4-2-6-7-3"></a>
## 4.2.6.7.3 L0p Priority Rules | L0p 优先级规则


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

<!-- 📄 Page 492 -->
---

the Link partner is going to request an L0p width change in the next Flit, it must show up on the
Transmitter pins in 100 ns with L0p.Priority set in the L0p Request. The requirements for setting
L0p.Priority are implementation specific and behavior is undefined if L0p.Priority bit is set on an
upsize.
- Any Port requesting a link width down-size that receives an Ack from its Link partner is responsible
for initiating the up-size request eventually when the underlying conditions no longer prohibit the
link from operating at full width.
- If both Ports are simultaneously requesting link width change the following rules apply to determine
which request wins. A Port is permitted to consider a pending request for L0p as simultaneous as
long as it has the L0p request scheduled to appear in its Transmitter pins within 100 ns. The Port that
does not win must Ack the request to the winning Port through the proper L0p response.
  - If both requests have L0p.Priority set:
    - if both sides are requesting the same width, the Downstream Port wins
    - else, the request with the lower width wins
  - If one request has L0p.Priority set, it wins
  - If neither side has L0p.Priority set:
    - if both sides are requesting the same width, the Downstream Port wins
    - else, the request with the higher width wins
- A Port Is permitted to Nak a request for a lower width than the current L0p width if L0p.Priority is not
set.
- A Port must ignore another L0p Request with the same width if it has already Ack'ed an L0p request
and its Transmitter Lanes are already at or in the process of transitioning to the same width.
- An entry to Recovery results in the Link going to its configured Link width on the most recent
transition from Configuration to L0 and all the associated L0p transition states (such as width change
request/ response and all the tracking information such as time since Ack/ Nak) are reset as if no
width change request was made.
Once an L0p request to change link width has been initiated, it must be completed or abandoned.
A Port must not initiate a request for a new Link width unless the following conditions are met:
1. No link width resizing has been in progress in the last 1 μs if 1b/1b or 128b/130b encoding is used or 4 μs if 8b/
10b encoding is used.
2. At least 16 μs, has elapsed since the Port abandoned its last L0p Request, if any
3. The last L0p request from the remote Port, if any, was abandoned after being Ack'ed by the Port

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

<!-- 📄 Page 492 -->
---

链路伙伴将在下一个 Flit 中请求 L0p 宽度变更,则它必须在 100 ns 内出现在发送器引脚上,且 L0p 请求中置位 L0p.Priority。置位 L0p.Priority 的要求是实现特定的;如果在 upsize 上置位 L0p.Priority 位,则行为未定义。
- 任何请求链路宽度 down-size 并从其链路伙伴收到 Ack 的端口负责在根本条件不再禁止链路以全宽运行时最终发起 up-size 请求。
- 如果两个端口同时请求链路宽度变更,则适用以下规则以确定哪个请求获胜。允许端口将挂起的 L0p 请求视为同时的,只要该 L0p 请求已安排在 100 ns 内出现在其发送器引脚上。未获胜的端口必须通过适当的 L0p 响应向获胜的端口 Ack 该请求。
  - 如果两个请求都置位了 L0p.Priority:
    - 如果两侧请求相同的宽度,则下游端口 (Downstream Port) 获胜
    - 否则,请求较低宽度的请求获胜
  - 如果一个请求置位了 L0p.Priority,则该请求获胜
  - 如果两侧都未置位 L0p.Priority:
    - 如果两侧请求相同的宽度,则下游端口获胜
    - 否则,请求较高宽度的请求获胜
- 如果 L0p.Priority 未置位,允许端口对请求宽度低于当前 L0p 宽度的请求进行 Nak。
- 如果端口已对 L0p 请求进行 Ack,且其发送器通道已处于或正在过渡到相同宽度,则必须忽略具有相同宽度的另一个 L0p 请求。
- 进入 Recovery 会使链路恢复为最近一次从 Configuration 转换到 L0 时的已配置链路宽度,并且所有相关的 L0p 转换状态(例如宽度变更请求/响应以及自 Ack/Nak 以来的时间等所有跟踪信息)被复位,就好像从未发出过宽度变更请求一样。
一旦发起 L0p 链路宽度变更请求,则必须完成或放弃该请求。
除非满足以下条件,否则端口不得发起新链路宽度的请求:
1. 在最近 1 μs(若使用 1b/1b 或 128b/130b 编码)或 4 μs(若使用 8b/10b 编码)内,链路宽度调整未在进行中。
2. 自端口放弃其最近一次 L0p 请求(如果有)以来已过去至少 16 μs。
3. 来自远端端口的最近一次 L0p 请求(如果有)已被该端口 Ack 后放弃。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>

<a id="sec-4-2-6-7-4"></a>
## 4.2.6.7.4 L0p Down-Size and Up-Size | L0p Down-Size 和 Up-Size

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

<!-- 📄 Page 492 -->
---

On a Link width down-size, the following steps must be taken by each Port independently after the Link width
down-configure request has been 'Ack'ed:
- On the next scheduled SKP Ordered Set interval, the Lanes that are being turned off will send an EIOSQ instead
of a SKP Ordered Set.
- The Lanes that sent an EIOSQ go to Electrical Idle after the EIOSQ is sent. The Lanes that sent a SKP Ordered
Set resume transmitting Flits with a SKP Ordered Set insertion interval that corresponds to the new Link width
(see § Section 4.2.3.4.5 ).
- If the requesting Port did not receive an Ack for the L0p request, but sees the Link Partner sending the EIOSQ
Ordered Set, it must treat that as an 'Ack'. (An example scenario where this may happen is if the Flit containing
the Ack DLLP was corrupted.)

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

<!-- 📄 Page 492 -->
---

对于链路宽度 down-size,在链路宽度下配置请求已被 'Ack' 后,每个端口必须独立执行以下步骤:
- 在下一个调度的 SKP (跳过有序集) 有序集间隔,即将关闭的通道将发送 EIOSQ 而不是 SKP 有序集。
- 发送了 EIOSQ 的通道在 EIOSQ 发送完成后进入电气空闲 (Electrical Idle)。发送了 SKP 有序集的通道以对应于新链路宽度的 SKP 有序集插入间隔恢复发送 Flit(参见 § Section 4.2.3.4.5 )。
- 如果请求端口未收到 L0p 请求的 Ack,但看到链路伙伴发送 EIOSQ 有序集,则必须将其视为 'Ack'。(可能发生这种情况的一个示例场景是包含 Ack DLLP 的 Flit 被损坏。)

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

---

<a id="sec-4-2-6-7-5"></a>
## 4.2.6.7.5 L0p Down-Size Lane Deactivation | L0p Down-Size 通道去激活


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

<!-- 📄 Page 493 -->
---

- During the down-size negotiation, an EIOS must be received on all lanes to be deactivated at the same time
after adjusting for lane to lane skew; else the LTSSM must enter Recovery.
  - A Port is permitted to enter Recovery after 24 msec of sending the EIOSQ on a downsize if the Link did
  not achieve the desired Link width in both directions
On a Link width up-size the following steps must be taken:
- The up-sizing action must be initiated by the requesting port. The non-requesting Port waits for detection of
Electrical Idle exit on the lanes to be activated before starting the up-sizing actions.
- Data Stream continues on the active Lanes
- For the Lanes to be activated the following sequence must be followed:
  - SKP Ordered Sets must be scheduled at the same time as Lane 0 and the same number of SKPs must
  be added or deleted as Lane 0 by the Receiver in the (Pseudo-)Port. A Port is permitted to truncate a
  TS1/TS2 Ordered Set in 8b/10b encoding to transmit the SKP Ordered Set at the same time as Lane 0.
  - Transmit TS1 Ordered Sets meeting the requirements for TS1 Ordered Sets in § Section 4.2.7.4.1 and
  § Section 4.2.5.3 . The Transmitter must ensure it follows the EIEOSQ rule of not being broken up by a
  SKP Ordered Set even though it has to send SKP Ordered Set at the same time as the active Lanes.
  This can be done by scheduling the start of EIEOSQ appropriately. However, a Transmitter is
  permitted to restart the EIEOSQ sequence after the SKP Ordered Set if the SKP Ordered Set
  interrupted an EIEOSQ in progress. If an EIEOSQ is scheduled to precede a subsequent scheduled
  SDS Ordered Set within 256 UI, the transmitter is permitted to not send that EIEOSQ or to delay the
  SDS until the next SKP interval.
  - If eight consecutive TS1 or TS2 Ordered sets are received on all Lanes that are to be activated, the
  transmitter must transition to sending TS2 Ordered Sets meeting the requirements for TS2 Ordered
  Sets in § Section 4.2.7.4.4 and § Section 4.2.5.3 .
  - After receiving eight consecutive TS2 Ordered Sets and sending at least 16 TS2 Ordered sets after the
  receipt of one TS2 Ordered Set on all the Lanes to be activated, the Port sends an SDS Ordered Set
  sequence if the data rate is 8.0 GT/s or higher just prior to sending the next scheduled SKP Ordered
  Set that will be sent (on the active as well as to be activated Lanes).
  - Lane to Lane de-skew must be completed by the Receiver using the SKP Ordered Set across all the
  currently active as well as the Lanes being activated that have received eight consecutive TS2
  Ordered Set and the SDS Ordered Set, if the data rate is 8.0 GT/s or higher. Starting with the SKP
  Ordered Set that follows the SDS Ordered Set, the SKP Ordered Set insertion interval that
  corresponds to the new Link width takes effect (see § Section 4.2.3.4.5 ).
  - If 24 ms has elapsed since the start of activation and the Lanes are not part of the active Lanes, the
  LTSSM must be directed to enter Recovery.
The DLLP encoding for the various L0p commands and responses is shown in § Figure 3-15. The definition of the various
fields in the DLLP are shown in § Table 4-44.

75. This is similar but not identical to Recovery.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

<!-- 📄 Page 493 -->
---

- 在 down-size 协商期间,在对通道间偏斜进行调整后,必须在所有要停用的通道上同时收到 EIOS;否则 LTSSM (链路训练与状态机) 必须进入 Recovery。
  - 如果在 down-size 上发送 EIOSQ 之后 24 ms 内链路未在两个方向上达到所需的链路宽度,则允许端口进入 Recovery。
对于链路宽度 up-size,必须执行以下步骤:
- up-sizing 操作必须由请求端口发起。非请求端口在开始 up-sizing 操作之前,等待检测要激活的通道上的 Electrical Idle exit。
- 数据流 (Data Stream) 在活动通道上继续。
- 对于要激活的通道,必须遵循以下序列:
  - SKP 有序集必须与 Lane 0 同时调度,且接收器在(伪)端口中必须添加或删除与 Lane 0 相同数量的 SKP。允许端口在 8b/10b 编码中截断 TS1/TS2 有序集以与 Lane 0 同时发送 SKP 有序集。
  - 发送满足 § Section 4.2.7.4.1 和 § Section 4.2.5.3 中 TS1 有序集要求的 TS1 有序集。发送器必须确保遵循 EIEOSQ 规则,即尽管必须与活动通道同时发送 SKP 有序集,但 EIEOSQ 不应被 SKP 有序集打断。这可以通过适当地调度 EIEOSQ 的开始来实现。但是,如果 SKP 有序集打断了正在进行的 EIEOSQ,则允许发送器在 SKP 有序集之后重新启动 EIEOSQ 序列。如果计划在后续调度的 SDS 有序集之前 256 UI 内调度 EIEOSQ,则发送器可以不发送该 EIEOSQ,或将 SDS 延迟到下一个 SKP 间隔。
  - 如果在所有要激活的通道上收到八个连续的 TS1 或 TS2 有序集,则发送器必须转换为发送满足 § Section 4.2.7.4.4 和 § Section 4.2.5.3 中 TS2 有序集要求的 TS2 有序集。
  - 在所有要激活的通道上收到八个连续的 TS2 有序集并发送至少 16 个 TS2 有序集后(从收到一个 TS2 有序集起),如果数据速率为 8.0 GT/s 或更高,端口在即将发送的下一个调度的 SKP 有序集之前(在活动通道和要激活的通道上)发送 SDS 有序集序列。
  - 接收器必须使用 SKP 有序集在所有当前活动的以及已收到八个连续 TS2 有序集和 SDS 有序集(如果数据速率为 8.0 GT/s 或更高)的要激活的通道上完成通道间去偏斜。从 SDS 有序集之后的 SKP 有序集开始,对应于新链路宽度的 SKP 有序集插入间隔生效(参见 § Section 4.2.3.4.5 )。
  - 如果自激活开始以来已过去 24 ms 且通道仍不是活动通道的一部分,则必须指示 LTSSM 进入 Recovery。
各种 L0p 命令和响应的 DLLP 编码如 § Figure 3-15 所示。DLLP 中各个字段的定义如 § Table 4-44 所示。

75. 这与 Recovery 类似但不完全相同。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>

## 4.2.6.7.6 Link Management DLLP Encoding | Link Management DLLP 编码

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>

<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

<!-- 📄 Page 494 -->
---

**Table 4-44 Link Management DLLP**

| Field | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| Byte 0 | Link Management DLLP - must be 0010 1000b |
| Byte 1 | Link Mgmt Type - qualifies Bytes 2 and 3 |
| | 00h - L0p DLLP |
| | Others - Reserved |
| Byte 2, Bit [4] | L0p.Priority - L0p Priority Request - Meaning of this field varies by L0p.Cmd value |
| | If L0p.Cmd is: L0p Request |
| | 0b - Normal Priority L0p Request |
| | 1b - High Priority L0p Request |
| | Others - Reserved |
| | Reserved if Link Mgmt Type is other than 00h. |
| Byte 2, Bits [3:0] | L0p.Cmd - L0p Command or Response |
| | 0000b - L0p Request |
| | 0001b - L0p Request Ack |
| | 0010b - L0p Request Nak |
| | 0100b - Reserved |
| | 0110b - Reserved |
| | 0111b - Reserved |
| | Others - Reserved |
| | Reserved if Link Mgmt Type is other than 00h. |
| Byte 3, Bits [7:4] | Meaning of this field varies by L0p.Cmd value |
| | If L0p.Cmd is: L0p Request Ack - Response Payload - Reflects the value of L0p Link Width of the corresponding command |
| | If L0p.Cmd is: L0p Request Nak - Response Payload - Reflects the value of L0p Link Width of the corresponding command |

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

<!-- 📄 Page 494 -->
---

**Table 4-44 Link Management DLLP | 表 4-44 Link Management DLLP**

| 字段 | 描述 |
|-------|-------------|
| Byte 0 | Link Management DLLP - 必须为 0010 1000b |
| Byte 1 | Link Mgmt Type - 限定 Bytes 2 和 3 |
| | 00h - L0p DLLP |
| | 其他 - 保留 |
| Byte 2, Bit [4] | L0p.Priority - L0p 优先级请求 - 此字段的含义因 L0p.Cmd 值而异 |
| | 若 L0p.Cmd 为:L0p Request |
| | 0b - 正常优先级 L0p 请求 |
| | 1b - 高优先级 L0p 请求 |
| | 其他 - 保留 |
| | 若 Link Mgmt Type 不为 00h,则保留。 |
| Byte 2, Bits [3:0] | L0p.Cmd - L0p 命令或响应 |
| | 0000b - L0p Request |
| | 0001b - L0p Request Ack |
| | 0010b - L0p Request Nak |
| | 0100b - 保留 |
| | 0110b - 保留 |
| | 0111b - 保留 |
| | 其他 - 保留 |
| | 若 Link Mgmt Type 不为 00h,则保留。 |
| Byte 3, Bits [7:4] | 此字段的含义因 L0p.Cmd 值而异 |
| | 若 L0p.Cmd 为:L0p Request Ack - Response Payload - 反映相应命令的 L0p Link Width 值 |
| | 若 L0p.Cmd 为:L0p Request Nak - Response Payload - 反映相应命令的 L0p Link Width 值 |

<a id="sec-4-2-6-7-7"></a>
## 4.2.6.7.7 Link Management DLLP Encoding (continued) | Link Management DLLP 编码(续)


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

<!-- 📄 Page 495 -->
---

| Field | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| Byte 3, Bits [3:0] | Meaning of this field varies by L0p.Cmd value |
| | If L0p.Cmd is: L0p Request - L0p Link Width - Desired Link Width |
| | 0001b - x1 |
| | 0010b - x2 |
| | 0100b - x4 |
| | 1000b - x8 |
| | 0000b - x16 |
| | Others - Reserved |
| | Reserved if Link Mgmt Type is other than 00h. |

Receivers must silently ignore Link Management DLLPs if:
- The Link is operating in Non-Flit Mode.
- The Link Mgmt Type field contains a Reserved value.
- The Link Mgmt Type field is 00h and the L0p.Cmd field contains a reserved value.
- The Link Mgmt Type field is 00h, the L0p.Cmd field contains L0p Request and the L0p Link Width field contains
a reserved value.
- The Link Mgmt Type field is 00h, the L0p.Cmd field contains L0p Request Ack or L0p Request Nak and Response
Payload contains a reserved value.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

<!-- 📄 Page 495 -->
---

| 字段 | 描述 |
|-------|-------------|
| Byte 3, Bits [3:0] | 此字段的含义因 L0p.Cmd 值而异 |
| | 若 L0p.Cmd 为:L0p Request - L0p Link Width - 期望的链路宽度 |
| | 0001b - x1 |
| | 0010b - x2 |
| | 0100b - x4 |
| | 1000b - x8 |
| | 0000b - x16 |
| | 其他 - 保留 |
| | 若 Link Mgmt Type 不为 00h,则保留。 |

在以下情况下,接收器必须静默忽略 Link Management DLLP:
- 链路在非 Flit 模式 (Non-Flit Mode) 下运行。
- Link Mgmt Type 字段包含保留值。
- Link Mgmt Type 字段为 00h 且 L0p.Cmd 字段包含保留值。
- Link Mgmt Type 字段为 00h,L0p.Cmd 字段包含 L0p Request 且 L0p Link Width 字段包含保留值。
- Link Mgmt Type 字段为 00h,L0p.Cmd 字段包含 L0p Request Ack 或 L0p Request Nak 且 Response Payload 包含保留值。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>


---

<a id="sec-4-2-6-7-8"></a>
## 4.2.6.7.8 L0p Flow Example | L0p 流程示例

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

<!-- 📄 Page 496 -->
---

Example of L0p Flows: § Figure 4-48 demonstrates L0p flows in a x16 Link.

1. L0p is enabled on the Link during the Configuration.Complete state.
2. The USP requests the Link to downsize to x8 which is Ack'ed by the DSP.
3. On the next SKP Ordered Set the L0p indication is provided by the Port on a per-Lane basis. Lanes 0-7 continue
with the traffic whereas Lanes 8-15 send an EIOSQ and go to electrical Idle. Thus, the Link now has 8 active
Lanes and 8 Lanes in idle state.
4. At a later point, the DSP requests upsizing the Link to x16 while the USP has made a request to further
downsize to x4. The x4 request gets NAK'ed, the x16 gets Ack'ed. Then link training proceeds in Lanes 8-15,
initiated by the DSP with the exchange of TS1/TS2 Ordered Sets with EIEOS inserted at appropriate intervals.
When Lanes 8-15 are ready, an SDS is sent immediately preceding the next scheduled SKP Ordered Set in the
Lanes.
5. The Link operates as a x16 Link after sending the SKP Ordered Set across all 16 Lanes. Even though L0p is
symmetric during the transitions, the two sides may be operating at different widths for a while. This is similar
to the situation during entry to L1 or L0.

Note that if the data rates is 2.5 GT/s, the EIEOS and SDS will not be present. If the data rate is 5.0 GT/s, EIEOS will be
present but not SDS.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

<!-- 📄 Page 496 -->
---

L0p 流程示例:§ Figure 4-48 演示了 x16 链路中的 L0p 流程。

1. L0p 在 Configuration.Complete 状态期间在链路上使能。
2. USP (上游端口) 请求将链路缩小到 x8,DSP (下游端口) 进行 Ack。
3. 在下一个 SKP 有序集上,L0p 指示由端口按通道提供。通道 0-7 继续传输流量,而通道 8-15 发送 EIOSQ 并进入电气空闲。因此,链路现在有 8 个活动通道和 8 个处于空闲状态的通道。
4. 在稍后某个时刻,DSP 请求将链路 upsize 到 x16,而 USP 已发出进一步缩小到 x4 的请求。x4 请求被 NAK,x16 请求被 Ack。然后通道 8-15 中的链路训练继续进行,由 DSP 启动,交换 TS1/TS2 有序集,并在适当间隔插入 EIEOS。当通道 8-15 就绪时,在通道中下一个调度的 SKP 有序集之前立即发送 SDS。
5. 在所有 16 个通道上发送 SKP 有序集后,链路以 x16 链路运行。尽管 L0p 在转换过程中是对称的,但两侧可能在一段时间内以不同宽度运行。这与进入 L1 或 L0 时的情况类似。

请注意,如果数据速率为 2.5 GT/s,则不会出现 EIEOS 和 SDS。如果数据速率为 5.0 GT/s,则会出现 EIEOS,但不会出现 SDS。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

---

> **Figure 4-48.** Example of L0p flow in a x16 Link | x16 链路中 L0p 流程示例

> <img src="figures/chapter_04/fig_0497_1_tight.png" width="700">

> <img src="figures/chapter_04/fig_0496_1_tight.png" width="700">

---

<a id="sec-4-2-6-8"></a>
## 4.2.6.8 L1 Overview | L1 概述

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

<!-- 📄 Page 497 -->
---

L1 is intended as a power savings state.
The L1 state allows an additional power savings over L0s at the cost of additional resume latency.
The entry to L1 occurs after being directed by the Data Link Layer and receiving an EIOS.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

<!-- 📄 Page 497 -->
---

L1 旨在作为一种节能状态。
L1 状态以额外的恢复延迟 (resume latency) 为代价,提供比 L0s 更大的节能效果。
在数据链路层指示并收到 EIOS 之后进入 L1。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

---

<a id="sec-4-2-6-9"></a>
## 4.2.6.9 L2 Overview | L2 概述


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

<!-- 📄 Page 497 -->
---

Power can be aggressively conserved in L2. Most of the Transmitter and Receiver may be shut off.
76 Main power and
clocks are not guaranteed, but Aux
77 power is available.
When Beacon support is required by the associated system or form factor specification, an Upstream Port that supports
the wakeup capability must be able to send; and a Downstream Port must be able to receive; a wakeup signal referred to
as a Beacon.
The entry to L2 occurs after being directed by the Data Link Layer and receiving an EIOS.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

<!-- 📄 Page 497 -->
---

L2 中可以积极节省功耗。大多数发送器和接收器可以关闭。
76 主电源和时钟无法保证,但辅助
77 电源可用。
当相关系统或外形规格 (form factor) 规范要求 Beacon 支持时,支持唤醒功能的上游端口 (Upstream Port) 必须能够发送唤醒信号;下游端口 (Downstream Port) 必须能够接收唤醒信号;该唤醒信号称为 Beacon。
在数据链路层指示并收到 EIOS 之后进入 L2。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>


---

<a id="sec-4-2-6-10-2"></a>
## 4.2.6.10 Disabled Overview | Disabled 概述

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

<!-- 📄 Page 497 -->
---

The intent of the Disabled state is to allow a configured Link to be disabled as long as directed or until Electrical Idle is
exited (i.e., due to a hot removal and insertion) after entering Disabled.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

<!-- 📄 Page 497 -->
---

Disabled 状态的目的是允许在已配置链路被指示期间或进入 Disabled 之后直到退出电气空闲(即由于热移除和插入)为止禁用该链路。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

---


---

<a id="sec-4-2-6-10"></a>
## 4.2.6.10 Disabled Overview | 禁用状态概述

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

76. The exception is the Receiver termination, which must remain in a low impedance state.
77. In this context, Aux power means a power source which can be used to drive the Beacon circuitry.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

76. 例外的是接收器端接(Receiver termination),它必须保持在低阻抗状态。
77. 在此上下文中,辅助电源 (Aux power) 指的是可用于驱动 Beacon 信号电路的电源。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

<!-- 📄 Page 498 -->

<a id="sec-4-2-6-11"></a>
## 4.2.6.11 Loopback Overview | 回环状态概述


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

Loopback is intended for test and fault isolation use. Only the entry and exit behavior is specified, all other details are implementation specific. Loopback can operate on either a per-Lane or configured Link basis.

A Loopback Lead is the component requesting Loopback.

A Loopback Follower is the component looping back the data.

In 8b/10b and 128b/130b encoding, Loopback uses bit 2 (Loopback) in the Training Control Field of TS1 and TS2 Ordered Sets (see § Table 4-25 and § Table 4-26). In 1b/1b encoding, Loopback uses an encoding of bits 3:0 in the Training Control field of TS1 and TS2 Ordered Sets (see § Table 4-28).

The entry mechanism for a Loopback Lead is device specific.

The Loopback Follower device enters Loopback whenever two consecutive TS1 Ordered Sets are received with the Loopback bit set.

> **IMPLEMENTATION NOTE:**
> **USE OF LOOPBACK**
>
> Once in the Loopback state, the Lead can send any pattern of Symbols as long as the encoding rules are followed. Once in Loopback, the concept of data scrambling is no longer relevant; what is sent out is looped back. The mechanism(s) and/or interface(s) utilized by the Data Link Layer to notify the Physical Layer to enter the Loopback state is implementation specific and beyond the scope of this specification.
> §

> **IMPLEMENTATION NOTE:**
> **LOOPBACK LEAD AND LOOPBACK FOLLOWER REPLACING OLDER TERMS**
>
> The terms Loopback Lead and Loopback Follower are newer terms while preserving the identical Loopback functionality as in [PCIe-5.0]. These terms appropriately represent the functionality performed by the component. Any Port or a test equipment can be a Loopback Lead if it has that optional capability. Every Port is required to be a Loopback Follower. The test set up needs to comprehend that in such a way that it designates one Port to be the Loopback Lead and its Link Partner to be the Loopback Follower.
> §

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

回环 (Loopback) 旨在用于测试和故障隔离。只有进入和退出行为是规范定义的,所有其他细节都是实现特定的。回环可以按 Lane (通道) 方式或按已配置 Link (链路) 方式运行。

回环引导方 (Loopback Lead) 是请求回环的组件。

回环跟随方 (Loopback Follower) 是回送数据的组件。

在 8b/10b 编码和 128b/130b 编码中,回环使用 TS1 和 TS2 有序集 (Ordered Sets) 训练控制字段中的 bit 2(Loopback 位)(参见 § Table 4-25 和 § Table 4-26)。在 1b/1b 编码中,回环使用 TS1 和 TS2 有序集训练控制字段中 bits 3:0 的编码(参见 § Table 4-28)。

回环引导方的进入机制由设备自行定义。

回环跟随方设备在收到两个连续的、Loopback 位置 1 的 TS1 有序集时,即进入回环。

> **实现说明:**
> **回环的使用**
>
> 一旦进入回环状态,引导方只要遵循编码规则,就可以发送任意码型的符号 (Symbol)。进入回环后,数据加扰 (Scrambling) 的概念不再相关;发送出去的内容会被回送回来。数据链路层 (Data Link Layer) 用于通知物理层 (Physical Layer) 进入回环状态所用的机制和/或接口是实现特定的,不在本规范的范围之内。
> §

> **实现说明:**
> **回环引导方和回环跟随方取代旧术语**
>
> 回环引导方 (Loopback Lead) 和回环跟随方 (Loopback Follower) 是较新的术语,但保留了与 [PCIe-5.0] 中完全相同的回环功能。这些术语恰当地反映了组件所执行的功能。任何端口 (Port) 或测试设备只要具备该可选能力,都可以作为回环引导方。每个端口都必须能够作为回环跟随方。测试设置需要以这样的方式来理解:它指定一个端口作为回环引导方,其链路对端 (Link Partner) 作为回环跟随方。
> §

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>


[⬆️ 返回目录](#-本章目录-table-of-contents)

---

<a id="sec-4-2-6-12"></a>
## 4.2.6.12 Hot Reset Overview | 热复位状态概述

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

The intent of the Hot Reset state is to allow a configured Link and associated downstream device to be reset using in-band signaling.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

热复位 (Hot Reset) 状态的目的是允许使用带内 (in-band) 信号对已配置的链路及其关联的下游设备进行复位。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

[⬆️ 返回目录](#-本章目录-table-of-contents)

---

<a id="sec-4-2-7"></a>
## 4.2.7 Link Training and Status State Rules | 链路训练与状态机规则


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

Various Link status bits are monitored through software with the exception of LinkUp which is monitored by the Data Link Layer. § Table 4-48 describes how the Link status bits must be handled throughout the LTSSM (for more information, see § Section 3.2 for LinkUp; § Section 7.5.3.8 for Link Speed, Link Width, and Link Training; § Section 6.2 for Receiver Error; and § Section 6.7 for In-Band Presence). A Receiver may also optionally report an 8b/10b Error in the Lane Error Status Register when operating in 8b/10b encoding, when allowed to report the error as a Receiver Error in § Table 4-48.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

各种链路状态位都通过软件来监控,LinkUp 例外,它由数据链路层监控。§ Table 4-48 描述了链路状态位在整个 LTSSM (链路训练与状态机) 中必须如何处理(更多信息请参见:§ Section 3.2 中关于 LinkUp;§ Section 7.5.3.8 中关于链路速度、链路宽度和链路训练;§ Section 6.2 中关于接收器错误 (Receiver Error);§ Section 6.7 中关于带内存在 (In-Band Presence))。在使用 8b/10b 编码时,当 § Table 4-48 允许将该错误作为接收器错误上报时,接收器也可以选择在 Lane 错误状态寄存器中上报 8b/10b 错误。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>


[⬆️ 返回目录](#-本章目录-table-of-contents)

---

**Table 4-48. Link Status Mapped to the LTSSM | 表 4-48 链路状态与 LTSSM 的映射**

| LTSSM State | Link Width | Link Speed | LinkUp | Link Training | Receiver Error | In-Band Presence |
|-------------|------------|------------|--------|---------------|----------------|------------------|
| Detect | Undefined | Undefined | 0b | 0b | No action | 0b |
| Polling | Undefined | Set to 2.5 GT/s on entry from Detect. Link speed may change on entry to Polling.Compliance. | 0b | 0b | No action | 1b |
| Configuration | Set | No action | 0b/1b <sup>79</sup> | 1b | Set on 8b/10b Error. Optional: Set on Link Error when using 128b/130b encoding. | 1b |
| Recovery | No action | Set to new speed when speed changes | 1b | 1b | Optionally set on Link Error. | 1b |
| L0 | No action | No action | 1b | 0b | Set on Link Error. | 1b |
| L0s | No action | No action | 1b | 0b | No action | 1b |
| L1 | No action | No action | 1b | 0b | No action | 1b |
| L2 | No action | No action | 1b | 0b | No action | 1b |
| Disabled | Undefined | Undefined | 0b | 0b | Optional: Set on 8b/10b Error | 1b |
| Loopback | No action | Link speed may change on entry to Loopback from Configuration. | 0b | 0b | No action | 1b |
| Hot Reset | No action | No action | 0b | 0b | Optional: Set on 8b/10b Error | 1b |

[⬆️ 返回目录](#-本章目录-table-of-contents)

---

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

The state machine rules for configuring and operating a PCI Express Link are defined in the following sections.

> **IMPLEMENTATION NOTE:**
> **RECEIVER ERRORS DURING CONFIGURATION AND RECOVERY STATES**
>
> Allowing Receiver Errors to be set while in Configuration or Recovery is intended to allow implementations to report Link Errors that occur while processing packets in those states. For example, if the LTSSM transitions from L0 to Recovery while a TLP is being received, a Link Error that occurs after the LTSSM transition can be reported.
> §

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

配置和操作 PCI Express 链路的状态机规则在后续各节中定义。

> **实现说明:**
> **Configuration 和 Recovery 状态期间的接收器错误**
>
> 允许在 Configuration 或 Recovery 状态下置位接收器错误,目的是让实现能够报告在这些状态下处理数据包时发生的链路错误。例如,如果 LTSSM 在接收 TLP 的过程中从 L0 转换到 Recovery,那么在 LTSSM 转换之后发生的链路错误就可以被报告。
> §

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

[⬆️ 返回目录](#-本章目录-table-of-contents)

---

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

78. In-band refers to the fact that no sideband signals are used to calculate the presence of a powered up device on the other end of a Link.
79. LinkUp will always be 0 if coming into Configuration via Detect → Polling → Configuration and LinkUp will always be 1 if coming into Configuration from any other state.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

78. "带内 (in-band)" 是指不使用任何边带 (sideband) 信号来判断链路另一端是否存在已上电设备。
79. 如果是通过 Detect → Polling → Configuration 进入 Configuration,则 LinkUp 始终为 0;如果从任何其他状态进入 Configuration,则 LinkUp 始终为 1。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

[⬆️ 返回目录](#-本章目录-table-of-contents)

---

<!-- 📄 Page 499 -->
---

<a id="sec-4-2-7-Matching-Lane-1b1b"></a>
## Matching Link and Lane Numbers for 1b/1b Encoding | 1b/1b 编码下的链路号与通道号匹配


<div style="overflow-x: auto; max-width: 100%;">
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th width="50%">🇬🇧 English</th>
<th width="50%" style="background-color:#e8e8e8">🇨🇳 中文</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>

With 1b/1b encoding, the Link and Lane numbers are not sent explicitly in the TS1/TS2 Ordered Sets in any States other than those listed in § Table 4-28. Thus, when using 1b/1b encoding in any LTSSM state other than where Link and Lane numbers are transmitted, any references to an exact match in the Link and/or Lane numbers between the transmitted and received TS0/TS1/TS2 Ordered Sets must be assumed to be true as long as the Ordered Set is a valid one. Similarly, when using 1b/1b encoding in any LTSSM state other than Configuration, any references to transmitting a TS0/TS1/TS2 Ordered Set using the Link number and/or Lane number negotiated during the Configuration LTSSM state must be interpreted as using the Lane number for the purposes of scrambling in the TS0/TS1/TS2 Ordered Set.

</td>
<td style="background-color:#e8e8e8">

在 1b/1b 编码下,除了 § Table 4-28 中所列的状态之外,链路号和通道号不会在 TS1/TS2 有序集中显式发送。因此,在传输链路号和通道号以外的任何 LTSSM 状态下使用 1b/1b 编码时,只要有序集是合法的,所发送和接收的 TS0/TS1/TS2 有序集之间关于链路号和/或通道号"完全匹配"的任何引用,都必须视为成立。类似地,在 Configuration 以外的任何 LTSSM 状态下使用 1b/1b 编码时,任何引用"使用在 Configuration LTSSM 状态期间协商好的链路号和/或通道号来传输 TS0/TS1/TS2 有序集"的地方,在 TS0/TS1/TS2 有序集加扰 (scrambling) 的目的上,都必须解释为使用通道号。

</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</div>


[⬆️ 返回目录](#-本章目录-table-of-contents)

---

> **Figure 4-49.** Main State Diagram for Link Training and Status State Machine
> <img src="figures/chapter_04/fig_0500_1_tight.png" width="700">

<!-- 📄 Page 500 -->
